title: Português de Portugal rumo ao DIPLE

days.title: O futuro simples
days.objective: Form the simple future (falarei, terá) including the three irregular stems, and use it for promises and predictions.
days.paretoFocus: One rule (full infinitive + -ei/-ás/-á/-emos/-ão) plus three irregular stems (dir-, far-, trar-) gives you every future form in the language.
drills: Conjugate 'falar' in the future out loud: falarei, falarás, falará, falaremos, falarão.
Make three promises for next year starting with 'Prometo que...' and a future verb.
Say the same plan twice: 'Amanhã falarei com ele' and the spoken version 'Vou falar com ele amanhã.'

items: Recall the five future endings added to the infinitive.
Say the three irregular future stems: dizer, fazer, trazer.
Translate: 'I will do everything to arrive on time.'

activities.title: Falarei, farei, direi
activities.intro: The simple future is built on the whole infinitive; only dizer, fazer and trazer change their stem. In speech it often gives way to ir + infinitive.

items.hr: Amanhã falarei com o diretor sobre o contrato.
items.en: Tomorrow I will speak with the director about the contract.
items.note: infinitive + endings: falarei, falarás, falará, falaremos, falarão

items.hr: Em 2030, Lisboa terá ainda mais turistas.
items.en: In 2030, Lisbon will have even more tourists.
items.note: predictions are a classic job of the simple future; ter is regular here: terei, terás, terá

items.hr: Farei tudo para chegar a horas, prometo.
items.en: I will do everything to arrive on time, I promise.
items.note: fazer → far-: farei, farás, fará

items.hr: Direi a verdade ao chefe amanhã de manhã.
items.en: I will tell the boss the truth tomorrow morning.
items.note: dizer → dir-: direi, dirás, dirá

items.hr: Trarei a sobremesa para o jantar de domingo.
items.en: I will bring the dessert for Sunday dinner.
items.note: trazer → trar-: trarei, trarás, trará

items.hr: Na fala de todos os dias: vou falar com ele amanhã.
items.en: In everyday speech: I'm going to talk to him tomorrow.
items.note: spoken pt-PT prefers ir + infinitive; the simple future belongs to writing and formal speech

items.hr: Prometo que nunca te esquecerei.
items.en: I promise I will never forget you.
items.note: próclise: 'nunca' pulls the pronoun before the verb, nunca te esquecerei

activities.title: Promessas e previsões
activities.intro: Build regular and irregular future forms and choose when the future fits.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct simple future: 'Amanhã ___ com o diretor sobre o aumento.'
questions.explanation: Future = infinitive + -ei: falarei. 'Falaria' is conditional and 'falei/falava' are past tenses.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct future of 'dizer'?
questions.explanation: Dizer shortens its stem to dir- in the future: direi, dirás, dirá.

questions.prompt: Complete the weather forecast: 'Segundo a previsão, amanhã ___ sol no Algarve.'
questions.explanation: A prediction about tomorrow takes the 3rd-person future: estará. 'Esteve' is past and 'esteja' is subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future of 'fazer': 'Prometo que ___ os trabalhos de casa logo à noite.'
questions.explanation: Fazer uses the irregular stem far-: farei. 'Fazerei' does not exist.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future of 'trazer': 'Eu ___ o vinho para a festa de sábado.'
questions.explanation: Trazer uses the irregular stem trar-: trarei, trarás, trará.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Next year we will travel to the Azores.'
questions.explanation: Time expression first, then the nós-future viajaremos: we will travel to the Azores.

activities.title: Planos para o ano novo
activities.intro: New Year's Eve in Porto: a friend asks about your resolutions. Play 'Me' and make promises in the future tense.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, que planos tens para o próximo ano?
lines.en: So, what plans do you have for next year?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muitos! Estudarei português todos os dias, vais ver.
lines.en: Lots! I will study Portuguese every day, you'll see.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sério? Toda a gente promete isso em janeiro...
lines.en: Really? Everyone promises that in January...

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Eu cumprirei! Em junho farei o exame B1 e depois viajaremos até aos Açores para celebrar.
lines.en: I will keep my word! In June I will take the B1 exam and then we will travel to the Azores to celebrate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa! E quem pagará a viagem?
lines.en: Nice! And who will pay for the trip?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pagarei eu, mas tu trarás os bolos para o barco.
lines.en: I will pay, but you will bring the cakes for the boat.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Combinado. Tenho a certeza de que será um ano fantástico.
lines.en: Deal. I'm sure it will be a fantastic year.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Será, sim. E no fim do ano falaremos outra vez sobre estas promessas!
lines.en: It will indeed. And at the end of the year we will talk about these promises again!

days.title: O condicional
days.objective: Use the conditional (gostaria, poderia, seria) for polite requests and hypothetical statements.
days.paretoFocus: The conditional shares its stem rules with the future, so it is almost free to learn, and 'gostaria de' plus 'poderia' instantly upgrade your politeness.
drills: Make three polite requests with 'poderia + infinitive' at a café, a hotel and a pharmacy.
Say three dream sentences with 'seria': 'Seria ótimo viver perto do mar...'
Repeat each conditional wish in the everyday spoken form: 'gostaria de ir' → 'gostava de ir'.

items: Recall the conditional endings: -ia, -ias, -ia, -íamos, -iam.
Say the irregular conditionals of dizer, fazer and trazer.
Translate politely: 'I would like to book a table for two.'

activities.title: Gostaria, poderia, seria
activities.intro: The conditional adds -ia endings to the same stems as the future. It softens requests, imagines hypotheticals, and in everyday speech is often replaced by the imperfect.

items.hr: Gostaria de reservar uma mesa para dois, por favor.
items.en: I would like to book a table for two, please.
items.note: gostaria de + infinitive = the polite 'I would like to'

items.hr: Poderia dizer-me onde fica a farmácia mais próxima?
items.en: Could you tell me where the nearest pharmacy is?
items.note: poderia + infinitive softens a question to a stranger

items.hr: Seria ótimo viver perto do mar.
items.en: It would be great to live near the sea.
items.note: seria = 'it would be'; the conditional imagines an unreal situation

items.hr: Sem o teu mapa, eu não encontraria o caminho.
items.en: Without your map, I wouldn't find the way.
items.note: hypothetical result: conditional endings -ia, -ias, -ia, -íamos, -iam on the infinitive

items.hr: Ele disse que faria o jantar hoje.
items.en: He said he would make dinner today.
items.note: fazer → faria; the conditional is the 'future of the past' after 'disse que'

items.hr: Ela prometeu que traria as fotografias da viagem.
items.en: She promised she would bring the photos from the trip.
items.note: trazer → traria; dizer → diria, same irregular stems as the future

items.hr: No dia a dia, os portugueses dizem: gostava de ir contigo.
items.en: In everyday life, the Portuguese say: I'd like to go with you.
items.note: spoken pt-PT replaces the conditional with the imperfect: gostava, podia, era

items.hr: Eu, no teu lugar, falaria com o senhorio.
items.en: If I were you, I would talk to the landlord.
items.note: 'eu, no teu lugar' + conditional = giving advice

activities.title: Pedidos com boas maneiras
activities.intro: Form the conditional, choose the politest option, and spot its spoken replacement.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct conditional of 'fazer'?
questions.explanation: Fazer uses the stem far- in the conditional too: faria. 'Fará' is future and 'fazia' is imperfect.

questions.prompt: You want a table at a restaurant. Which is the most polite?
options: Gostaria de uma mesa perto da janela.
questions.answer: Gostaria de uma mesa perto da janela.
questions.explanation: 'Gostaria de' softens the request; commands and 'quero' sound abrupt with strangers.

questions.prompt: In relaxed speech, which form usually replaces 'gostaria'?
questions.explanation: Everyday European Portuguese swaps the conditional for the imperfect: gostava de um café.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of 'ser': 'Viver no Algarve ___ um sonho, mas o trabalho está em Lisboa.'
questions.explanation: An imagined situation takes the conditional: seria um sonho = it would be a dream.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite question: '___ ajudar-me com esta mala, por favor?' (could you)
questions.explanation: 'Poderia (or podia) + infinitive' is the courteous way to ask a stranger for help.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'If I were you, I would talk to the landlord.'
questions.explanation: 'Eu, no teu lugar' frames advice, and the conditional falaria carries the hypothetical.

activities.title: Reserva por telefone
activities.intro: You phone a Lisbon restaurant to book a table. Play 'Me' and keep every request in the conditional.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Restaurante O Cais, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.en: O Cais restaurant, good afternoon. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Gostaria de reservar uma mesa para sábado à noite.
lines.en: Good afternoon. I would like to book a table for Saturday evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza. Para quantas pessoas seria a reserva?
lines.en: Of course. For how many people would the booking be?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Para quatro. Seria possível uma mesa perto da janela?
lines.en: For four. Would a table near the window be possible?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vou verificar... Sim, temos uma às oito horas.
lines.en: Let me check... Yes, we have one at eight o'clock.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito. E poderia guardar-nos também um lugar de estacionamento?
lines.en: Perfect. And could you also keep a parking space for us?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso não fazemos, mas há um parque público mesmo ao lado.
lines.en: We don't do that, but there's a public car park right next door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ótimo, obrigado. Então fica a reserva em nome de Silva.
lines.en: Great, thank you. Then the booking stays under the name Silva.

days.title: Opiniões e sentimentos
days.objective: Express opinions, agreement and emotions with acho que, na minha opinião, concordo/discordo, ter medo de and estar farto de.
days.paretoFocus: A handful of opinion openers plus the core emotion nouns (saudade, medo, vergonha) let you join any real discussion, the heart of B1.
drills: Give your opinion on three topics starting with 'Na minha opinião...', 'Acho que...' and 'Parece-me que...'.
Disagree politely with an imaginary friend: 'Concordo em parte, no entanto...'
Say one true sentence each with 'tenho medo de', 'estou farto de' and 'tenho saudades de'.

items: Recall three ways to introduce an opinion in Portuguese.
Say the untranslatable Portuguese word for missing someone or something.
Translate: 'On the one hand it's expensive; on the other hand it's worth it.'

activities.title: Cá para mim...
activities.intro: How to state a view, agree and disagree, and name your feelings, with the discussion vocabulary you are learning in the Words tab.

items.hr: Na minha opinião, o novo horário é melhor para todos.
items.en: In my opinion, the new schedule is better for everyone.
items.note: na minha opinião / do meu ponto de vista = opinion openers

items.hr: Acho que tens razão, mas o Pedro discorda.
items.en: I think you're right, but Pedro disagrees.
items.note: achar que = to think that; ter razão = to be right

items.hr: Parece-me que esta proposta tem mais vantagens do que desvantagens.
items.en: It seems to me that this proposal has more advantages than disadvantages.
items.note: parece-me que = it seems to me; proposta, vantagem, desvantagem

items.hr: Por um lado concordo contigo; por outro lado, o preço assusta-me.
items.en: On the one hand I agree with you; on the other hand, the price scares me.
items.note: por um lado... por outro lado = weighing two sides

items.hr: Tenho medo de falar em público, confesso.
items.en: I'm afraid of speaking in public, I confess.
items.note: ter medo de + infinitive/noun = to be afraid of

items.hr: Estou farto de discussões; prefiro chegar a acordo.
items.en: I'm fed up with arguments; I prefer to reach an agreement.
items.note: estar farto/farta de = to be fed up with; chegar a acordo = to reach an agreement

items.hr: Tenho saudades da comida da minha avó.
items.en: I miss my grandmother's cooking.
items.note: saudade, the famous Portuguese word for longing; ter saudades de = to miss

items.hr: Ele desabafou comigo e sentiu-se aliviado.
items.en: He opened up to me and felt relieved.
items.note: desabafar = to vent, get something off your chest; aliviado = relieved

activities.title: Concordas ou discordas?
activities.intro: Pick the right opinion phrase and the right emotion for each situation.

questions.prompt: Which phrase introduces your personal opinion?
questions.explanation: 'Na minha opinião' opens an opinion; 'no entanto' contrasts ideas (however).

questions.prompt: Your friend says something you fully agree with. What do you say?
questions.explanation: Concordar com = to agree with; discordar is its opposite.

questions.prompt: What does 'saudade' express?
options: longing for someone or something absent
questions.answer: longing for someone or something absent
questions.explanation: Saudade is the famously Portuguese feeling of nostalgic missing, ter saudades de alguém.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Estou ___ de esperar pelo autocarro, vou a pé!' (fed up)
questions.explanation: Estar farto/farta de = to be fed up with; it agrees with the speaker.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Tenho ___ de andar de avião, por isso vou de comboio.'
questions.explanation: Ter medo de = to be afraid of; medo is the noun 'fear'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It seems to me that he is right.'
questions.explanation: 'Parece-me que' (it seems to me that) introduces the opinion; ter razão = to be right.

activities.title: Cidade ou campo?
activities.intro: Your friend is thinking of leaving Lisbon for the countryside. Play 'Me' and debate with opinion phrases.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estou farto do trânsito de Lisboa. Penso mudar-me para o campo.
lines.en: I'm fed up with Lisbon traffic. I'm thinking of moving to the countryside.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A sério? Na minha opinião, ias sentir falta da cidade.
lines.en: Really? In my opinion, you would miss the city.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não sei... Do meu ponto de vista, a qualidade de vida é mais importante.
lines.en: I don't know... From my point of view, quality of life is more important.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo em parte. No entanto, o teu trabalho e os teus amigos estão cá.
lines.en: I agree in part. However, your job and your friends are here.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tens razão nisso. Por outro lado, posso trabalhar a partir de casa.
lines.en: You're right about that. On the other hand, I can work from home.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parece-me que já decidiste! E as saudades dos pastéis de nata?
lines.en: It seems to me you've already decided! And missing the custard tarts?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso resolve-se com visitas! Prometo não desaparecer.
lines.en: That gets solved with visits! I promise not to disappear.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está bem, mudei de ideias: até acho que vais ser feliz no campo.
lines.en: Alright, I've changed my mind: I actually think you'll be happy in the countryside.

days.title: Presente do conjuntivo I
days.objective: Form the present subjunctive from the eu-form (fale, coma, parta; seja, esteja, vá, faça, tenha) and use it after querer que, esperar que and é preciso que.
days.paretoFocus: One trick (take the eu-form, drop -o, swap the vowel) generates almost every subjunctive, and three triggers cover most B1 uses.
drills: Derive the subjunctive out loud from the eu-form: falo → fale, como → coma, parto → parta, faço → faça.
Make three wishes about friends with 'Quero que...' or 'Espero que...'.
Give three household orders with 'É preciso que tu...' and the subjunctive.

items: Recall the vowel swap: -ar verbs take -e, -er/-ir verbs take -a.
Say the fully irregular subjunctives of ser, estar and ir.
Translate: 'I want you to come to dinner on Saturday.'

activities.title: Quero que venhas
activities.intro: The present subjunctive expresses wish and necessity, not fact. Build it from the eu-form of the present: drop the -o and add the opposite vowel.

items.hr: Quero que fales com a tua irmã ainda hoje.
items.en: I want you to talk to your sister today.
items.note: falo → fale, fales: -ar verbs take -e

items.hr: Espero que comas bem antes da viagem.
items.en: I hope you eat well before the trip.
items.note: como → coma, comas: -er verbs take -a

items.hr: É preciso que partas cedo para apanhar o comboio.
items.en: You need to leave early to catch the train.
items.note: parto → parta, partas: -ir verbs also take -a

items.hr: Quero que venhas jantar cá a casa no sábado.
items.en: I want you to come to dinner at my place on Saturday.
items.note: the eu-form carries irregular stems: venho → venha, venhas

items.hr: A minha mãe quer que eu faça a cama todas as manhãs.
items.en: My mother wants me to make the bed every morning.
items.note: faço → faça; digo → diga works the same way

items.hr: O médico quer que tenhas mais cuidado com o sol.
items.en: The doctor wants you to be more careful with the sun.
items.note: tenho → tenha, tenhas

items.hr: Espero que sejas feliz na tua casa nova e que estejas bem.
items.en: I hope you are happy in your new house and that you are well.
items.note: truly irregular: ser → seja, estar → esteja

items.hr: É preciso que ela vá à farmácia antes do jantar.
items.en: She needs to go to the pharmacy before dinner.
items.note: ir → vá; also dar → dê, saber → saiba, querer → queira, haver → haja

activities.title: Da forma 'eu' ao conjuntivo
activities.intro: Build each form starting at the eu present, then spot which openers force the switch.

questions.prompt: The subjunctive comes from the eu-form. Since 'fazer' gives 'faço', its subjunctive is...
questions.explanation: Drop the -o of faço and add -a: faça. The irregular stem travels from the eu-form.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'É preciso que tu ___ na estação às nove em ponto.'
questions.explanation: 'É preciso que' triggers the subjunctive; estar is fully irregular: esteja, estejas.

questions.prompt: Which trigger requires the subjunctive after 'que'?
questions.explanation: Wishes take the subjunctive: quero que venhas. Saber/ver/dizer que report facts and take the indicative.

questions.prompt: Complete with 'vir' in the conjuntivo: 'Quero que ___ jantar cá a casa no sábado.'
questions.explanation: Venho → venha, venhas: the irregular stem of the eu-form carries into the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Espero que o exame não ___ muito difícil.' (ser)
questions.explanation: Ser is one of the seven fully irregular subjunctives: seja.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I want you to talk to the teacher tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: Querer que + subjunctive: quero que fales = I want you to speak.

activities.title: A festa surpresa
activities.intro: A friend is organising a surprise dinner for Marta and gives you instructions. Play 'Me' and listen for the subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Preciso da tua ajuda para o jantar de anos da Marta.
lines.en: I need your help with Marta's birthday dinner.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Claro! O que queres que eu faça?
lines.en: Of course! What do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quero que compres o bolo e que chegues antes das sete.
lines.en: I want you to buy the cake and to arrive before seven.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. Esperas que ela não desconfie de nada?
lines.en: Deal. Are you hoping she won't suspect anything?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Espero que sim! É preciso que ninguém lhe diga nada até sábado.
lines.en: I hope so! No one can tell her anything until Saturday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fica descansado, eu sei guardar segredos.
lines.en: Don't worry, I know how to keep secrets.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quero que tragas a tua máquina fotográfica, está bem?
lines.en: And I want you to bring your camera, okay?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trago, sim. Espero que ela goste da surpresa!
lines.en: I will. I hope she likes the surprise!

days.title: Presente do conjuntivo II
days.objective: Use the subjunctive after talvez, oxalá, para que, duvido que and não acho que, and contrast it with the indicative after acho que.
days.paretoFocus: Doubt and hope are everyday territory: talvez, oxalá and duvido que appear constantly in real Portuguese conversation.
drills: Make three uncertain plans for the weekend starting with 'Talvez...'.
Express three hopes with 'Oxalá...' about the weather, a friend and an exam.
Say the contrast pair out loud: 'Acho que ele vem' vs 'Não acho que ele venha'.

items: Recall four triggers from today: duvido que, para que, oxalá, talvez.
Explain why 'acho que' takes the indicative but 'não acho que' takes the subjunctive.
Translate: 'Maybe it will rain tomorrow.'

activities.title: Talvez, oxalá, duvido...
activities.intro: More subjunctive triggers (doubt, hope and purpose) and the crucial contrast: affirmative belief takes the indicative, doubt takes the subjunctive.

items.hr: Talvez chova amanhã; leva o guarda-chuva.
items.en: Maybe it will rain tomorrow; take the umbrella.
items.note: talvez + subjunctive (no 'que' needed)

items.hr: Oxalá o tempo esteja bom no fim de semana!
items.en: Let's hope the weather is good at the weekend!
items.note: oxalá (from Arabic) expresses a hope and always takes the subjunctive

items.hr: Telefona-me para que eu saiba a que horas chegas.
items.en: Call me so that I know what time you arrive.
items.note: para que (so that) = purpose → subjunctive; saber → saiba

items.hr: Duvido que ele queira mudar de emprego.
items.en: I doubt he wants to change jobs.
items.note: duvidar que = doubt → subjunctive; querer → queira

items.hr: Acho que o preço é justo, mas não acho que o serviço seja bom.
items.en: I think the price is fair, but I don't think the service is good.
items.note: the key contrast: acho que + indicative, NÃO acho que + subjunctive

items.hr: Talvez eu vá ao Porto na sexta, mas ainda não sei.
items.en: Maybe I'll go to Porto on Friday, but I don't know yet.
items.note: ir → vá; talvez signals uncertainty, so the subjunctive follows

items.hr: Duvido que haja bilhetes para o concerto de sábado.
items.en: I doubt there are tickets for Saturday's concert.
items.note: haver → haja, one of the seven fully irregular forms

activities.title: Certeza ou dúvida?
activities.intro: Choose between indicative and subjunctive depending on how sure the speaker is.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Talvez eu ___ ao cinema logo à noite.'
questions.explanation: Talvez expresses uncertainty and takes the subjunctive: vá, not vou.

questions.prompt: Affirmative belief: 'Acho que ele ___ razão.'
questions.explanation: Affirmative 'acho que' states a belief as fact, so the indicative follows: tem.

questions.prompt: Negated belief: 'Não acho que o preço ___ justo.'
questions.explanation: Negating the belief introduces doubt: não acho que + subjunctive (seja).

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Talvez ___ amanhã, leva o guarda-chuva.' (chover)
questions.explanation: Talvez + subjunctive: chove → chova.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Oxalá o tempo ___ bom para irmos à praia!' (estar)
questions.explanation: Oxalá always takes the subjunctive; estar is irregular: esteja.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I doubt he wants to come with us.'
questions.explanation: Duvidar que triggers the subjunctive queira; 'connosco' is the European 'with us'.

activities.title: Caminhada na Arrábida
activities.intro: You and a friend plan a Sunday hike, but the weather is uncertain. Play 'Me' with talvez, oxalá and duvido que.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vamos fazer a caminhada na Arrábida no domingo?
lines.en: Shall we do the hike in Arrábida on Sunday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Talvez. Duvido que o tempo ajude, a previsão dá chuva.
lines.en: Maybe. I doubt the weather will cooperate, the forecast says rain.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oxalá estejam enganados! Preciso de sair de casa.
lines.en: Let's hope they're wrong! I need to get out of the house.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Eu também. Talvez seja melhor levarmos os impermeáveis, por precaução.
lines.en: Me too. Maybe it's better if we take raincoats, just in case.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa ideia. E não acho que o caminho seja difícil, é bom para todos.
lines.en: Good idea. And I don't think the trail is hard, it's good for everyone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito. Falo com a Rita para que ela venha connosco?
lines.en: Perfect. Shall I talk to Rita so that she comes with us?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sim! Oxalá ela possa, faz sempre boa companhia.
lines.en: Yes! Let's hope she can, she's always good company.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então combinado. Talvez até faça sol, quem sabe!
lines.en: Deal then. Maybe it will even be sunny, who knows!

days.title: Serviços e burocracia
days.objective: Handle Portuguese bureaucracy: Finanças, correios and câmara municipal, with NIF, morada, requerimento and comprovativo.
days.paretoFocus: Every resident of Portugal faces the Finanças and the câmara, knowing senha, balcão, NIF and comprovativo turns a stressful errand into a routine one.
drills: List the documents you would take to change your morada fiscal: cartão de cidadão, comprovativo de morada.
Rehearse arriving at a public office: 'Tiro uma senha, espero pela minha vez, vou ao balcão.'
Explain to an imaginary friend what the NIF is and where you get it.

items: Recall: where do you go in Portugal to sort out taxes?
Say the official words for a written request and a supporting document.
Translate: 'You have to take a queue ticket and wait for your turn.'

activities.title: Uma manhã nas Finanças
activities.intro: The survival vocabulary of Portuguese public services (tax office, post office and town hall) straight from your Words tab pack.

items.hr: Para tratar dos impostos, tens de ir às Finanças.
items.en: To sort out your taxes, you have to go to the tax office.
items.note: as Finanças = the Portuguese tax office; tratar de = to deal with

items.hr: Preciso do seu número de contribuinte e da morada fiscal.
items.en: I need your taxpayer number and your registered tax address.
items.note: número de contribuinte = the NIF, needed for everything in Portugal

items.hr: Ao chegar, tire uma senha e aguarde pela sua vez no balcão.
items.en: When you arrive, take a queue ticket and wait for your turn at the counter.
items.note: tirar uma senha = to take a numbered ticket; o balcão = the counter

items.hr: Entreguei o requerimento na câmara municipal dentro do prazo.
items.en: I handed in the written request at the town hall within the deadline.
items.note: o requerimento = formal written request; o prazo = deadline

items.hr: Vou aos correios levantar uma encomenda registada.
items.en: I'm going to the post office to pick up a registered parcel.
items.note: os correios = the post office; correio registado = registered mail

items.hr: É preciso preencher o formulário e juntar um comprovativo de morada.
items.en: You need to fill in the form and attach a proof of address.
items.note: preencher um formulário; o comprovativo = supporting document/proof

items.hr: Na Loja do Cidadão pode renovar o cartão de cidadão num só sítio.
items.en: At the Citizen's Shop you can renew your citizen card in one single place.
items.note: Loja do Cidadão = one-stop public services centre; cartão de cidadão = national ID

items.hr: Pedi uma certidão e paguei a taxa ao balcão.
items.en: I requested a certificate and paid the fee at the counter.
items.note: a certidão = official certificate; a taxa = fee

activities.title: Papelada sem pânico
activities.intro: Match each errand to the right office, document and expression.

questions.prompt: Where do the Portuguese go to sort out tax matters?
questions.explanation: As Finanças is the tax office; os correios handle mail and parcels.

questions.prompt: What is the NIF?
questions.explanation: NIF = número de identificação fiscal, your taxpayer number, asked for everywhere in Portugal.

questions.prompt: What is the first thing you do when you arrive at a busy public office?
questions.explanation: You take a numbered queue ticket (senha) and wait for it to be called.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Tem de entregar este ___ na câmara municipal dentro do prazo.' (written request)
questions.explanation: O requerimento is the formal written request used with public services.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Junte ao formulário um ___ de morada, por exemplo uma fatura da luz.'
questions.explanation: Um comprovativo is any document that proves something, here, proof of address.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I need to renew my citizen card.'
questions.explanation: Precisar keeps its 'de' in European Portuguese: preciso de renovar.

activities.title: Ao balcão das Finanças
activities.intro: Your queue number is called at the Finanças. Play 'Me' and change your tax address.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia, senha A quarenta e dois. Em que posso ajudá-lo?
lines.en: Good morning, ticket A forty-two. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Preciso de alterar a minha morada fiscal.
lines.en: Good morning. I need to change my registered tax address.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza. Trouxe o seu cartão de cidadão e um comprovativo de morada?
lines.en: Of course. Did you bring your citizen card and a proof of address?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, trouxe o cartão e uma fatura da luz do mês passado.
lines.en: Yes, I brought the card and an electricity bill from last month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ótimo. Só preciso que preencha este formulário e assine aqui em baixo.
lines.en: Great. I just need you to fill in this form and sign here at the bottom.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está feito. Há algum prazo ou taxa a pagar?
lines.en: Done. Is there any deadline or fee to pay?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não, a alteração é gratuita e fica registada ainda hoje.
lines.en: No, the change is free and will be registered today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito, muito obrigado pelo atendimento.
lines.en: Perfect, thank you very much for your help.

days.title: Tratamento formal
days.objective: Address strangers correctly with o senhor / a senhora and soften requests with podia...?, queria..., era possível...? and dava-me jeito.
days.paretoFocus: Getting formality right opens every door in Portugal: one wrong 'tu' can sour a conversation, while 'podia' and 'queria' make everything easier.
drills: Ask the same favour twice: to a friend with 'podes...?' and to a stranger with 'podia...?'.
Rehearse a formal phone opening: 'Bom dia, queria falar com...' and closing: 'Muito obrigado, bom dia.'
Say three appointment preferences with 'dava-me jeito': morning, Tuesday, after five.

items: Recall the three levels of address: tu, no pronoun + 3rd person, o senhor / a senhora.
Say two softened requests: 'podia...?' and 'importa-se de...?'.
Translate: 'It would suit me better in the morning.'

activities.title: O senhor podia...?
activities.intro: Formal European Portuguese avoids 'você' in careful speech: use o senhor / a senhora or just the 3rd-person verb, and soften requests with the imperfect.

items.hr: O senhor podia dizer-me as horas, por favor?
items.en: Could you (sir) tell me the time, please?
items.note: o senhor / a senhora + 3rd-person verb = the safe formal address

items.hr: A senhora deseja mais alguma coisa?
items.en: Would you (madam) like anything else?
items.note: shop and café staff use 'deseja...?' with formal customers

items.hr: Queria marcar uma consulta para quinta-feira.
items.en: I'd like to book an appointment for Thursday.
items.note: queria + noun/infinitive, the everyday polite 'I'd like'

items.hr: Era possível falar com o doutor Santos?
items.en: Would it be possible to speak with Dr. Santos?
items.note: era possível + infinitive? the imperfect softens the request

items.hr: Importa-se de esperar um momento?
items.en: Would you mind waiting a moment?
items.note: importar-se de + infinitive = would you mind...?

items.hr: Dava-me jeito receber a encomenda de manhã.
items.en: It would suit me to receive the parcel in the morning.
items.note: dar jeito = to be convenient; dava-me jeito = it would suit me

items.hr: Quer deixar mensagem? Eu digo-lhe que telefonou.
items.en: Do you want to leave a message? I'll tell him you called.
items.note: 3rd person with no pronoun ('quer...?') is neutral-polite on the phone

items.hr: Com amigos: podes ajudar-me? Com desconhecidos: podia ajudar-me?
items.en: With friends: can you help me? With strangers: could you help me?
items.note: never mix tu forms with o senhor, pick one register and keep it

activities.title: Com licença...
activities.intro: Choose the right register and the softest request for each situation.

questions.prompt: What is the most polite way to ask a stranger for the time?
options: O senhor podia dizer-me as horas?
questions.answer: O senhor podia dizer-me as horas?
questions.explanation: Formal address (o senhor) plus the softened 'podia' is the courteous combination.

questions.prompt: What does 'Importa-se de fechar a janela?' mean?
options: Would you mind closing the window?
Is the window important to you?
Did you already close the window?
You must close the window now.
questions.answer: Would you mind closing the window?
questions.explanation: Importar-se de + infinitive is the polite 'would you mind...?'.

questions.prompt: What does 'dava-me jeito' mean?
options: it would suit me / be convenient for me
it points me in the right direction
questions.answer: it would suit me / be convenient for me
questions.explanation: Dar jeito = to be handy/convenient; 'dava-me jeito de manhã' = morning would suit me.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite request: 'Desculpe, ___ indicar-me o balcão das encomendas?' (could you)
questions.explanation: The imperfect 'podia' (or conditional 'poderia') softens the request to a stranger.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ marcar uma consulta com a doutora Silva, por favor.' (I'd like)
questions.explanation: 'Queria' is the everyday polite 'I'd like' at counters and on the phone.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Would it be possible to change tables, please?'
questions.explanation: 'Era possível + infinitive?' asks for something delicately, here, changing tables.

activities.title: Marcação por telefone
activities.intro: You phone the health centre to book an appointment. Play 'Me' and stay formal from start to finish.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Centro de Saúde da Alameda, bom dia.
lines.en: Alameda Health Centre, good morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Queria marcar uma consulta com a doutora Ferreira, por favor.
lines.en: Good morning. I'd like to book an appointment with Dr. Ferreira, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza. O senhor podia dizer-me o seu número de utente?
lines.en: Of course. Could you tell me your health service user number?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É o um dois três, quatro cinco seis, sete oito nove. Era possível marcar para esta semana?
lines.en: It's one two three, four five six, seven eight nine. Would it be possible to book for this week?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Temos vaga na quinta às nove ou na sexta às quinze.
lines.en: We have an opening on Thursday at nine or Friday at three.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dava-me mais jeito na quinta de manhã.
lines.en: Thursday morning would suit me better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fica marcado. Importa-se de chegar dez minutos antes?
lines.en: It's booked. Would you mind arriving ten minutes early?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Com certeza. Muito obrigado pela atenção, bom dia.
lines.en: Certainly. Thank you very much for your help, good day.

days.title: Pretérito perfeito composto
days.objective: Use 'tenho estudado' for repeated or ongoing recent actions and never confuse it with the simple past for single events.
days.paretoFocus: This tense is the classic European Portuguese trap for English speakers: 'I have visited' is usually 'visitei', and 'tenho visitado' means something else entirely.
drills: Say three true sentences about your recent habits with 'ultimamente tenho + participle'.
Test the rule: if you can add 'lately' in English, use the composto; say one pair aloud.
Answer the catch-up question 'O que é que tens feito?' with three activities.

items: Recall: does 'tenho visitado' work for a single visit? What do you use instead?
Say three time markers that go with the composto: ultimamente, nos últimos tempos, este mês.
Translate: 'It has been raining a lot this winter.'

activities.title: O que tens feito?
activities.intro: Ter (present) + participle looks like the English present perfect but means only repetition or duration from the recent past up to now.

items.hr: Ultimamente tenho dormido muito mal.
items.en: Lately I have been sleeping very badly.
items.note: tenho + participle = repeated/ongoing recent action, almost always with 'ultimamente'

items.hr: Tem chovido imenso este inverno.
items.en: It has been raining a lot this winter.
items.note: 3rd person: tem chovido = it has been raining (repeatedly)

items.hr: Temos falado muito ao telefone nas últimas semanas.
items.en: We have been talking a lot on the phone in recent weeks.
items.note: nós: temos falado; eles: têm falado

items.hr: O que é que tens feito? Tenho estudado para o exame.
items.en: What have you been up to? I have been studying for the exam.
items.note: the standard catch-up question between friends

items.hr: Já visitaste o Porto? Sim, visitei duas vezes.
items.en: Have you visited Porto? Yes, I have (visited) twice.
items.note: single completed events take the SIMPLE past, never 'tenho visitado' here

items.hr: Ele tem trabalhado ao fim de semana para acabar o projeto.
items.en: He has been working at weekends to finish the project.
items.note: if you can add 'lately/repeatedly' in English, the composto fits

items.hr: Este mês tenho ido ao ginásio todos os dias.
items.en: This month I have been going to the gym every day.
items.note: ir → ido; markers: este mês, nos últimos tempos, ultimamente

activities.title: Composto ou simples?
activities.intro: Decide between the composto (repetition up to now) and the simple past (single event).

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I have visited Lisbon (once)' in European Portuguese?
questions.explanation: A single completed event takes the simple past. 'Tenho visitado' would mean repeated recent visits.

questions.prompt: What does 'Tenho estudado muito' mean?
options: I have been studying a lot lately.
I had studied a lot before.
questions.answer: I have been studying a lot lately.
questions.explanation: The composto expresses repetition or duration from the recent past up to now.

questions.prompt: Which time word typically accompanies the pretérito perfeito composto?
questions.explanation: Ultimamente, nos últimos tempos and este mês signal the recent, ongoing meaning of the composto.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ultimamente ___ dormido muito mal por causa do calor.' (I have been)
questions.explanation: Ter in the present + participle: tenho dormido = I have been sleeping.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Tem ___ imenso nas últimas semanas.' (chover)
questions.explanation: Tem chovido = it has been raining (repeatedly) over the last weeks.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I have been working a lot lately.'
questions.explanation: Composto + time marker: I have been working a lot in recent times.

activities.title: Reencontro no café
activities.intro: You bump into a friend you haven't seen since summer. Play 'Me', recent habits in the composto, single events in the simple past.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quanto tempo! Não te via desde o verão. O que é que tens feito?
lines.en: It's been ages! I hadn't seen you since summer. What have you been up to?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tenho trabalhado imenso, mas este mês tenho saído mais.
lines.en: I've been working a lot, but this month I've been going out more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Eu tenho andado cansado. Tem chovido tanto que nem apetece sair.
lines.en: I've been feeling tired. It has rained so much that you don't even feel like going out.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É verdade. Já foste ver a exposição no CCB?
lines.en: True. Have you been to see the exhibition at the CCB?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fui, sim, vi-a na semana passada. Vale muito a pena.
lines.en: I have, I saw it last week. It's really worth it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Eu ainda não fui, mas tenho pensado nisso.
lines.en: I haven't been yet, but I've been thinking about it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vai! E depois conta-me o que achaste.
lines.en: Go! And then tell me what you thought.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. Vamos marcando cafés, tenho saudades das nossas conversas.
lines.en: Deal. Let's keep scheduling coffees, I miss our conversations.

days.title: Mais-que-perfeito composto
days.objective: Sequence past events with 'tinha feito': say what had already happened before another past moment.
days.paretoFocus: 'Quando cheguei, ele já tinha saído', one pattern unlocks storytelling, excuses and reported speech all at once.
drills: Build three 'too late' sentences: 'Quando cheguei, ... já tinha + participle.'
Tell a small disaster from your week using one tinha-form and two simple pasts.
Conjugate out loud: tinha partido, tinhas partido, tinha, tínhamos, tinham partido.

items: Recall the formula: ter in the imperfect + participle.
Say what 'partira' in a novel corresponds to in everyday speech.
Translate: 'When I arrived at the station, the train had already left.'

activities.title: Já tinha acontecido
activities.intro: The past before the past: ter in the imperfect + participle marks what was already done when something else happened.

items.hr: Quando cheguei à estação, o comboio já tinha partido.
items.en: When I arrived at the station, the train had already left.
items.note: the signature pattern: quando + simple past, ... já tinha + participle

items.hr: Quando ela telefonou, eu já tinha saído de casa.
items.en: When she called, I had already left the house.
items.note: tinha, tinhas, tinha, tínhamos, tinham + participle

items.hr: Ela disse que nunca tinha provado bacalhau à Brás.
items.en: She said she had never tried bacalhau à Brás.
items.note: reported speech loves this tense: disse que tinha...

items.hr: Tínhamos combinado encontrar-nos às oito, mas ele esqueceu-se.
items.en: We had agreed to meet at eight, but he forgot.
items.note: tínhamos combinado = we had arranged (before the forgetting)

items.hr: Percebi que me tinha enganado no caminho.
items.en: I realised I had taken the wrong way.
items.note: próclise after 'que': ...que me tinha enganado

items.hr: O filme já tinha começado quando encontrámos lugar.
items.en: The film had already begun when we found a seat.
items.note: 'já' underlines that the earlier action was complete

items.hr: Nos livros encontras 'partira' e 'fizera', a forma literária de 'tinha partido' e 'tinha feito'.
items.en: In books you'll find 'partira' and 'fizera', the literary form of 'tinha partido' and 'tinha feito'.
items.note: recognise the one-word pluperfect in writing, but speak with the compound form

activities.title: Antes de quê?
activities.intro: Put earlier past actions in the mais-que-perfeito and later ones in the simple past.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quando cheguei ao cinema, o filme já ___.'
questions.explanation: The film started before my arrival, earlier past action, so mais-que-perfeito: tinha começado.

questions.prompt: The mais-que-perfeito composto describes...
options: an action completed before another past action
questions.answer: an action completed before another past action
questions.explanation: It is the 'past before the past': what had already happened by a past moment.

questions.prompt: In a novel you read 'Ele partira cedo.' In everyday speech this corresponds to...
questions.explanation: The literary one-word pluperfect (partira) equals the spoken compound 'tinha partido'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quando o João chegou, eu já ___ acabado de jantar.'
questions.explanation: Ter in the imperfect + participle: já tinha acabado = I had already finished.

questions.prompt: Complete with the participle: 'Quando telefonei, ela já tinha ___ de casa.' (sair)
questions.explanation: Sair → saído: ela já tinha saído = she had already left.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The train had already left when I arrived.'
questions.explanation: Earlier action in the mais-que-perfeito (tinha partido), later action in the simple past (cheguei).

activities.title: O dia em que tudo correu mal
activities.intro: You arrived home very late yesterday. Play 'Me' and explain the chain of disasters with 'já tinha...'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Chegaste tão tarde ontem! O que aconteceu?
lines.en: You got home so late yesterday! What happened?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Foi um desastre. Quando cheguei à estação, o comboio já tinha partido.
lines.en: It was a disaster. When I got to the station, the train had already left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não tinhas visto os horários antes de sair?
lines.en: Hadn't you checked the timetable before leaving?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tinha, mas o telemóvel ficou sem bateria e eu tinha-me esquecido do carregador.
lines.en: I had, but my phone ran out of battery and I had forgotten the charger.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E depois? Apanhaste o comboio seguinte?
lines.en: And then? Did you catch the next train?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, mas quando cheguei a Coimbra, a reunião já tinha começado.
lines.en: Yes, but when I arrived in Coimbra, the meeting had already started.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que azar! Ao menos almoçaste bem?
lines.en: What bad luck! At least did you have a good lunch?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nem isso, quando entrei no restaurante, a cozinha já tinha fechado.
lines.en: Not even that, when I walked into the restaurant, the kitchen had already closed.

days.title: Contar histórias
days.objective: Tell a past story combining the simple past, the imperfect and the mais-que-perfeito, linked by primeiro, depois, entretanto and no fim.
days.paretoFocus: Narration is the B1 oral exam in miniature: background in the imperfeito, events in the perfeito simples, earlier past in tinha + participle.
drills: Tell yesterday in four sentences, one connector per sentence: primeiro, depois, entretanto, no fim.
Describe the background of a memory in the imperfeito: weather, place, mood, then add one event in the perfeito.
Retell a trip that went wrong including at least one 'já tinha + participle'.

items: Recall the narrator's recipe: which tense for background, events, and the earlier past?
Say the four connectors: primeiro, depois, entretanto, no fim.
Translate: 'While we were waiting, it started to rain.'

activities.title: A receita do narrador
activities.intro: Every good Portuguese story mixes three past tenses and a few connectors. Here is the full recipe in action.

items.hr: Primeiro fomos ao mercado, depois almoçámos à beira-rio.
items.en: First we went to the market, then we had lunch by the river.
items.note: events advance the story in the perfeito simples; primeiro/depois order them

items.hr: Estava sol e as ruas estavam cheias de gente.
items.en: It was sunny and the streets were full of people.
items.note: background scenery and states go in the imperfeito

items.hr: Quando chegámos ao restaurante, os amigos já tinham pedido as bebidas.
items.en: When we got to the restaurant, our friends had already ordered the drinks.
items.note: earlier past inside the story: tinha/tinham + participle

items.hr: Enquanto eu descansava, ele foi comprar os bilhetes.
items.en: While I was resting, he went to buy the tickets.
items.note: enquanto + imperfeito for the ongoing action, perfeito for the event

items.hr: Entretanto, começou a chover e fugimos para um café.
items.en: Meanwhile, it started to rain and we ran into a café.
items.note: entretanto = meanwhile / in the meantime

items.hr: No fim, voltámos a casa cansados mas felizes.
items.en: In the end, we went back home tired but happy.
items.note: no fim = in the end, the natural closing connector

items.hr: A viagem tinha começado mal, mas acabou da melhor maneira.
items.en: The trip had started badly, but it ended in the best way.
items.note: one sentence, two layers of past: tinha começado (before) + acabou (event)

activities.title: Monta a história
activities.intro: Choose the right tense for each layer of the story and the right connector between events.

questions.prompt: In a story, which tense paints the background, weather, places, feelings?
questions.explanation: The imperfeito sets the scene (estava sol, havia gente); events move in the perfeito simples.

questions.prompt: Which connector means 'meanwhile'?
questions.explanation: Entretanto = meanwhile; primeiro opens the story and no fim closes it.

questions.prompt: Choose the right pair: 'Quando ___ ao hotel, o quarto ainda não ___ pronto.'
questions.explanation: The arrival is an event (perfeito: chegámos); the room's state is background (imperfeito: estava).

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quando entrámos no cinema, os trailers já ___ acabado.'
questions.explanation: Os trailers is plural, so the earlier past takes tinham: já tinham acabado.

questions.prompt: Complete the closing connector: 'No ___, voltámos a casa cansados mas felizes.' (in the end)
questions.explanation: No fim = in the end, the classic way to wrap up a story.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'First we went to the beach and then we had dinner in Porto.'
questions.explanation: Primeiro... depois... orders two perfeito-simples events into a mini narrative.

activities.title: O passeio a Sintra
activities.intro: A friend asks about your Saturday trip to Sintra. Play 'Me' and narrate with all three past tenses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como foi o passeio a Sintra no sábado?
lines.en: So, how was the trip to Sintra on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Foi uma aventura! Primeiro visitámos o Palácio da Pena, estava um nevoeiro lindíssimo.
lines.en: It was an adventure! First we visited the Pena Palace, there was a beautiful fog.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E depois? Almoçaram na vila?
lines.en: And then? Did you have lunch in the town?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quisemos, mas quando chegámos, o restaurante já tinha fechado a cozinha.
lines.en: We wanted to, but when we arrived, the restaurant had already closed the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Outra vez sem sorte! E o que fizeram?
lines.en: Unlucky again! And what did you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Entretanto encontrámos uma tasca pequenina onde se comia muito bem.
lines.en: Meanwhile we found a tiny tavern where the food was really good.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que bom! E no fim, correu tudo bem?
lines.en: Great! And in the end, did everything go well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No fim, sim: enquanto esperávamos pelo comboio, ainda provámos os travesseiros de Sintra.
lines.en: In the end, yes: while we were waiting for the train, we even tried the Sintra pastries.

days.title: Media e tecnologia
days.objective: Talk about news, devices and life online using the European tech lexicon.
days.paretoFocus: A dozen pt-PT tech words (ecrã, rato, telemóvel, descarregar, ligação, palavra-passe) cover almost every daily conversation about phones, computers and the news.
drills: Name aloud five objects on your desk in pt-PT: ecrã, teclado, rato, portátil, carregador.
Explain to an imaginary friend how to get an app working: descarregar, instalar, iniciar sessão.
Describe your media habits: which telejornal, podcasts and redes sociais you follow.

items: Recall the pt-PT words for screen, mouse, file and password.
Say three online verbs in a sentence each: descarregar, partilhar, pesquisar.
Translate: 'Send me the link by email, with the file attached.'

activities.title: O português da internet
activities.intro: News and technology the European way, where the screen is an 'ecrã' and you 'descarregas' instead of downloading.

items.hr: Vi as notícias no telejornal e depois li um artigo no telemóvel.
items.en: I watched the news on the TV news programme and then read an article on my phone.
items.note: o telejornal = TV news; o telemóvel = mobile phone (pt-PT)

items.hr: O ecrã do meu portátil é pequeno, por isso liguei-o a um monitor.
items.en: My laptop's screen is small, so I connected it to a monitor.
items.note: o ecrã = the screen (pt-PT); o portátil = laptop

items.hr: Perdi o rato do computador e agora só uso o teclado.
items.en: I lost my computer mouse and now I only use the keyboard.
items.note: o rato = the (computer) mouse in Portugal

items.hr: Vou descarregar a aplicação e instalá-la no telemóvel.
items.en: I'm going to download the app and install it on my phone.
items.note: descarregar = to download (pt-PT); a aplicação = app

items.hr: Envia-me a ligação por correio eletrónico, com o ficheiro em anexo.
items.en: Send me the link by email, with the file attached.
items.note: a ligação = link; o correio eletrónico = email; o ficheiro = file

items.hr: Esqueci-me da palavra-passe e tive de a redefinir para iniciar sessão.
items.en: I forgot my password and had to reset it to log in.
items.note: a palavra-passe = password; iniciar sessão = to log in

items.hr: Ela partilhou a reportagem na rede social e toda a gente comentou.
items.en: She shared the news report on the social network and everyone commented.
items.note: partilhar = to share; a rede social = social network

activities.title: Tecnologia à portuguesa
activities.intro: Choose the European word and use the tech verbs correctly.

questions.prompt: Which is the European Portuguese word for the computer mouse?
questions.explanation: In Portugal the computer mouse is 'o rato', the same word as the animal.

questions.prompt: What are you doing when you 'descarregas' a file?
questions.explanation: 'Descarregar' is the pt-PT verb for downloading, literally 'to unload'.

questions.prompt: Choose the pt-PT term: 'Cliquei na ___ que me enviaste e o sítio abriu logo.'
questions.explanation: 'A ligação' is both a link and a connection in European Portuguese; 'o sítio' is a website.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pt-PT word for screen: 'O ___ do telemóvel partiu-se quando caiu ao chão.'
questions.explanation: 'O ecrã' is the European Portuguese word for a screen.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não consigo iniciar sessão porque me esqueci da ___.' (password)
questions.explanation: 'A palavra-passe' is the pt-PT word for password.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I'm going to download the app on my phone.'
questions.explanation: 'Ir + infinitive' for the near future: I'm going to download the app onto my phone.

activities.title: Socorro informático
activities.intro: Your flatmate is having a tech meltdown before a deadline. Play 'Me' and rescue the situation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Socorro! O meu portátil não liga e tenho um trabalho para entregar hoje.
lines.en: Help! My laptop won't turn on and I have an assignment to hand in today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Calma. O ecrã está completamente preto? Experimenta ligar o carregador.
lines.en: Calm down. Is the screen completely black? Try plugging in the charger.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah, era isso... a bateria estava no fim. Já está a arrancar.
lines.en: Ah, that was it... the battery was almost dead. It's starting up now.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ótimo. Aproveita e atualiza o sistema, está sempre a pedir.
lines.en: Great. While you're at it, update the system, it's always asking.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa ideia. Podes enviar-me a ligação daquele artigo sobre podcasts?
lines.en: Good idea. Can you send me the link to that article about podcasts?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Claro, mando-ta por correio eletrónico, com o ficheiro em anexo.
lines.en: Of course, I'll send it to you by email, with the file attached.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Obrigada! E qual é a palavra-passe do wi-fi cá de casa?
lines.en: Thanks! And what's the wi-fi password here at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está escrita atrás do router, mas não a partilhes com toda a gente!
lines.en: It's written on the back of the router, but don't share it with everyone!

days.title: Discurso indireto
days.objective: Report what people said, asked and requested, shifting tenses and time words correctly.
days.paretoFocus: Three reporting frames (disse que, perguntou se, pediu para) plus the one-step tense backshift let you retell any conversation, a core B1 exam skill.
drills: Report three things a friend told you yesterday, starting each with 'Disse que...'.
Turn these into reported questions: 'Onde moras?' and 'Vens à festa?', 'Perguntou-me onde..., se...'.
Report a command you heard today with 'pediu para + infinitivo'.

items: Recall the backshift pairs: presente → ?, perfeito simples → ?, futuro → ?
Say how 'hoje', 'amanhã' and 'aqui' change in reported speech.
Translate: 'She asked me if I lived in Lisbon.'

activities.title: Ele disse que...
activities.intro: Reporting after a past verb shifts every tense one step back, and moves the time and place words too.

items.hr: «Estou muito ocupada.» → A Rita disse que estava muito ocupada.
items.en: 'I'm very busy.' → Rita said she was very busy.
items.note: presente → imperfeito

items.hr: «Vi esse filme ontem.» → Ele disse que tinha visto aquele filme no dia anterior.
items.en: 'I saw that film yesterday.' → He said he had seen that film the day before.
items.note: perfeito simples → mais-que-perfeito; ontem → no dia anterior; esse → aquele

items.hr: «Virei amanhã.» → Prometeu que viria no dia seguinte.
items.en: 'I will come tomorrow.' → He promised he would come the following day.
items.note: futuro → condicional; amanhã → no dia seguinte

items.hr: «Moras em Lisboa?» → Perguntou-me se eu morava em Lisboa.
items.en: 'Do you live in Lisbon?' → He asked me if I lived in Lisbon.
items.note: yes/no questions are reported with 'perguntar se'

items.hr: «Espera aqui!» → Pediu-me para esperar ali.
items.en: 'Wait here!' → He asked me to wait there.
items.note: commands: pedir para + infinitivo; aqui → ali

items.hr: «Hoje não posso.» → Disse que naquele dia não podia.
items.en: 'I can't today.' → He said he couldn't that day.
items.note: hoje → naquele dia

activities.title: Passar ao indireto
activities.intro: Shift the tenses, the time words and the persons, report each sentence correctly.

questions.prompt: Report «Estou cansado.»: 'Ele disse que ___ cansado.'
questions.explanation: The present backshifts to the imperfeito after 'disse que': estou → estava.

questions.prompt: Report «Comprei o bilhete.»: 'Ela disse que ___ o bilhete.'
questions.explanation: The perfeito simples backshifts to the mais-que-perfeito composto: comprei → tinha comprado.

questions.prompt: How do you report the question «Vens à festa?»?
options: Perguntou-me se eu ia à festa.
Perguntou-me que eu vou à festa.
questions.answer: Perguntou-me se eu ia à festa.
questions.explanation: Yes/no questions are reported with 'perguntar se' plus the backshifted verb (vens → ia).

questions.prompt: Report the promise «Telefonarei amanhã.»: 'Prometeu que ___ no dia seguinte.' (futuro → condicional)
questions.explanation: The future backshifts to the conditional: telefonarei → telefonaria; amanhã → no dia seguinte.

questions.prompt: Report the command «Fecha a janela!»: 'A mãe pediu-me ___ fechar a janela.' (preposition)
questions.explanation: Spoken European Portuguese reports commands with 'pedir para + infinitivo'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'She said she was very tired.'
questions.explanation: Reporting frame 'disse que' plus the imperfeito: she said she was very tired.

activities.title: O recado
activities.intro: You were out and your flatmate took a phone message. Play 'Me' and get all the details.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ligou o senhor Costa enquanto estavas fora.
lines.en: Mr. Costa called while you were out.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ah sim? O que é que ele disse?
lines.en: Oh really? What did he say?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Disse que a reunião de amanhã tinha sido adiada.
lines.en: He said tomorrow's meeting had been postponed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Outra vez? E perguntou alguma coisa?
lines.en: Again? And did he ask anything?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perguntou se podias ligar-lhe hoje à tarde.
lines.en: He asked if you could call him this afternoon.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está bem. Ele pediu para eu levar os documentos?
lines.en: Alright. Did he ask me to bring the documents?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sim, pediu para os levares na sexta-feira.
lines.en: Yes, he asked you to take them on Friday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito, obrigado por me dares o recado.
lines.en: Perfect, thanks for giving me the message.

days.title: Pronomes relativos
days.objective: Combine sentences with que, quem, onde, o que and cujo instead of repeating nouns.
days.paretoFocus: Relative pronouns turn choppy A2 sentences into fluent B1 speech, 'que' alone links half of everything you say, and 'cujo' is a favourite exam trap.
drills: Describe your street with three relatives: a rua onde..., o vizinho que..., o café de que mais gosto.
Combine pairs of short sentences about your day using 'que'.
Build one sentence with 'cujo' about a writer or musician you admire.

items: Recall: which relative pronoun is used for people after a preposition?
Say the rule for 'cujo': does it agree with the possessor or the thing possessed?
Translate: 'The city where I was born has a beautiful river.'

activities.title: Que, quem, onde, cujo
activities.intro: One pronoun per job: que for almost everything, quem for people after a preposition, onde for places, o que for whole ideas, cujo for possession.

items.hr: O livro que me emprestaste é ótimo.
items.en: The book (that) you lent me is great.
items.note: que = all-purpose relative for people and things

items.hr: A senhora com quem falei foi muito simpática.
items.en: The lady with whom I spoke was very kind.
items.note: quem = people only, normally after a preposition

items.hr: A aldeia onde os meus avós nasceram fica no Minho.
items.en: The village where my grandparents were born is in the Minho.
items.note: onde replaces 'em que' for places

items.hr: Não percebi o que disseste, podes repetir?
items.en: I didn't understand what you said, can you repeat?
items.note: o que = 'what / that which', picks up whole ideas

items.hr: O escritor cujo romance ganhou o prémio é português.
items.en: The writer whose novel won the prize is Portuguese.
items.note: cujo agrees with the thing possessed, not the possessor

items.hr: A empresa para a qual trabalho fica no Porto.
items.en: The company I work for is in Porto.
items.note: o qual/a qual = formal alternative after a preposition

items.hr: Comprei um telemóvel. O telemóvel tem um bom ecrã. → Comprei um telemóvel que tem um bom ecrã.
items.en: I bought a phone. The phone has a good screen. → I bought a phone that has a good screen.
items.note: relatives merge two sentences and delete the repeated noun

activities.title: Juntar as frases
activities.intro: Pick the right relative and merge the sentences like a native speaker.

questions.prompt: 'O colega com ___ almoço todos os dias está de férias.'
questions.explanation: After a preposition, people take 'quem': com quem, de quem, a quem.

questions.prompt: 'Não concordo com ___ ele disse na reunião.'
questions.explanation: 'O que' refers to a whole idea ('what he said') not to a specific noun.

questions.prompt: 'O poeta ___ casa visitámos viveu em Coimbra.'
questions.explanation: 'Cujo' agrees with the thing possessed: a casa is feminine, so 'cuja casa'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the place relative: 'O café ___ nos conhecemos já fechou.'
questions.explanation: 'Onde' replaces 'em que' for places: the café where we met.

questions.prompt: Combine into one sentence: 'Li um artigo. O artigo fala das alterações climáticas.' → 'Li um artigo ___ fala das alterações climáticas.'
questions.explanation: 'Que' is the all-purpose relative: it replaces the repeated noun 'o artigo'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The lady I spoke with was very nice.'
questions.explanation: The preposition travels with the relative: com quem falei = with whom I spoke.

activities.title: O bairro onde moro
activities.intro: You are showing a friend around your neighbourhood. Play 'Me' and describe it with relatives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então é este o bairro onde moras? É muito giro!
lines.en: So this is the neighbourhood where you live? It's really nice!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É! Aquele é o café onde tomo o pequeno-almoço todos os dias.
lines.en: It is! That's the café where I have breakfast every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quem é aquela senhora que te cumprimentou?
lines.en: And who is that lady who greeted you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É a vizinha de quem te falei, a que faz os melhores bolos da rua.
lines.en: She's the neighbour I told you about, the one who makes the best cakes on the street.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E aquele prédio cujas janelas são azuis? Parece antigo.
lines.en: And that building whose windows are blue? It looks old.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É o prédio onde vivia um escritor cujo nome está na placa.
lines.en: It's the building where a writer lived, his name is on the plaque.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Adoro estas ruas! Não admira que gostes de viver aqui.
lines.en: I love these streets! No wonder you like living here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois é. O que mais me agrada aqui é o sossego.
lines.en: Exactly. What I like most here is the peace and quiet.

days.title: Natureza e ambiente
days.objective: Discuss the environment, recycling and Portuguese landscapes with B1 vocabulary.
days.paretoFocus: Environment is a guaranteed B1 conversation topic, reciclagem, alterações climáticas, poluição and incêndios cover the news, the exam and everyday small talk.
drills: List what goes in each ecoponto container (vidro, papel, plástico) with two example objects each.
Give your opinion on 'as alterações climáticas' in three sentences.
Describe a Portuguese landscape you love: serra, floresta, costa, falésia.

items: Recall the pt-PT words for recycling point, drought and wildfire.
Say three eco-habits, each with a different verb: reciclar, poupar, separar.
Translate: 'We must protect the environment and save water.'

activities.title: Um país mais verde
activities.intro: From the ecoponto on your street to the sobreiro in the Alentejo, the vocabulary of Portuguese nature and its problems.

items.hr: Em Portugal separamos o lixo e levamo-lo ao ecoponto.
items.en: In Portugal we sort the rubbish and take it to the recycling point.
items.note: o ecoponto = street recycling containers; note levamos + o → levamo-lo

items.hr: A reciclagem do vidro, do papel e do plástico faz parte da rotina.
items.en: Recycling glass, paper and plastic is part of the routine.
items.note: a reciclagem = recycling; reciclar = to recycle

items.hr: As alterações climáticas provocam secas mais longas no sul do país.
items.en: Climate change causes longer droughts in the south of the country.
items.note: as alterações climáticas (pt-PT) = climate change; a seca = drought

items.hr: A poluição dos rios diminuiu, mas ainda há muito para fazer.
items.en: River pollution has decreased, but there is still a lot to do.
items.note: a poluição = pollution; poluir = to pollute

items.hr: A floresta portuguesa sofre com os incêndios todos os verões.
items.en: The Portuguese forest suffers from wildfires every summer.
items.note: o incêndio = (wild)fire; a floresta = forest

items.hr: O sobreiro é uma árvore protegida, é dele que vem a cortiça.
items.en: The cork oak is a protected tree, cork comes from it.
items.note: o sobreiro = cork oak, a national symbol

items.hr: Instalámos painéis solares para usar energia renovável e poupar água quente.
items.en: We installed solar panels to use renewable energy and save on hot water.
items.note: energia renovável = renewable energy; poupar = to save

activities.title: Ambiente em ação
activities.intro: Recycle the right words: environment vocabulary in real contexts.

questions.prompt: Where do you take glass and paper for recycling in Portugal?
questions.explanation: The 'ecoponto' is the set of coloured street containers for recycling.

questions.prompt: Complete the fixed pt-PT expression for climate change: 'as ___ climáticas'.
questions.explanation: European Portuguese says 'alterações climáticas' for climate change.

questions.prompt: Which protected Portuguese tree gives us cork?
questions.explanation: Cork ('a cortiça') comes from the sobreiro; Portugal is the world's biggest producer.

questions.prompt: Complete with the problem of dirty air and rivers: 'A ___ é um dos maiores problemas das grandes cidades.'
questions.explanation: 'A poluição' = pollution; the verb is 'poluir'.

questions.prompt: Complete the eco-habit: 'Devemos separar o ___ em casa: vidro, papel e plástico.'
questions.explanation: 'Separar o lixo' = to sort the rubbish for recycling.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The forest suffers from fires in the summer.'
questions.explanation: 'Sofrer com' = to suffer from; wildfires are a sad summer topic in Portugal.

activities.title: Mais verde lá em casa
activities.intro: You and your flatmate decide to make the house more eco-friendly. Play 'Me' and negotiate the new habits.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Temos de começar a separar o lixo lá em casa. Está tudo misturado.
lines.en: We have to start sorting the rubbish at home. It's all mixed up.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão. Eu compro três caixotes: vidro, papel e plástico.
lines.en: You're right. I'll buy three bins: glass, paper and plastic.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E onde fica o ecoponto mais próximo?
lines.en: And where is the nearest recycling point?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Há um ao fundo da rua, ao lado do café. Levo lá o vidro à sexta.
lines.en: There's one at the end of the street, next to the café. I'll take the glass there on Fridays.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfeito. Também devíamos poupar água, os teus duches são eternos.
lines.en: Perfect. We should also save water, your showers are endless.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prometo tomar duches mais curtos se desligares as luzes da sala.
lines.en: I promise to take shorter showers if you turn off the living-room lights.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Combinado! Assim contribuímos para proteger o ambiente.
lines.en: Deal! That way we contribute to protecting the environment.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E poupamos dinheiro na conta da luz, o que também não é mau.
lines.en: And we save money on the electricity bill, which isn't bad either.

days.title: A passiva e os particípios duplos
days.objective: Build passive sentences with ser + participle + por and choose the right double participle with ser/estar vs ter.
days.paretoFocus: The passive dominates news and formal writing, and the aceite/entregue/pago choices are classic pt-PT points that instantly mark you as a careful speaker.
drills: Turn three active sentences about your city into the passive: 'A ponte foi construída...'.
Recite the short participles aloud: pago, aceite, entregue, aceso, ganho, gasto.
Describe your flat with participles as adjectives: a conta está paga, a luz está acesa.

items: Recall: which participle of 'aceitar' goes with ser/estar in Portugal?
Say the passive formula: ser + ? + por.
Translate: 'The parcels were delivered yesterday.'

activities.title: Foi feito por...
activities.intro: The passive is ser + participle (which agrees) + por. Some verbs carry two participles: the long one with ter, the short one with ser/estar.

items.hr: Este fado foi cantado pela Amália Rodrigues.
items.en: This fado was sung by Amália Rodrigues.
items.note: passive = ser + particípio + por (por + a = pela)

items.hr: As cartas foram escritas à mão.
items.en: The letters were written by hand.
items.note: the participle agrees with the subject: cartas → escritas

items.hr: A proposta foi aceite por todos os sócios.
items.en: The proposal was accepted by all the partners.
items.note: aceitar with ser/estar → aceite (the pt-PT form); with ter → aceitado

items.hr: As encomendas foram entregues ontem; o carteiro tinha entregado outras de manhã.
items.en: The parcels were delivered yesterday; the postman had delivered others in the morning.
items.note: entregue with ser/estar, entregado with ter

items.hr: Já tinha pago a renda quando o senhorio ligou.
items.en: I had already paid the rent when the landlord called.
items.note: pagar, ganhar and gastar use the short forms (pago, ganho, gasto) even with ter

items.hr: A luz ficou acesa toda a noite.
items.en: The light stayed on all night.
items.note: acender: aceso with ser/estar/ficar, acendido with ter

items.hr: O mosteiro foi construído no século XVI e é visitado por milhares de turistas.
items.en: The monastery was built in the 16th century and is visited by thousands of tourists.
items.note: regular verbs have one participle, no choice to make

activities.title: Particípio certo
activities.intro: Agreement, agent and the double-participle choice, pick the right form every time.

questions.prompt: 'As encomendas foram ___ esta manhã.'
questions.explanation: With ser, entregar uses the short participle 'entregue', agreeing in the plural: entregues.

questions.prompt: 'A proposta foi ___ por todos.' Choose the European Portuguese participle:
questions.explanation: In Portugal the passive of aceitar is 'aceite'; 'aceito' is the Brazilian form and 'aceitado' goes with ter.

questions.prompt: 'O poema foi escrito ___ Camões.' Which word introduces the agent?
questions.explanation: The agent of a passive takes 'por' (contracting to pelo/pela): escrito por Camões.

questions.prompt: Complete with the participle of 'pagar' after estar: 'Não te preocupes, a conta já está ___.'
questions.explanation: With estar you use the short form, agreeing with 'a conta': paga.

questions.prompt: Complete with the participle of 'acender' after ficar: 'A luz ficou ___ toda a noite.'
questions.explanation: With ficar/estar you use the short form 'aceso', agreeing with 'a luz': acesa.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'This fado was sung by Amália.'
questions.explanation: Passive word order: subject + ser + participle + pela + agent.

activities.title: A encomenda perdida
activities.intro: Your online order is late. Play 'Me' and phone the shop to sort it out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Loja Ponto Azul, boa tarde. Em que posso ser útil?
lines.en: Ponto Azul shop, good afternoon. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Fiz uma encomenda há uma semana e ainda não foi entregue.
lines.en: Good afternoon. I placed an order a week ago and it still hasn't been delivered.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Deixe-me ver... A encomenda foi enviada na quarta-feira pelo correio normal.
lines.en: Let me see... The parcel was sent on Wednesday by regular mail.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mas o pagamento foi feito logo no dia da compra. A conta está paga há dias!
lines.en: But the payment was made on the day of purchase. The bill has been paid for days!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tem razão, peço desculpa. O atraso foi causado por uma greve nos correios.
lines.en: You're right, I apologise. The delay was caused by a postal strike.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Compreendo. E quando é que a encomenda vai ser entregue?
lines.en: I understand. And when will the parcel be delivered?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Amanhã de manhã. O envio expresso já foi aceite pelo sistema, sem custos.
lines.en: Tomorrow morning. Express shipping has already been accepted by the system, at no cost.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito, muito obrigado. Fico à espera.
lines.en: Perfect, thank you very much. I'll be waiting.

days.title: Regência verbal
days.objective: Use the fixed prepositions that common verbs demand: gostar de, sonhar com, depender de, confiar em, apaixonar-se por.
days.paretoFocus: Wrong prepositions are the most persistent foreign accent in grammar, learning verb + preposition as one unit fixes hundreds of future sentences at once.
drills: Say one true sentence for each: apaixonar-se por, confiar em, depender de, sonhar com.
Ask three questions that keep the preposition: 'De que precisas?', 'Em que pensas?'.
Tell a two-minute story using habituar-se a, esforçar-se and desistir de.

items: Recall the prepositions: precisar ___, sonhar ___, confiar ___, apaixonar-se ___.
Say a sentence where 'depender de' contracts to 'do'.
Translate: 'She married a colleague and never gave up on her dreams.'

activities.title: Verbo + preposição
activities.intro: Learn each verb glued to its preposition, like a single word, the pairs rarely match English.

items.hr: Em Portugal precisamos sempre DE: preciso de ajuda, preciso de dormir.
items.en: In Portugal 'precisar' always takes DE: I need help, I need to sleep.
items.note: pt-PT keeps the de even before nouns: preciso de tempo

items.hr: Sonho com uma casa à beira-mar no Algarve.
items.en: I dream of a house by the sea in the Algarve.
items.note: sonhar com = to dream of/about

items.hr: O resultado depende do esforço de cada um.
items.en: The result depends on each person's effort.
items.note: depender de (+ o = do)

items.hr: Ela apaixonou-se por um músico e casou-se com ele no ano seguinte.
items.en: She fell in love with a musician and married him the following year.
items.note: apaixonar-se por / casar-se com

items.hr: Confio em ti, mas desconfio das promessas fáceis.
items.en: I trust you, but I distrust easy promises.
items.note: confiar em / desconfiar de

items.hr: Ainda não me habituei a acordar cedo.
items.en: I still haven't got used to waking up early.
items.note: habituar-se a; próclise after 'não'

items.hr: Ele dedica-se ao voluntariado e participa em vários projetos.
items.en: He devotes himself to volunteering and takes part in several projects.
items.note: dedicar-se a (+ o = ao) / participar em

items.hr: Nunca desistas dos teus sonhos.
items.en: Never give up on your dreams.
items.note: desistir de (+ os = dos); próclise after 'nunca'

activities.title: A preposição certa
activities.intro: Each verb wants exactly one preposition, give it what it wants.

questions.prompt: 'Sonho ___ umas férias na Madeira.'
