questions.explanation: 'Sonhar com' = to dream of, unlike English, it takes 'com'.

questions.prompt: 'Podes confiar ___ mim, não conto a ninguém.'
questions.explanation: 'Confiar em' = to trust (in) someone.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct in European Portuguese?
questions.explanation: In Portugal 'precisar' keeps its 'de' even before a noun: preciso de ajuda.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ela apaixonou-se ___ um colega de trabalho.' (preposition)
questions.explanation: 'Apaixonar-se por' = to fall in love with.

questions.prompt: Complete with preposition + article: 'O sucesso depende ___ trabalho de todos.'
questions.explanation: 'Depender de' + o trabalho contracts to 'do': depende do trabalho.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I still haven't got used to waking up early.'
questions.explanation: 'Habituar-se a' + infinitive; 'não' pulls the pronoun forward (próclise).

activities.title: Novidades do trabalho
activities.intro: A friend grills you about your new job, and a certain someone at the office. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como vai o novo emprego? Já te habituaste ao horário?
lines.en: So, how's the new job going? Have you got used to the schedule yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não me habituei a acordar às seis, mas gosto do trabalho.
lines.en: I still haven't got used to waking up at six, but I like the job.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E dizem que te apaixonaste por alguém do escritório...
lines.en: And they say you've fallen in love with someone from the office...

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quem te contou isso?! Bem... é verdade, penso nela todos os dias.
lines.en: Who told you that?! Well... it's true, I think about her every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Eu sabia! E podes confiar nos colegas para guardar o segredo?
lines.en: I knew it! And can you trust your colleagues to keep the secret?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não confio em ninguém ali! Agora tudo depende de eu ter coragem.
lines.en: I don't trust anyone there! Now it all depends on me having courage.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não desistas! Convida-a para um café.
lines.en: Don't give up! Invite her for a coffee.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão. Amanhã atrevo-me a falar com ela.
lines.en: You're right. Tomorrow I'll dare to talk to her.

days.title: Clíticos: revisão
days.objective: Master ênclise vs próclise and the contracted double pronouns mo, to, lho.
days.paretoFocus: Clitic placement is THE sound of European Portuguese (chamo-me vs não me chamo) and the contractions mo/to/lho appear constantly in real speech.
drills: Introduce yourself and your routine with five ênclise verbs: chamo-me, levanto-me, deito-me...
Negate each of them: 'Não me chamo..., nunca me deito antes das onze...'.
Practise the contractions aloud: deu-mo, dei-to, contei-lho.

items: Recall five próclise triggers, words that pull the pronoun in front of the verb.
Say the contractions: me + o, te + o, lhe + o.
Translate: 'I want you to tell me the truth.'

activities.title: Antes ou depois do verbo?
activities.intro: Ênclise (verb-pronoun) is the European default; negation, 'que', question words and adverbs like 'já' force próclise. And two pronouns can merge into one.

items.hr: Chamo-me Rita e deito-me sempre cedo.
items.en: My name is Rita and I always go to bed early.
items.note: ênclise = the default in affirmative main clauses

items.hr: Não me chamo Maria, enganaste-te no nome.
items.en: My name isn't Maria, you got the name wrong.
items.note: negation forces próclise: não me chamo

items.hr: O bebé já se deitou; fala baixo.
items.en: The baby has already gone to bed; keep your voice down.
items.note: já, ainda, também, só, sempre attract the pronoun

items.hr: Quero que me digas a verdade.
items.en: I want you to tell me the truth.
items.note: the conjunction 'que' forces próclise in the subordinate clause

items.hr: Deste-lhe o recado? Sim, dei-lho ao almoço.
items.en: Did you give him the message? Yes, I gave it to him at lunch.
items.note: lhe + o = lho

items.hr: Ele emprestou-me o carro, emprestou-mo por uma semana.
items.en: He lent me the car, he lent it to me for a week.
items.note: me + o = mo

items.hr: Pediste-me o dicionário e eu dei-to logo.
items.en: You asked me for the dictionary and I gave it to you straight away.
items.note: te + o = to

items.hr: Vou levar as chaves. → Vou levá-las já.
items.en: I'm going to take the keys. → I'm going to take them now.
items.note: after infinitive -r: levar + as → levá-las

activities.title: Pronomes no sítio certo
activities.intro: Default ênclise, forced próclise and the merged pronouns, place every clitic correctly.

questions.prompt: Choose the European default for introducing yourself:
questions.explanation: In an affirmative main clause the pronoun follows the verb with a hyphen: ênclise.

questions.prompt: Which is correct with 'já'?
questions.explanation: 'Já' is an attractor adverb: it forces próclise, já se deitou, never já deitou-se.

questions.prompt: 'Deste o recado ao chefe?', 'Sim, dei-___ de manhã.' Combine lhe + o:
questions.explanation: lhe + o contracts to 'lho'; likewise me + o = mo and te + o = to.

questions.prompt: Rewrite with the clitic: 'Vou levar as chaves.' → 'Vou ___ já.' (levar + as)
questions.explanation: The infinitive drops -r and the pronoun becomes -las: levá-las.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pronoun: 'Não ___ posso dizer o segredo agora.' (to you, informal)
questions.explanation: Negation forces próclise: não te posso dizer.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I want you to tell me the truth.'
questions.explanation: 'Que' forces próclise (que me digas) and 'querer que' takes the conjuntivo (digas).

activities.title: O segredo
activities.intro: Your friend is dying to know a secret you promised to keep. Play 'Me' and hold the line, with perfect clitics.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, já sabes a novidade da Marta?
lines.en: So, do you already know Marta's news?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sei, mas prometi que não a contava a ninguém.
lines.en: I do, but I promised I wouldn't tell anyone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vá lá, conta-ma! Eu guardo segredo, juro.
lines.en: Come on, tell it to me! I'll keep the secret, I swear.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não ta posso contar, prometi mesmo.
lines.en: I can't tell it to you, I really promised.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se eu adivinhar? Ela vai casar-se, não vai?
lines.en: And if I guess? She's getting married, isn't she?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não te digo nada! Pergunta-lhe a ela.
lines.en: I'm not telling you anything! Ask her yourself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem, pergunto-lho hoje à noite.
lines.en: Fine, I'll ask her about it tonight.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa sorte, mas não digas que fui eu!
lines.en: Good luck, but don't say it was me!

days.title: Conectores do discurso
days.objective: Structure a spoken opinion with porém, no entanto, além disso, por isso, portanto, ou seja and aliás.
days.paretoFocus: Seven connectors turn isolated sentences into an argument, exactly what the B1 oral exam rewards and what makes you sound organised in any discussion.
drills: Give a one-minute opinion on city life using porém, além disso and por isso.
Rephrase three ideas with 'ou seja': say it once, then say it more simply.
Contrast two restaurants using 'no entanto' and 'apesar de'.

items: Recall which connectors mark contrast, addition and consequence.
Say the difference between 'ou seja' and 'aliás'.
Translate: 'The flat is small; however, it is very bright.'

activities.title: Ligar as ideias
activities.intro: Each connector has one job: contrast, addition, consequence, rephrasing or reinforcement. Master the seven big ones.

items.hr: Estudei imenso; porém, o exame correu mal.
items.en: I studied a lot; however, the exam went badly.
items.note: porém = however (contrast)

items.hr: O restaurante é caro; no entanto, vale a pena.
items.en: The restaurant is expensive; however, it's worth it.
items.note: no entanto = nevertheless (contrast, very common in speech)

items.hr: A comida é ótima; além disso, o serviço é rápido.
items.en: The food is great; besides, the service is fast.
items.note: além disso = besides, moreover (addition)

items.hr: Estava a chover, por isso ficámos em casa.
items.en: It was raining, so we stayed at home.
items.note: por isso = so, that's why (consequence)

items.hr: Não tenho dinheiro; portanto, não vou de férias este ano.
items.en: I have no money; therefore, I'm not going on holiday this year.
items.note: portanto = therefore (conclusion, slightly more formal)

items.hr: Ele é autarca, ou seja, trabalha na câmara municipal.
items.en: He's a local official, that is, he works at the town hall.
items.note: ou seja = that is / in other words (rephrasing)

items.hr: Gosto de Lisboa; aliás, é a minha cidade preferida.
items.en: I like Lisbon; in fact, it's my favourite city.
items.note: aliás = in fact / by the way (reinforces or corrects)

items.hr: Apesar de estar cansada, foi ao ginásio.
items.en: Despite being tired, she went to the gym.
items.note: apesar de + infinitive = despite

activities.title: O conector certo
activities.intro: Contrast, consequence or rephrasing? Choose the connector that fits the logic.

questions.prompt: 'O filme era longo; ___, ninguém saiu da sala.' (contrast)
questions.explanation: The second idea contradicts the expectation, so a contrast connector fits: no entanto.

questions.prompt: 'Estava doente, ___ fiquei em casa.' (consequence)
questions.explanation: Being ill causes staying home, 'por isso' introduces the consequence.

questions.prompt: 'Ele é tradutor, ___, passa livros do inglês para português.' (rephrasing)
questions.explanation: 'Ou seja' rephrases the same idea in other words; 'isto é' works the same way.

questions.prompt: Complete with the addition connector: 'O hotel é barato; ___ disso, fica no centro.'
questions.explanation: 'Além disso' = besides / moreover, adding a second argument in the same direction.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Trabalhou a vida toda; ___, merece a reforma.' (therefore)
questions.explanation: 'Portanto' draws the logical conclusion from what came before.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Despite being tired, I went to the gym.'
questions.explanation: 'Apesar de' + infinitive opens the concession; the main clause follows after the comma.

activities.title: Campo ou cidade?
activities.intro: A friend asks where life is better. Play 'Me' and build a structured opinion, connector by connector.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Achas que se vive melhor no campo ou na cidade?
lines.en: Do you think life is better in the countryside or in the city?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na cidade há mais emprego; além disso, os transportes são melhores.
lines.en: In the city there are more jobs; besides, public transport is better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É verdade. No entanto, as rendas estão caríssimas.
lines.en: That's true. However, rents are extremely expensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois estão, por isso muita gente se muda para os arredores.
lines.en: They really are, which is why many people move to the outskirts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ou seja, ficas com casa mais barata e apanhas o comboio para o centro.
lines.en: In other words, you get a cheaper house and take the train into the centre.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exatamente. Aliás, estou a pensar fazer isso no próximo ano.
lines.en: Exactly. In fact, I'm thinking of doing that next year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sério? Portanto, vais trocar Lisboa por Sintra!
lines.en: Seriously? So you're going to swap Lisbon for Sintra!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Talvez! Apesar de adorar a cidade, preciso de mais sossego.
lines.en: Maybe! Despite loving the city, I need more peace and quiet.

days.title: O conjuntivo depois de expressões impessoais
days.objective: Use the present subjunctive after impersonal expressions such as é importante que, é pena que and é melhor que, and recognise the ones that keep the indicative.
days.paretoFocus: Impersonal openers are the fastest way to give an opinion or a recommendation without saying I think, which makes your Portuguese sound far less repetitive.
drills: Write five sentences about your week starting with É pena que.
Turn three pieces of advice you would give a friend into É melhor que sentences.
List the impersonal expressions you know and mark which ones take the subjunctive and which do not.

items: Which impersonal expressions state a fact and therefore keep the indicative?
Give the present subjunctive of ser, ter and haver for tu and for eles.
Say out loud one thing it is important that you do this week.

activities.title: É importante que fales com ele
activities.intro: When an impersonal expression judges, values or doubts something, the verb in the que clause goes into the present subjunctive. Expressions of certainty such as é verdade que, é evidente que and é certo que keep the indicative instead. The pattern is always expression plus que plus subjunctive.

items.hr: É importante que fales com o senhorio antes de assinares.
items.en: It is important that you speak to the landlord before signing.
items.note: falar -> que tu fales

items.hr: É pena que eles não possam vir ao jantar.
items.en: It is a pity that they cannot come to dinner.
items.note: poder -> que eles possam

items.hr: É provável que o comboio chegue com atraso.
items.en: The train will probably arrive late.
items.note: chegar -> que chegue

items.hr: Não é justo que tenhas de pagar tudo sozinho.
items.en: It is not fair that you have to pay for everything on your own.
items.note: ter -> que tenhas

items.hr: É evidente que ela sabe muito mais do que diz.
items.en: It is obvious that she knows much more than she says.
items.note: certainty -> indicativo, sabe and not saiba

activities.title: É pena que...
activities.intro: Practise choosing between the subjunctive and the indicative after impersonal expressions.

questions.prompt: É pena que vocês não ___ ficar mais tempo connosco.
questions.explanation: É pena que expresses a feeling about the fact, so the verb takes the present subjunctive: possam.

questions.prompt: Which sentence keeps the indicative because the expression states something as certain?
options: É verdade que o preço subiu.
É provável que ela mude de ideias.
questions.answer: É verdade que o preço subiu.
questions.explanation: É verdade que presents the information as a fact, so the indicative subiu is used. The other three express value, feeling or doubt and need the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *ser*: É essencial que tu ___ pontual na entrevista.
questions.explanation: É essencial que triggers the subjunctive, and the second person singular of ser in the present subjunctive is sejas.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *haver*: É pena que não ___ mais lugares no autocarro.
questions.explanation: Haver in the present subjunctive is haja. It stays singular because it is impersonal here.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It is important that we arrive on time.'
questions.explanation: É importante que is followed by the present subjunctive of chegar for nós, which is cheguemos. A horas is the usual way to say on time.

activities.title: Antes da entrevista
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague gives you advice before a job interview, and you answer using impersonal expressions with the subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É importante que leves cópias do teu currículo.
lines.en: It is important that you take copies of your CV.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Já as imprimi. É melhor que leve também as cartas de recomendação?
lines.en: I have already printed them. Is it better that I also take the recommendation letters?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É melhor que leves tudo, nunca se sabe.
lines.en: It is better that you take everything, you never know.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É pena que a entrevista seja tão cedo. Vou ter de apanhar o comboio das sete.
lines.en: It is a pity that the interview is so early. I will have to catch the seven o'clock train.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É provável que haja obras na linha, por isso sai de casa mais cedo.
lines.en: There are probably works on the line, so leave home earlier.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não é justo que tenha de acordar às cinco, mas tens razão.
lines.en: It is not fair that I have to get up at five, but you are right.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É evidente que estás nervoso. Respira fundo.
lines.en: It is obvious that you are nervous. Take a deep breath.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É natural que fique assim, é o emprego que eu queria.
lines.en: It is natural that I get like this, it is the job I wanted.

days.title: O conjuntivo em orações relativas
days.objective: Choose between the indicative and the subjunctive in relative clauses depending on whether the person or thing you are describing actually exists for you.
days.paretoFocus: This one choice separates what you are searching for from what you already have, which comes up constantly in house hunting, job hunting and shopping.
drills: Describe the flat you would like using three relative clauses with the subjunctive.
Rewrite each of those sentences as if you had already found the flat.
Write two sentences with não há ninguém que about your workplace or your street.

items: What is the difference between procuro um curso que funcione and frequento um curso que funciona?
Why does não conheço ninguém que take the subjunctive?
Say what kind of mobile phone you need, using que plus subjunctive.

activities.title: Procuro uma casa que tenha varanda
activities.intro: In a relative clause, use the indicative when the person or thing is real and identified for you. Use the present subjunctive when it is only a wish, a requirement or something that may not exist. Negative expressions like não há ninguém que and não conheço nada que always take the subjunctive.

items.hr: Procuro alguém que saiba consertar bicicletas.
items.en: I am looking for someone who knows how to repair bicycles.
items.note: the person is not identified yet -> conjuntivo

items.hr: Conheço alguém que sabe consertar bicicletas.
items.en: I know someone who knows how to repair bicycles.
items.note: the person exists -> indicativo

items.hr: Queremos um apartamento que seja perto do metro.
items.en: We want a flat that is close to the metro.
items.note: querer plus a requirement -> seja

items.hr: Não há ninguém aqui que fale russo.
items.en: There is nobody here who speaks Russian.
items.note: negation -> fale

items.hr: Vivo numa rua que tem muito comércio.
items.en: I live on a street that has a lot of shops.
items.note: a real street -> tem

activities.title: Alguém que saiba
activities.intro: Decide whether the relative clause describes something real or something still hypothetical.

questions.prompt: Procuro um curso de português que ___ ao sábado de manhã.
questions.explanation: The speaker has not found the course yet, so the relative clause takes the present subjunctive funcione.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes a flat the speaker has already found?
options: Quero uma casa que tenha jardim.
Vivo numa casa que tem jardim.
Procuro uma casa que tenha jardim.
Preciso de uma casa que tenha jardim.
questions.answer: Vivo numa casa que tem jardim.
questions.explanation: Only this sentence uses the indicative tem, which shows the house is real and known.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *fazer*: Não conheço ninguém que ___ pão em casa.
questions.explanation: After não conheço ninguém que the person does not exist for the speaker, so the subjunctive faça is required.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *custar*: Queremos um bilhete que não ___ mais de vinte euros.
questions.explanation: The ticket is a requirement rather than a ticket already chosen, so the subjunctive custe is used.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I am looking for a job that pays better.'
questions.explanation: Procurar plus an unidentified job means the relative clause takes the subjunctive pague, not paga.

activities.title: Na agência imobiliária
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are at an estate agency describing the flat you want, so use relative clauses with the subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Que tipo de apartamento procura?
lines.en: Good afternoon. What kind of flat are you looking for?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Procuro um T2 que fique perto de uma estação de comboios.
lines.en: I am looking for a two bedroom flat that is near a train station.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Temos um em Alvalade que tem uma varanda enorme.
lines.en: We have one in Alvalade that has a huge balcony.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E não têm nada que custe menos de novecentos euros?
lines.en: And do you not have anything that costs less than nine hundred euros?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nesta zona não há nada que seja assim tão barato.
lines.en: In this area there is nothing that is that cheap.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então prefiro algo que seja mais pequeno mas com garagem.
lines.en: Then I prefer something that is smaller but with a garage.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Conheço um senhorio que aluga um estúdio com lugar de garagem.
lines.en: I know a landlord who rents a studio with a parking space.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ótimo. Quero ver tudo o que esteja disponível esta semana.
lines.en: Great. I want to see everything that is available this week.

days.title: As orações concessivas
days.objective: Express contrast and concession with embora, mesmo que and apesar de, choosing the subjunctive or the infinitive according to the connector.
days.paretoFocus: Concession is what turns a flat statement into a real argument, and it is expected in any B1 discussion or written opinion.
drills: Write three sentences about your city starting with Embora.
Rewrite each of them with apesar de plus an infinitive.
Say two things you will do even if circumstances are against you, using mesmo que.

items: Which concessive connectors are followed by the subjunctive?
What follows apesar de, a verb form or a noun?
Turn but it was raining, we went out into a concessive sentence.

activities.title: Embora esteja a chover
activities.intro: Embora, mesmo que and ainda que are always followed by the subjunctive. Apesar de is different: it takes an infinitive or a noun, never que plus a conjugated verb. All of them mean roughly although or even though.

items.hr: Embora esteja a chover, vamos dar uma volta.
items.en: Even though it is raining, we are going for a walk.
items.note: embora -> conjuntivo esteja

items.hr: Apesar de estar cansado, ainda vou ao ginásio.
items.en: Despite being tired, I am still going to the gym.
items.note: apesar de -> infinitivo estar

items.hr: Apesar do frio, a esplanada estava cheia.
items.en: Despite the cold, the terrace was full.
items.note: apesar de plus a noun, de o -> do

items.hr: Mesmo que ele peça desculpa, não mudo de ideias.
items.en: Even if he apologises, I am not changing my mind.
items.note: mesmo que -> conjuntivo peça

items.hr: Ainda que custe caro, vale sempre a pena viajar.
items.en: Even if it is expensive, travelling is always worth it.
items.note: ainda que works like embora

activities.title: Embora ou apesar de
activities.intro: Practise picking the right concessive connector and the verb form it demands.

questions.prompt: Embora ___ pouco tempo, consigo acabar isto hoje.
questions.explanation: Embora always requires the subjunctive, so the first person form is tenha and not tenho.

questions.prompt: Which connector is followed directly by an infinitive rather than by que?
questions.explanation: Apesar de takes an infinitive or a noun. The other three introduce a clause with a verb in the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *ser*: Mesmo que o bilhete ___ mais caro, prefiro o comboio.
questions.explanation: Mesmo que introduces a hypothetical concession, so the present subjunctive seja is used.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *ficar*: Embora a loja ___ longe, vale a pena ir lá.
questions.explanation: Embora demands the subjunctive, and ficar becomes fique in the third person singular.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Although it is late, I want to finish this.'
questions.explanation: Embora opens the concessive clause with the subjunctive seja, and the main clause follows after it.

activities.title: Sair ou ficar em casa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend wants to cancel your plans because of the weather, and you keep insisting using concessive clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está um tempo horrível. Não é melhor ficarmos em casa?
lines.en: The weather is horrible. Would it not be better to stay at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Embora esteja frio, apetece-me muito ir ver a exposição.
lines.en: Even though it is cold, I really feel like going to see the exhibition.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Apesar de gostar de museus, hoje estou sem energia nenhuma.
lines.en: Despite liking museums, today I have no energy at all.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mesmo que fiques só meia hora, já vale a pena.
lines.en: Even if you only stay half an hour, it is already worth it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o autocarro? Apesar da chuva, queres esperar na paragem?
lines.en: And the bus? Despite the rain, do you want to wait at the stop?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda que demore, prefiro o autocarro ao táxi.
lines.en: Even if it takes a while, I prefer the bus to a taxi.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem, eu vou. Embora ache que vamos ficar encharcados.
lines.en: All right, I will go. Even though I think we are going to get soaked.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Levo dois guarda-chuvas, mesmo que só precisemos de um.
lines.en: I will take two umbrellas, even if we only need one.

days.title: As orações causais
days.objective: Give reasons with porque, visto que, uma vez que, como and devido a, and match each connector to the right register and sentence position.
days.paretoFocus: Explaining why is the core of every complaint, apology and justification, and moving beyond porque immediately raises your level.
drills: Explain three recent decisions of yours, using a different causal connector each time.
Write two sentences with devido a plus a noun about transport or weather.
Start two sentences with Como to give the reason before the result.

items: Which causal connectors are followed by a noun rather than a clause?
Where in the sentence does Como meaning since have to appear?
Which of these connectors would you choose in a formal email?

activities.title: Visto que já cá estamos
activities.intro: Porque is the neutral everyday connector and normally comes after the main clause. Visto que and uma vez que are more formal and usually open the sentence. Como also opens the sentence, while devido a and por causa de are followed by a noun rather than a verb.

items.hr: Cheguei atrasado porque o autocarro avariou.
items.en: I arrived late because the bus broke down.
items.note: porque comes after the result

items.hr: Visto que já toda a gente chegou, podemos começar.
items.en: Given that everyone has arrived, we can start.
items.note: formal, opens the sentence

items.hr: Uma vez que conheces bem a cidade, guias tu.
items.en: Since you know the city well, you drive.
items.note: uma vez que has the same value as visto que

items.hr: Devido à greve, não houve comboios durante o dia.
items.en: Due to the strike, there were no trains during the day.
items.note: devido a plus a noun, a a -> à

items.hr: Como não tinha o telemóvel comigo, não te consegui avisar.
items.en: As I did not have my phone with me, I could not let you know.
items.note: Como only works at the start of the sentence

activities.title: Porque e por causa de
activities.intro: Practise selecting the causal connector that fits the structure and the register.

questions.prompt: ___ ao mau tempo, o voo foi cancelado.
questions.explanation: Only devido a can be followed by a noun. Here a plus o gives ao mau tempo. The other three introduce a clause with a conjugated verb.

questions.prompt: Which sentence puts the reason first in a way typical of formal writing?
options: Fiquei em casa porque estava doente.
Uma vez que o prazo terminou, não aceitamos mais candidaturas.
Não fui porque não me apetecia.
Ele saiu cedo porque estava atrasado.
questions.answer: Uma vez que o prazo terminou, não aceitamos mais candidaturas.
questions.explanation: Uma vez que belongs to formal register and opens the sentence. Porque is neutral and normally follows the main clause.

questions.prompt: Complete with the neutral everyday causal conjunction: Não fui à reunião ___ estava com febre.
questions.explanation: The reason comes after the main clause and the register is informal, which is exactly where porque is used.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing contraction of *a* and the article: Devido ___ obras, a rua está cortada.
questions.explanation: Devido a plus the feminine plural article as gives às obras.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Given that it is raining, we are staying at home.'
questions.explanation: Visto que opens the causal clause with an indicative verb, and estar a plus infinitive is the European Portuguese progressive.

activities.title: A justificar o atraso
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You arrive late at work and explain what happened using different causal connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia. A reunião já começou há vinte minutos.
lines.en: Good morning. The meeting started twenty minutes ago.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Peço desculpa. Cheguei tarde porque houve um acidente na ponte.
lines.en: I am sorry. I arrived late because there was an accident on the bridge.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não podias ter vindo de comboio?
lines.en: Could you not have come by train?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Devido à greve, não havia comboios antes das nove.
lines.en: Due to the strike, there were no trains before nine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Uma vez que já estás aqui, entra e senta-te ao meu lado.
lines.en: Since you are already here, come in and sit next to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Como perdi a primeira parte, podes resumir-me o que já foi dito?
lines.en: As I missed the first part, can you summarise what has already been said?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Visto que o orçamento foi aprovado, falámos só de prazos.
lines.en: Given that the budget was approved, we only talked about deadlines.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda bem, por causa do acidente pensei que tinha perdido tudo.
lines.en: Just as well, because of the accident I thought I had missed everything.

days.title: As orações consecutivas
days.objective: Express consequence with tão... que, tanto... que and de tal forma que, making tanto agree with the noun it accompanies.
days.paretoFocus: Consequence structures let you tell a story with impact instead of listing facts, and they are everywhere in spoken anecdotes.
drills: Describe three things from last week using tão plus adjective plus que.
Write two sentences with tanto or tanta agreeing with a noun.
Retell a queue or a crowd you were in using de tal forma que.

items: When do you use tão and when do you use tanto?
How does tanto change before pessoas, ruído and horas?
Say one thing that was so expensive that you did not buy it.

activities.title: Estava tão cansado que adormeci
activities.intro: Tão goes before an adjective or an adverb, while tanto goes with a noun or after a verb. With a noun, tanto agrees in gender and number: tanto ruído, tanta gente, tantos carros, tantas pessoas. De tal forma que and de tal maneira que work with verbs and sound slightly more formal.

items.hr: O filme foi tão longo que adormeci no fim.
items.en: The film was so long that I fell asleep at the end.
items.note: tão plus adjective

items.hr: Havia tanta gente na estação que não consegui entrar.
items.en: There were so many people at the station that I could not get in.
items.note: tanta agrees with gente

items.hr: Ele falou de tal forma que ninguém percebeu nada.
items.en: He spoke in such a way that nobody understood anything.
items.note: de tal forma que goes with a verb

items.hr: Comi tanto ao almoço que agora nem me mexo.
items.en: I ate so much at lunch that now I can barely move.
items.note: tanto after a verb, invariable

items.hr: O quarto era tão pequeno que a mala não cabia.
items.en: The room was so small that the suitcase did not fit.
items.note: tão pequeno, never tanto pequeno

activities.title: Tanto barulho que...
activities.intro: Practise choosing between tão and the agreeing forms of tanto.

questions.prompt: Havia ___ ruído na rua que não ouvi o telefone.
questions.explanation: Ruído is a masculine singular noun, so it takes tanto. Tão would only work before an adjective or an adverb.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the intensifier correctly before an adverb?
options: Ele fala tanto depressa que ninguém percebe.
Ele fala tão depressa que ninguém percebe.
Ele fala tanta depressa que ninguém percebe.
Ele fala tal depressa que ninguém percebe.
questions.answer: Ele fala tão depressa que ninguém percebe.
questions.explanation: Depressa is an adverb, and adverbs and adjectives take tão.

questions.prompt: Complete with the form that agrees with *pessoas*: Estavam ___ pessoas na fila que desistimos.
questions.explanation: Pessoas is feminine plural, so the intensifier is tantas.

questions.prompt: Complete the consecutive structure before the adjective: O exame foi ___ difícil que quase toda a gente chumbou.
questions.explanation: Difícil is an adjective, and adjectives always take tão followed by que.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The suitcase was so heavy that I could not carry it.'
questions.explanation: Tão goes before the adjective pesada, and the negative não pulls the clitic a in front of the verb group.

activities.title: O concerto de sábado
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You tell a friend about a crowded concert, using consequence structures throughout.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como foi o concerto de sábado?
lines.en: So, how was the concert on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estava tanta gente que demorámos uma hora só para entrar.
lines.en: There were so many people that it took us an hour just to get in.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sério? E ouviu-se bem?
lines.en: Really? And could you hear well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O som estava tão alto que saí de lá com os ouvidos a apitar.
lines.en: The sound was so loud that I left with my ears ringing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o bilhete? Ouvi dizer que estavam caríssimos.
lines.en: And the ticket? I heard they were extremely expensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Custaram tanto que este mês não saio mais de casa.
lines.en: They cost so much that this month I am not going out again.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pelo menos a banda tocou bem?
lines.en: At least the band played well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tocaram de tal forma que toda a gente ficou até ao fim.
lines.en: They played in such a way that everyone stayed until the end.

days.title: As orações finais
days.objective: Express purpose with para que plus subjunctive, and with para, a fim de and com o objetivo de plus infinitive, choosing according to whether the subject changes.
days.paretoFocus: Saying why you are doing something is essential at work and in any request, and para que is the structure that lets you talk about someone else's benefit.
drills: Write three sentences with para plus infinitive about your own goals.
Rewrite them with para que so that the purpose concerns another person.
Draft one formal sentence with com o objetivo de about a project or a service.

items: When do you use para plus infinitive instead of para que?
Which purpose connectors are followed by an infinitive?
Say why you are learning Portuguese, using a purpose clause.

activities.title: Para que todos percebam
activities.intro: When the subject of both parts is the same, use para plus infinitive. When the purpose concerns a different subject, use para que plus the present subjunctive. A fim de and, more formally still, com o objetivo de also take an infinitive.

items.hr: Falo devagar para que todos percebam.
items.en: I speak slowly so that everyone understands.
items.note: different subject -> para que plus conjuntivo

items.hr: Saí cedo para apanhar o comboio das oito.
items.en: I left early to catch the eight o'clock train.
items.note: same subject -> para plus infinitivo

items.hr: Enviei o formulário a fim de evitar problemas mais tarde.
items.en: I sent the form in order to avoid problems later.
items.note: a fim de plus infinitivo, formal

items.hr: A câmara criou o programa com o objetivo de apoiar as famílias.
items.en: The council created the programme with the aim of supporting families.
items.note: com o objetivo de plus infinitivo

items.hr: Vou repetir a morada para que não haja enganos.
items.en: I am going to repeat the address so that there are no mistakes.
items.note: haver -> que haja

activities.title: Para que não haja enganos
activities.intro: Practise the purpose connectors and the verb form each one requires.

questions.prompt: Escrevi tudo num papel para que tu não te ___ de nada.
questions.explanation: Para que introduces a purpose clause with a new subject, so the verb goes into the present subjunctive: esqueças.

questions.prompt: Which purpose expression is followed by an infinitive and never by que?
questions.explanation: Com o objetivo de always ends in de plus infinitive. The other three take que plus a verb in the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *ser*: Reservei a mesa para que não ___ preciso esperar.
questions.explanation: After para que the verb takes the present subjunctive, and ser becomes seja in the third person singular.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *poder*: Deixo a luz acesa para que vocês ___ ver o caminho.
questions.explanation: Poder in the present subjunctive for vocês is possam, required by para que.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I am writing it down so that you do not forget.'
questions.explanation: Estar a plus infinitive gives the progressive, and para que forces the subjunctive esqueças with the clitic te before the verb.

activities.title: Instruções para a casa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are leaving your flat to a friend for a week and explain each instruction with a purpose clause.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Deixaste-me algum papel com as instruções?
lines.en: Did you leave me any note with the instructions?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Escrevi tudo na cozinha para que não tenhas dúvidas.
lines.en: I wrote everything down in the kitchen so that you have no doubts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o aquecimento? Deixo ligado?
lines.en: And the heating? Should I leave it on?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Desliga-o à noite para poupar na conta da luz.
lines.en: Turn it off at night to save on the electricity bill.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: As plantas precisam de água todos os dias?
lines.en: Do the plants need water every day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rega-as de dois em dois dias para que não sequem.
lines.en: Water them every other day so that they do not dry out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se alguém tocar à campainha?
lines.en: And if someone rings the doorbell?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Deixei o número da vizinha a fim de resolveres tudo depressa.
lines.en: I left the neighbour's number in order for you to sort everything out quickly.

days.title: As orações temporais
days.objective: Link events in time with assim que, logo que, sempre que, mal and enquanto when talking about the past or about habits.
days.paretoFocus: These connectors let you sequence a story precisely instead of stringing everything together with e depois.
drills: Narrate yesterday evening using assim que and mal at least once each.
Write three habits with sempre que in the present tense.
Describe two things that were happening at the same time using enquanto.

items: Which connector means the moment that and is only one word long?
Which tense do you use after sempre que when you describe a habit?
Say what you did as soon as you got home yesterday.

activities.title: Assim que cheguei, liguei-te
activities.intro: Assim que, logo que and mal all mean as soon as. Sempre que means whenever and marks a repeated habit. Enquanto means while and sets a background. When these clauses refer to the past or to a habit, the verb is in the indicative, and you will meet a special form for future reference later on.

items.hr: Assim que cheguei a casa, liguei-te.
items.en: As soon as I got home, I called you.
items.note: past event -> pretérito perfeito

items.hr: Logo que soube da notícia, avisei os teus pais.
items.en: As soon as I heard the news, I told your parents.
items.note: logo que works like assim que

items.hr: Sempre que vou a Lisboa, passo por aquela pastelaria.
items.en: Whenever I go to Lisbon, I stop by that pastry shop.
items.note: habit -> presente do indicativo

items.hr: Enquanto esperava pelo autocarro, li o jornal todo.
items.en: While I was waiting for the bus, I read the whole newspaper.
items.note: enquanto plus imperfeito for the background

items.hr: Mal saímos de casa, começou a chover.
items.en: The moment we left home, it started to rain.
items.note: mal is the shortest way to say as soon as

activities.title: Sempre que chove
activities.intro: Practise the time connectors and the tense that goes with each type of event.

questions.prompt: ___ que vejo este filme, choro no fim.
questions.explanation: The sentence describes something repeated, and sempre que is the connector for whenever.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes a habit rather than a single past event?
options: Logo que ele entrou, todos se calaram.
Sempre que como marisco, fico com alergia.
Mal ouvi o barulho, corri para a janela.
questions.answer: Sempre que como marisco, fico com alergia.
questions.explanation: Sempre que with the present indicative marks a repeated situation. The other three describe one completed moment in the past.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct past form of *receber*: Logo que ___ o teu recado, respondi.
questions.explanation: The action is a single completed moment in the past, so the pretérito perfeito simples recebi is used.

questions.prompt: Complete with the connector that means *while*: ___ jantávamos, falámos do trabalho.
questions.explanation: Enquanto introduces a background action running at the same time as the main one.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'As soon as the film ended, we left.'
questions.explanation: Assim que opens the time clause with the pretérito perfeito acabou, and ir embora means to leave.

activities.title: A noite de ontem
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks what you did last night and you sequence the events with time connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ontem tentei ligar-te e não atendeste.
lines.en: Yesterday I tried to call you and you did not answer.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mal cheguei a casa, adormeci no sofá.
lines.en: The moment I got home, I fell asleep on the sofa.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trabalhaste até tarde outra vez?
lines.en: Did you work late again?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sempre que há fecho de mês, saio depois das nove.
lines.en: Whenever there is a month end closing, I leave after nine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E jantaste alguma coisa?
lines.en: And did you have any dinner?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Enquanto esperava pelo comboio, comi uma sandes na estação.
lines.en: While I was waiting for the train, I had a sandwich at the station.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Devias ter avisado que estavas tão cansado.
lines.en: You should have said you were so tired.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Assim que acordei esta manhã, vi as tuas chamadas e escrevi-te.
lines.en: As soon as I woke up this morning, I saw your calls and wrote to you.

days.title: Os pronomes contraídos
days.objective: Combine an indirect and a direct object pronoun into the contracted forms mo, to, lho, lha, mas and lhas.
days.paretoFocus: Native speakers contract these pronouns automatically, so recognising them is the difference between following a real conversation and losing the thread.
drills: Write the full contraction table for me, te and lhe with o, a, os and as.
Rewrite five sentences replacing both objects with a single contracted pronoun.
Say three things you lent or gave to someone using the contracted forms.

items: What do me plus a and lhe plus os become?
Which pronoun comes first in the contraction, the direct or the indirect one?
Say give it to me tomorrow using one contracted pronoun.

activities.title: Empresta-mo, por favor
activities.intro: When a verb carries two object pronouns at once, one for the receiver and one for the thing, Portuguese fuses them into one word. The indirect pronoun comes first: me plus o gives mo, te plus a gives ta, lhe plus o gives lho, and lhes behaves like lhe. Everything after the verb is attached with a hyphen.

items.hr: Se queres o meu guarda-chuva, empresto-to já.
items.en: If you want my umbrella, I will lend it to you right now.
items.note: te plus o -> to

items.hr: A Ana pediu-me o endereço e eu dei-lho ontem.
items.en: Ana asked me for the address and I gave it to her yesterday.
items.note: lhe plus o -> lho

items.hr: Ainda não me devolveste as chaves. Devolve-mas amanhã.
items.en: You still have not given me back the keys. Give them back to me tomorrow.
items.note: me plus as -> mas

items.hr: O senhor precisa do recibo? Nós enviamos-lho por correio.
items.en: Do you need the receipt, sir? We will send it to you by post.
items.note: lho also works for formal o senhor

items.hr: Eu tenho as fotografias do casamento e mostro-tas depois.
items.en: I have the wedding photos and I will show them to you later.
items.note: te plus as -> tas

activities.title: Dei-lho ontem
activities.intro: Practise fusing the indirect and direct pronouns into one form.

questions.prompt: Pediste-me o número de telefone. Eu já ___ dei na semana passada.
questions.explanation: The number goes to you, so the indirect pronoun is te and the direct one is o, which fuse into to.

questions.prompt: The pronouns *lhe* and *as* fuse into which single form?
questions.explanation: Lhe keeps its lh and takes the ending of the direct pronoun, so lhe plus as gives lhas.

questions.prompt: Use one contracted pronoun standing for the bag and for her: A Rita emprestou-me a mala e ainda não ___ devolvi.
questions.explanation: Lhe refers to Rita and a refers to a mala, which fuse into lha.

questions.prompt: Fill in the single pronoun form required here: Precisava do passaporte e o meu irmão entregou-___ ontem à noite.
questions.explanation: The passport is handed to me, so me plus o gives mo, attached to the verb with a hyphen.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Lend it to me tomorrow morning, please.'
questions.explanation: In an affirmative command the pronouns attach to the verb, and me plus o gives mo.

activities.title: As coisas emprestadas
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a flatmate sort out who lent what to whom, using contracted pronouns.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sabes onde está o meu carregador? Já não o vejo há dias.
lines.en: Do you know where my charger is? I have not seen it for days.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Emprestei-o ao João. Peço-lho amanhã.
lines.en: I lent it to João. I will ask him for it tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E os livros da biblioteca? Prometeste devolver-mos esta semana.
lines.en: And the library books? You promised to give them back to me this week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Já os tenho na mochila. Devolvo-tos hoje à noite.
lines.en: I already have them in my backpack. I will give them back to you tonight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A tua irmã ainda tem a minha bicicleta?
lines.en: Does your sister still have my bicycle?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tem, mas eu peço-lha no domingo quando for lá a casa.
lines.en: She does, but I will ask her for it on Sunday when I go over there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E aquelas fotografias da viagem, mostras-mas quando?
lines.en: And those holiday photos, when are you showing them to me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mostro-tas ao jantar, estão todas no telemóvel.
lines.en: I will show them to you at dinner, they are all on my phone.

days.title: A colocação dos clíticos com dois verbos
days.objective: Place object pronouns correctly when there are two verbs, attaching them to the infinitive or moving them before the first verb when an attractor appears.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every sentence with vou, tenho de, quero or estou a also has a pronoun, so getting the position right makes your speech instantly more fluent.
drills: Write five sentences with vou plus infinitive and a pronoun attached to the infinitive.
Make each of those sentences negative and move the pronoun accordingly.
Write three sentences with estar a plus infinitive and an object pronoun.

items: Which words pull the pronoun in front of the verb group?
What happens to the infinitive ending when o or a is attached to it?
Say I am reading it right now in two different positions.

activities.title: Vou fazê-lo ou vou-o fazer
activities.intro: With an auxiliary plus an infinitive, the pronoun normally attaches to the infinitive. If the sentence contains an attractor such as não, nunca, ninguém, já, só, que or a question word, the pronoun moves in front of the whole verb group. When o, a, os or as attach to an infinitive, the final r drops and the vowel takes an accent.

items.hr: Vou enviar-te o mapa esta noite.
items.en: I am going to send you the map tonight.
items.note: no attractor -> pronoun on the infinitive

items.hr: Não te vou enviar o mapa agora.
items.en: I am not going to send you the map now.
items.note: não pulls the pronoun to the front

items.hr: Estou a lê-lo neste momento.
items.en: I am reading it right now.
items.note: ler plus o -> lê-lo

items.hr: Ela quer ajudar-nos com a mudança de casa.
items.en: She wants to help us with the house move.
items.note: querer plus infinitivo, pronoun attached

items.hr: Ninguém me quis dizer a verdade.
items.en: Nobody wanted to tell me the truth.
items.note: ninguém attracts the pronoun before quis

activities.title: Onde fica o pronome
activities.intro: Practise deciding where the object pronoun goes when two verbs are involved.

questions.prompt: Choose the correctly formed negative sentence.
questions.explanation: Não is an attractor, so the pronoun te has to stand before the verb group and never attach to vou.

questions.prompt: Tenho de ___ hoje sem falta, using *fazer* with the pronoun *o*.
questions.explanation: Before o the infinitive loses its final r, the pronoun becomes lo and the stressed e takes a circumflex.

questions.prompt: Complete with the first person singular object pronoun: Ela nunca ___ quis dizer nada sobre o assunto.
questions.explanation: Nunca is an attractor, so the pronoun stands in front of the verb group rather than attached to the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Write the infinitive of *comprar* joined to the third person object pronoun: Ainda não decidi se vou ___.
questions.explanation: The final r drops, the pronoun takes the form lo and the a of the stem receives an acute accent.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'She does not want to tell us the truth.'
questions.explanation: Não attracts the pronoun nos to a position before quer, so it cannot stay attached to dizer.

activities.title: A tratar da mudança
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are organising a house move with a friend and every answer uses a pronoun with two verbs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já falaste com a empresa das mudanças?
lines.en: Have you spoken to the moving company yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não, mas vou ligar-lhes esta tarde.
lines.en: Not yet, but I am going to call them this afternoon.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as caixas? Precisas de ajuda para as encher?
lines.en: And the boxes? Do you need help filling them?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estou a enchê-las desde ontem, já só faltam duas.
lines.en: I have been filling them since yesterday, only two are left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O contrato de arrendamento, já o assinaste?
lines.en: The rental contract, have you signed it yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não o quis assinar sem ler tudo com calma.
lines.en: I did not want to sign it without reading everything carefully.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Queres que eu leve as plantas no meu carro?
lines.en: Do you want me to take the plants in my car?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Podes levá-las, mas não me tragas o sofá, esse fica.
lines.en: You can take them, but do not bring me the sofa, that one stays.

days.title: Os verbos com preposição
days.objective: Use verbs that require a fixed preposition and join that preposition to demonstratives and pronouns in forms such as nisto, disso and naquele.
days.paretoFocus: Pensar nisso, gostar disto and precisar disso appear in almost every conversation, and the fused forms are impossible to guess from the separate words.
drills: List ten verbs you use often and write the preposition each one takes.
Write five sentences using nisto, nisso, disto or disso.
Turn three of your sentences into questions starting with Em que or De que.

items: What do em plus isso and de plus aquilo become?
Which preposition follows precisar, gostar and depender?
Say I never thought about that using a fused form.

activities.title: Penso nisso todos os dias
activities.intro: Many Portuguese verbs demand a fixed preposition, and that preposition then merges with a following demonstrative or article. Em plus isto gives nisto, de plus isso gives disso, and em plus aquele gives naquele. When the complement is a whole clause, precisar and gostar often drop de before que.

items.hr: Estou sempre a pensar nisso, não consigo evitar.
items.en: I keep thinking about that, I cannot help it.
items.note: pensar em, em plus isso -> nisso

items.hr: Não gosto disto, prefiro o outro modelo.
items.en: I do not like this, I prefer the other model.
items.note: gostar de, de plus isto -> disto

items.hr: Preciso que me ajudes com a mudança no sábado.
items.en: I need you to help me with the move on Saturday.
items.note: preciso de plus nome, mas preciso que plus conjuntivo

items.hr: Ela concordou com a nossa proposta sem discutir.
items.en: She agreed with our proposal without arguing.
items.note: concordar com

items.hr: Já reparaste naquele prédio novo ao pé do mercado?
items.en: Have you noticed that new building next to the market?
items.note: reparar em, em plus aquele -> naquele

activities.title: Preposições obrigatórias
activities.intro: Practise the preposition each verb demands and the forms it fuses into.

questions.prompt: Nunca me esqueço ___ dia em que nos conhecemos.
questions.explanation: Esquecer-se takes de, and de plus o gives do.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the preposition that *depender* requires?
questions.explanation: Depender always governs de, and before a stressed pronoun the form is de ti.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word that joins a preposition and a demonstrative: Ainda não pensei ___, dá-me tempo para decidir.
questions.explanation: Pensar takes em, and em fuses with isto or isso to give nisto or nisso.

questions.prompt: Complete with the preposition required by *assistir* meaning to watch: Ontem assisti ___ jogo todo.
questions.explanation: Assistir in the sense of watching governs a, and a plus o gives ao.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I need you to help me with this.'
questions.explanation: Precisar drops de before a que clause, and the clause takes the present subjunctive ajudes.

activities.title: A decidir as férias
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a friend discuss holiday plans and every line uses a verb with its fixed preposition.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já pensaste no destino das férias?
lines.en: Have you thought about the holiday destination yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Penso nisso todos os dias, mas ainda não decidi nada.
lines.en: I think about it every day, but I have not decided anything yet.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gostas mais da serra ou do litoral?
lines.en: Do you prefer the mountains or the coast?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gosto muito da serra, mas tudo depende do preço do alojamento.
lines.en: I really like the mountains, but everything depends on the price of the accommodation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Precisas de reservar já, senão fica tudo cheio.
lines.en: You need to book now, otherwise everything will be full.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo contigo, mas preciso que me digas as tuas datas.
lines.en: I agree with you, but I need you to tell me your dates.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Reparaste naquele hotel perto da estação de comboios?
lines.en: Did you notice that hotel near the train station?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Reparei, e não me esqueci disso quando fiz as contas.
lines.en: I did, and I did not forget about that when I did the maths.

days.title: A voz passiva com ser e a passiva com se
days.objective: Choose between the passive with ser and the passive with se, and make the verb agree with its subject in the se construction.
days.paretoFocus: Notices, adverts and news reports are full of vendem-se and foi aprovado, and each form answers a different question about who did it.
drills: Write three small ads using the se passive, one singular and two plural.
Rewrite two of them with ser plus past participle and an agent.
Find three everyday signs you could translate using aluga-se or vende-se.

items: Why is it vendem-se apartamentos and not vende-se apartamentos?
Which passive form lets you name the agent, and with which preposition?
Say the results were published last week in the passive.

activities.title: Vendem-se apartamentos
activities.intro: The passive with ser uses ser plus a past participle that agrees with the subject, and the agent can be added with por. The passive with se attaches se to the verb and hides the agent completely, but the verb still agrees with the subject that follows it.

items.hr: Vendem-se apartamentos novos nesta zona.
items.en: New flats are for sale in this area.
items.note: plural subject -> vendem-se

items.hr: Aluga-se quarto mobilado a estudantes.
items.en: Furnished room for rent to students.
items.note: singular subject -> aluga-se

items.hr: O relatório foi entregue pelo diretor na sexta-feira.
items.en: The report was handed in by the director on Friday.
items.note: ser plus particípio plus por

items.hr: Aqui fala-se português e espanhol.
items.en: Portuguese and Spanish are spoken here.
items.note: the agent is left unsaid

items.hr: As obras foram concluídas no mês passado.
items.en: The works were completed last month.
items.note: concluídas agrees with as obras

activities.title: Precisa-se de empregados
activities.intro: Practise agreement in the se passive and the choice between the two passive forms.

questions.prompt: ___ empregados de mesa com experiência para o verão.
questions.explanation: Empregados is the plural subject of the sentence, so the verb goes into the plural and se attaches with a hyphen.

questions.prompt: Which sentence actually names who performed the action?
options: A carta foi escrita pela advogada.
questions.answer: A carta foi escrita pela advogada.
questions.explanation: Only the passive with ser can add an agent introduced by por, here fused as pela advogada.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of *ser*: As chaves ___ entregues ao novo inquilino na semana passada.
questions.explanation: The subject as chaves is plural and the time reference is a finished past, so ser takes the form foram.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb so that it agrees with its subject: Nesta loja consertam-se relógios e ___-se chaves.
questions.explanation: Chaves is plural, so the verb in the se passive must also be plural: fazem-se chaves.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The results were published last week.'
questions.explanation: The participle publicados agrees with the masculine plural subject os resultados.

activities.title: Os anúncios da montra
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are reading the notices in a shop window with a friend and you comment on them using both passive forms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Olha, aqui diz aluga-se quarto a partir de setembro.
lines.en: Look, here it says room to rent from September.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E ao lado vendem-se móveis usados. Isso interessa-me.
lines.en: And next to it used furniture is for sale. That interests me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não dizem quem os vende, pois não?
lines.en: They do not say who is selling them, do they?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não. Se fosse importante, escreviam que são vendidos pela loja.
lines.en: No. If it mattered, they would write that they are sold by the shop.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Este anúncio diz que precisa-se de ajudante de cozinha.
lines.en: This advert says a kitchen assistant is needed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E este diz procuram-se estudantes para o fim de semana.
lines.en: And this one says students are wanted for the weekend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A minha candidatura foi enviada ontem por email.
lines.en: My application was sent yesterday by email.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então as respostas serão dadas na próxima semana, imagino.
lines.en: So the answers will be given next week, I imagine.

days.title: A nominalização: do verbo ao nome
days.objective: Build nouns from verbs with the suffixes -cao, -mento, -ura and -a, and use them to write in a denser, more formal style.
days.paretoFocus: Headlines, notices and formal writing prefer nouns to verbs, so knowing the suffix patterns doubles your vocabulary without new words.
drills: Turn ten verbs you know into nouns and mark the gender of each one.
Rewrite three sentences so the verb becomes the subject noun of the sentence.
Write two short headline style sentences using only nouns and one verb.

items: Which suffix creates feminine nouns almost without exception?
Give the noun matching construir, crescer and chegar.
Rewrite the factory closed down as a sentence with a noun.

activities.title: Do verbo ao nome
activities.intro: Portuguese builds nouns from verbs with a small set of suffixes. Words in -cao and -ura are feminine, words in -mento are masculine, and some verbs simply drop their ending, like chegar giving a chegada. Using these nouns lets you say more in fewer words.

items.hr: A construção da nova ponte demorou três anos.
items.en: The construction of the new bridge took three years.
items.note: construir -> a construção

items.hr: O crescimento da cidade tem sido muito rápido.
items.en: The growth of the city has been very fast.
items.note: crescer -> o crescimento

items.hr: A abertura da loja está marcada para segunda-feira.
items.en: The opening of the shop is set for Monday.
items.note: abrir -> a abertura

items.hr: A chegada do comboio foi anunciada com atraso.
items.en: The arrival of the train was announced late.
items.note: chegar -> a chegada

items.hr: A escolha do candidato gerou muita discussão.
items.en: The choice of candidate caused a lot of discussion.
items.note: escolher -> a escolha

activities.title: O nome certo
activities.intro: Practise forming nouns from verbs and knowing their gender.

questions.prompt: Which noun is correctly formed from *decidir*?
questions.explanation: Decidir has an irregular stem in the noun, giving decisão, which is feminine like all nouns in this suffix.

questions.prompt: Nouns such as construção and decisão are normally of which gender?
questions.explanation: This suffix produces feminine nouns, so they take a and uma: a construção, uma decisão.

questions.prompt: Write the noun formed from *desenvolver*: O ___ do projeto levou dois anos.
questions.explanation: Verbs in -er often take the masculine suffix -mento, giving desenvolvimento.

questions.prompt: Write the noun formed from *aumentar*: O ___ dos preços preocupa toda a gente.
questions.explanation: Some verbs simply drop the infinitive ending, so aumentar gives the masculine noun aumento.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The closing of the factory surprised everyone.'
questions.explanation: Encerrar gives the masculine noun encerramento, which takes the article o and the complement da fábrica.

activities.title: As notícias da cidade
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You comment on local news with a neighbour, replacing verbs with nouns wherever possible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ouviu falar do encerramento da fábrica velha?
lines.en: Have you heard about the closing of the old factory?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ouvi. Dizem que a decisão foi tomada há meses.
lines.en: I have. They say the decision was made months ago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a construção do novo centro de saúde?
lines.en: And the construction of the new health centre?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A abertura está prevista para o próximo verão.
lines.en: The opening is expected for next summer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O crescimento desta zona tem sido enorme.
lines.en: The growth of this area has been enormous.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois tem, e o aumento das rendas também.
lines.en: It certainly has, and so has the rise in rents.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já viu a melhoria dos transportes na avenida?
lines.en: Have you seen the improvement of the transport on the avenue?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vi, mas a escolha do percurso não agradou a toda a gente.
lines.en: I have, but the choice of route did not please everyone.

days.title: Os diminutivos e os aumentativos
days.objective: Form and interpret diminutives in -inho and -zinho and augmentatives in -ao, and recognise the affection or the irony they carry.
days.paretoFocus: Portuguese speakers use these endings constantly in everyday speech, and missing them means missing the speaker's attitude.
drills: Form the diminutive of ten common nouns and note which need -zinho.
Write three sentences where the diminutive softens a request.
List three augmentatives you have heard and say what feeling each one adds.

items: When do you use -zinho instead of -inho?
What does the diminutive add besides small size?
Say wait a little moment in a friendly way.

activities.title: Um cafezinho e um carrão
activities.intro: The diminutive is usually -inho or -inha, added after dropping the final vowel. Words ending in a stressed vowel, in e or in a consonant take -zinho instead. Beyond size, the diminutive adds warmth or softens a request, while the augmentative in -ao suggests something large and sometimes rough.

items.hr: Vamos beber um cafezinho antes do trabalho.
items.en: Let us have a nice little coffee before work.
items.note: café -> cafezinho, stressed vowel takes -zinho

items.hr: Espera só um bocadinho, por favor.
items.en: Just wait a little moment, please.
items.note: bocado -> bocadinho, softens the request

items.hr: Ela mora numa casinha branca perto do mar.
items.en: She lives in a little white house near the sea.
items.note: casa -> casinha

items.hr: O meu tio tem um carrão que gasta imenso.
items.en: My uncle has a huge car that uses a lot of fuel.
items.note: carro -> carrão, large

items.hr: A mãezinha dele liga-lhe todos os dias.
items.en: His dear mother calls him every day.
items.note: mãe -> mãezinha, affection

activities.title: Pequenino ou enorme
activities.intro: Practise forming diminutives and augmentatives and reading the attitude behind them.

questions.prompt: The diminutive of *mão* is formed how?
questions.explanation: Words ending in a nasal diphthong take -zinho or -zinha, so mão gives mãozinha.

questions.prompt: Which ending usually adds affection or the idea of small size?
questions.explanation: The suffix -inho is the standard diminutive and often carries warmth rather than literal smallness.

questions.prompt: Write the diminutive of *pouco*: Fala mais devagar e um ___ mais alto.
questions.explanation: The c becomes qu before the suffix so that the sound stays hard, giving pouquinho.

questions.prompt: Write the augmentative of *palavra* used for a swear word: Ele disse um ___ e saiu porta fora.
questions.explanation: The augmentative suffix turns palavra into palavrão, which in everyday use means a rude word.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'They live in a big old house near the river.'
questions.explanation: Casa becomes the augmentative casarão, which is masculine, so it takes num and antigo.

activities.title: No café da esquina
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You order at a neighbourhood café and the small talk is full of diminutives and augmentatives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia. O que vai ser?
lines.en: Good morning. What will it be?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Um cafezinho e uma torradinha com manteiga, se faz favor.
lines.en: A small coffee and a little slice of toast with butter, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quer o pão inteiro ou só metade?
lines.en: Do you want the whole bread or just half?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Só metade, hoje estou com um bocadinho de pressa.
lines.en: Just half, today I am in a bit of a hurry.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Viu o carrão que estacionou ali à porta?
lines.en: Did you see the huge car that parked over there by the door?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vi. E ocupou dois lugares, o que me deixou irritadinho.
lines.en: I did. And it took up two spaces, which left me rather annoyed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O dono mora naquele casarão do fim da rua.
lines.en: The owner lives in that big house at the end of the street.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ah, o da senhora que tem aquele cãozinho branco?
lines.en: Ah, the one belonging to the lady with that little white dog?

days.title: As conjunções coordenativas
days.objective: Join clauses of equal weight with mas, porém, nem, portanto and ou seja, and use the correlative pair não só... como também.
days.paretoFocus: Coordinating conjunctions are the cheapest way to make long sentences sound organised instead of rambling.
drills: Rewrite three of your short sentences into one long sentence using coordination.
Write two sentences with não só and como também.
Reformulate one complicated idea of yours with ou seja.

items: Which conjunction is the more formal equivalent of mas?
What does nem replace in a negative sentence?
Give two ways of introducing a reformulation.

activities.title: Mas, porém, ou seja
activities.intro: Coordinating conjunctions join two parts of equal weight. Mas is the neutral contrast, while porém, contudo and no entanto are more formal and often sit after a comma. Portanto introduces a conclusion, nem joins two negatives, and ou seja or isto é rephrase what was just said.

items.hr: Queria muito ir, mas não tenho tempo nenhum.
items.en: I would really like to go, but I have no time at all.
items.note: mas, neutral contrast

items.hr: O plano era bom, porém ninguém o seguiu.
items.en: The plan was good, however nobody followed it.
items.note: porém, more formal than mas

items.hr: O prazo acaba amanhã, ou seja, temos de nos despachar.
items.en: The deadline ends tomorrow, that is, we have to hurry up.
items.note: ou seja rephrases the idea

items.hr: Não só chegou tarde, como também se esqueceu dos documentos.
items.en: Not only did he arrive late, he also forgot the documents.
items.note: não só... como também

items.hr: Ele não estuda nem trabalha, portanto tem o dia todo livre.
items.en: He neither studies nor works, so he has the whole day free.
items.note: nem joins negatives, portanto concludes

activities.title: Ligar as ideias
activities.intro: Practise choosing the coordinating conjunction that fits the logical relation.

questions.prompt: O comboio estava cheio, ___ conseguimos lugar sentado.
questions.explanation: The two facts contrast with each other, and porém is the formal contrast conjunction after a comma.

questions.prompt: Which expression introduces a rephrasing of what was just said?
questions.explanation: Isto é and ou seja restate the previous idea in other words. The other three mark contrast.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conjunction that joins two negative ideas: Ele não bebe álcool ___ fuma.
questions.explanation: Nem carries the negation across to the second verb, so não is not repeated.

questions.prompt: Complete the correlative structure: Não só me ajudou a estudar, ___ também me emprestou os apontamentos.
questions.explanation: Não só is regularly answered by como também in European Portuguese.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The shop is closed, therefore we will come back tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: Portanto introduces the conclusion that follows from the first clause.

activities.title: A organizar o fim de semana
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a friend weigh up weekend plans, linking every idea with a coordinating conjunction.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Podemos ir à praia no sábado ou ficar pela cidade.
lines.en: We can go to the beach on Saturday or stay in the city.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gostava da praia, mas dizem que vai chover a tarde toda.
lines.en: I would like the beach, but they say it is going to rain all afternoon.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então ficamos cá. E ao domingo?
lines.en: Then we stay here. And on Sunday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ao domingo não trabalho nem tenho compromissos, portanto decides tu.
lines.en: On Sunday I neither work nor have commitments, so you decide.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O museu é gratuito até à uma, porém fecha às cinco.
lines.en: The museum is free until one, however it closes at five.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ou seja, temos de sair de casa antes do meio-dia.
lines.en: That is, we have to leave home before midday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A exposição é grande e há visitas guiadas.
lines.en: The exhibition is big and there are guided tours.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não só vemos a exposição, como também almoçamos por lá.
lines.en: Not only will we see the exhibition, we will also have lunch there.

days.title: Rever os tempos do passado
days.objective: Choose confidently between pretérito perfeito simples, pretérito imperfeito and pretérito mais-que-perfeito composto when telling a story.
days.paretoFocus: Narration is the backbone of any conversation about your life, and picking the wrong past tense is the mistake that most often confuses listeners.
drills: Tell the story of a trip using all three past tenses at least once each.
Underline every imperfect in your story and check it really is background or habit.
Rewrite two sentences so that one action clearly happened before the other.

items: Which tense carries the story forward and which one sets the scene?
When do you need tinha plus past participle?
Retell your morning in three sentences using two different past tenses.

activities.title: Escolher o tempo certo
activities.intro: The pretérito perfeito simples moves the story forward with completed events. The imperfeito paints the background, describes states and marks habits. The mais-que-perfeito composto with tinha plus past participle places an action before another past action.

items.hr: Ontem fui ao médico e depois voltei para casa.
items.en: Yesterday I went to the doctor and then came back home.
items.note: completed events in sequence

items.hr: Quando era pequeno, ia à praia todos os verões.
items.en: When I was little, I went to the beach every summer.
items.note: imperfeito for a past habit

items.hr: Estava a chover quando saímos de casa.
items.en: It was raining when we left the house.
items.note: background imperfeito plus event

items.hr: Quando cheguei ao escritório, ele já tinha saído.
items.en: When I got to the office, he had already left.
items.note: tinha saído happens first

items.hr: Ele contou que já tinha visitado o Porto duas vezes.
items.en: He said he had already visited Porto twice.
items.note: earlier than the reporting verb

activities.title: Três tempos, uma história
activities.intro: Practise matching each past tense to the job it does in a narrative.

questions.prompt: Quando cheguei ao cinema, o filme já ___.
questions.explanation: The film starting happened before the arrival, which is exactly what the mais-que-perfeito composto expresses.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes something repeated over a period in the past?
options: Aos domingos almoçávamos sempre em casa da avó.
questions.answer: Aos domingos almoçávamos sempre em casa da avó.
questions.explanation: The imperfeito almoçávamos with sempre marks a habit, while the other options refer to single or earlier events.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct past form of *ser*: Naquela altura, a vida ___ muito mais simples.
questions.explanation: The sentence describes a state that lasted, so the imperfeito era is the right choice.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pluperfect form of *perder*: Ele contou-me que ___ o telemóvel no comboio.
questions.explanation: The loss happened before he told the story, so it takes tinha plus the past participle perdido.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'When we arrived, they had already eaten.'
questions.explanation: Chegámos is the completed event and tinham comido places the meal earlier still.

activities.title: O fim de semana na aldeia
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You tell a colleague about a weekend away, mixing the three past tenses naturally.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como correu o fim de semana na aldeia?
lines.en: So, how did the weekend in the village go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Correu bem. Chegámos ao sábado de manhã e o tempo estava ótimo.
lines.en: It went well. We arrived on Saturday morning and the weather was great.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ficaram em casa de familiares?
lines.en: Did you stay at relatives' place?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim. Quando chegámos, a minha tia já tinha feito o almoço.
lines.en: Yes. When we arrived, my aunt had already made lunch.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Costumavas passar lá as férias em criança, não era?
lines.en: You used to spend your holidays there as a child, did you not?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ia lá todos os verões e brincava no rio a tarde inteira.
lines.en: I went there every summer and played in the river all afternoon.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E voltaram no domingo à noite?
lines.en: And did you come back on Sunday evening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Voltámos, mas apanhámos trânsito porque tinha havido um acidente.
lines.en: We did, but we hit traffic because there had been an accident.

days.title: A saúde e o bem-estar: falar dos seus hábitos
days.objective: Describe your daily health habits and how they make you feel, using costumar plus infinitive and reflexive verbs with correct European Portuguese pronoun placement.
