days.paretoFocus: Habits come up in every check-up, every catch-up with a friend and every small-talk opening, and costumar plus a reflexive verb covers most of what you need to say.
drills: Write five sentences with costumar about your own weekday routine, three positive and two negative.
Take three of those sentences and rewrite them with nunca or não, moving the pronoun in front of the verb.
Say out loud three things that faz bem and three things that faz mal to you, with a reason for each.

items: How do you say 'I usually go to bed early' without using normalmente?
Where does the pronoun go in 'nunca ... deito' and why?
Give two ways to say an activity does you good.

activities.title: Falar dos hábitos de saúde
activities.intro: Portuguese speakers describe habits with costumar plus an infinitive rather than with an adverb. Reflexive pronouns are attached to the end of the verb with a hyphen in a plain affirmative sentence, but they jump in front of the verb after a negative word such as não, nunca or ninguém. Learn the two patterns together and your habit sentences will sound native straight away.

items.hr: Costumo deitar-me antes da meia-noite durante a semana.
items.en: I usually go to bed before midnight during the week.
items.note: costumar + infinitivo = 'usually do'

items.hr: Não me sinto bem quando passo o dia todo sentado.
items.en: I don't feel well when I spend the whole day sitting down.
items.note: não pulls the pronoun forward: não me sinto

items.hr: Faz-me bem caminhar meia hora ao fim do dia.
items.en: Walking for half an hour at the end of the day does me good.
items.note: fazer bem / fazer mal a alguém

items.hr: Ando a tentar reduzir o café e o açúcar.
items.en: I am trying to cut down on coffee and sugar.
items.note: andar a + infinitivo = an ongoing effort

items.hr: De vez em quando salto o pequeno-almoço, mas depois arrependo-me.
items.en: Every now and then I skip breakfast, but then I regret it.
items.note: arrepender-se, enclisis in an affirmative clause

activities.title: Hábitos e bem-estar
activities.intro: Practise habitual expressions and reflexive pronoun placement.

questions.prompt: Choose the natural European Portuguese for 'I usually train twice a week.'
options: Costumo treinar duas vezes por semana.
Estou treinando duas vezes por semana.
Uso treinar duas vezes por semana.
Tenho de treinar duas vezes por semana.
questions.answer: Costumo treinar duas vezes por semana.
questions.explanation: Habits use costumar plus the infinitive. The gerund form belongs to Brazilian usage, usar does not express habit, and ter de expresses obligation, not habit.

questions.prompt: Which sentence places the reflexive pronoun correctly after a negative word?
options: Nunca me deito depois da meia-noite.
Nunca me deito-me depois da meia-noite.
Nunca deito eu depois da meia-noite.
questions.answer: Nunca me deito depois da meia-noite.
questions.explanation: Negative words such as nunca, não and ninguém attract the pronoun in front of the verb, so the hyphenated ending is dropped.

questions.prompt: Complete with the reflexive verb meaning to go to bed, pronoun included: 'Costumo ___ antes da meia-noite.'
questions.explanation: The pronoun attaches to the end of the infinitive with a hyphen when the infinitive follows another verb such as costumar.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb that expresses being in the habit of something: 'Ao domingo, ___ almoçar em casa dos meus pais.'
questions.explanation: Costumar in the present, first person costumo, plus an infinitive is the standard way to state a habit.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'Walking every day does me good.'
questions.explanation: The pronoun me attaches to faz, and the infinitive caminhar works as the subject of the sentence.

activities.title: Na consulta de rotina
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A doctor asks about your daily habits and you answer using costumar and reflexive verbs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, conte-me: como é o seu dia normal?
lines.en: So, tell me: what is a normal day like for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Costumo levantar-me às sete e trabalhar sentado até às seis.
lines.en: I usually get up at seven and work sitting down until six.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E faz algum exercício durante a semana?
lines.en: And do you do any exercise during the week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ando a tentar caminhar todos os dias, porque me faz bem às costas.
lines.en: I am trying to walk every day, because it does my back good.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dorme bem? Acorda descansado?
lines.en: Do you sleep well? Do you wake up rested?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nem sempre. Se me deitar tarde, não me sinto bem no dia seguinte.
lines.en: Not always. If I go to bed late, I don't feel well the next day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Aconselho-o a deitar-se sempre à mesma hora durante um mês.
lines.en: I advise you to go to bed at the same time every day for a month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. Vou deixar de ver o telemóvel antes de dormir.
lines.en: Agreed. I am going to stop looking at my phone before sleeping.

days.title: A habitação e a vida urbana: vantagens e inconvenientes
days.objective: Weigh up the good and bad sides of where you live, using por um lado / por outro lado, apesar de plus infinitive and tanto que.
days.paretoFocus: Balanced pros and cons are the backbone of B1 speaking: the same three connectors work for housing, jobs, cities and technology.
drills: Write one advantage and one inconvenience of your neighbourhood, linked with por um lado and por outro.
Turn three sentences of yours into apesar de plus infinitive, keeping the same subject in both halves.
Describe your ideal home in four sentences, including one with tanto que.

items: Which preposition always follows apesar when it is followed by an infinitive?
Say 'the rents went up so much that people left' in Portuguese.
Give two connectors that introduce the opposite side of an argument.

activities.title: Vantagens e inconvenientes de viver na cidade
activities.intro: To weigh two sides of a question, Portuguese uses the pair por um lado and por outro lado. Concessions are usually made with apesar de plus an infinitive, which avoids the subjunctive entirely when the subject is the same. Tanto que and o pior é que let you add intensity and a complaint.

items.hr: Por um lado tenho tudo à mão, por outro o barulho é constante.
items.en: On the one hand I have everything close by, on the other the noise is constant.
items.note: por um lado ... por outro (lado)

items.hr: Apesar de ser pequeno, o apartamento tem muita luz.
items.en: Although it is small, the flat gets a lot of light.
items.note: apesar de + infinitivo, no subjunctive needed

items.hr: O que mais me agrada no bairro é haver comércio tradicional.
items.en: What I like most about the neighbourhood is that there are traditional shops.
items.note: haver as an infinitive = 'there being'

items.hr: As rendas subiram tanto que muita gente foi viver para os arredores.
items.en: Rents went up so much that a lot of people went to live in the outskirts.
items.note: tanto que = so much that

items.hr: O pior é que não há estacionamento nenhum à noite.
items.en: The worst thing is that there is no parking at all at night.
items.note: o pior é que + indicativo

activities.title: A cidade e o campo
activities.intro: Practise contrast connectors and housing vocabulary.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces the second, contrasting side of an argument?
questions.explanation: Por outro lado balances por um lado. De facto confirms, ou seja reformulates and além disso adds a similar idea.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word: 'Apesar ___ viver longe, chego sempre a horas.'
questions.explanation: Apesar is always followed by de before an infinitive. Apesar de que is not used in careful European Portuguese.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for the monthly payment a tenant makes: 'A ___ deste apartamento é de setecentos euros por mês.'
questions.explanation: In Portugal the monthly payment for a rented home is a renda; aluguer refers more to the act of renting or to short-term hire.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun that opens the first side of an argument: 'Por um ___, o centro é caríssimo.'
questions.explanation: The fixed pair is por um lado, followed later by por outro lado.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'The flat is small, but it has a lot of light.'
questions.explanation: Mas joins two full clauses, and the second one keeps the same subject without repeating it.

activities.title: Mudar de bairro
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks whether you would move, and you answer weighing advantages against inconveniences.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ouvi dizer que estás a pensar mudar de casa. É verdade?
lines.en: I heard you are thinking of moving house. Is that true?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estou. Por um lado adoro o bairro, por outro a renda subiu muito.
lines.en: I am. On the one hand I love the neighbourhood, on the other the rent has gone up a lot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E ir para os arredores? Sai bem mais barato.
lines.en: What about going to the outskirts? It works out much cheaper.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Apesar de ser mais barato, ficava longe do trabalho.
lines.en: Although it is cheaper, I would be far from work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas tinhas mais espaço e menos barulho.
lines.en: But you would have more space and less noise.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É verdade. O pior aqui é que não há estacionamento nenhum.
lines.en: That is true. The worst thing here is that there is no parking at all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então já sabes: menos ruído ou menos tempo no trânsito.
lines.en: So you know what it is: less noise or less time in traffic.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois. Gosto tanto do centro que ainda vou ficar mais um ano.
lines.en: Right. I like the centre so much that I am going to stay another year.

days.title: Os transportes e a mobilidade
days.objective: Talk about how you get around, how long journeys take and what happens when things go wrong, using apanhar, demorar and the future subjunctive after se.
days.paretoFocus: Getting around is daily life, and the 'se' clause about a future journey is exactly the structure that lifts travel talk from A2 to B1.
drills: Describe your commute in four sentences, giving the means of transport and the time it takes.
Write three sentences beginning with Se, using the future subjunctive of haver, ser and chegar.
List five transport words you did not know before and use each in a sentence.

items: Which verb do people in Portugal use for catching a bus?
What is the future subjunctive of haver, and when do you use it?
Give two ways to say how long a journey takes.

activities.title: Deslocar-se na cidade e no país
activities.intro: In Portugal you apanhar a bus or a train and perder it if you are late. Journey length is expressed with demorar or levar plus a time. After se referring to the future, Portuguese uses the future subjunctive, which is built from the third person plural of the preterite: houveram gives houver, foram gives for.

items.hr: Apanho o comboio das sete e vinte para Lisboa.
items.en: I catch the seven twenty train to Lisbon.
items.note: apanhar o comboio, o autocarro, o metro

items.hr: A viagem demora cerca de hora e meia, se não houver atrasos.
items.en: The journey takes about an hour and a half, if there are no delays.
items.note: futuro do conjuntivo: haver gives houver

items.hr: Prefiro ir de metro em vez de apanhar o carro.
items.en: I prefer going by underground instead of taking the car.
items.note: em vez de + infinitivo

items.hr: Perdi o autocarro por dois minutos e tive de esperar meia hora.
items.en: I missed the bus by two minutes and had to wait half an hour.
items.note: perder = to miss a service

items.hr: O passe mensal fica mais barato do que comprar bilhetes avulso.
items.en: The monthly pass works out cheaper than buying single tickets.
items.note: bilhete avulso = single ticket

activities.title: Transportes e horários
activities.intro: Practise transport verbs and the future subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Which verb is used in Portugal for catching a bus or a train?
questions.explanation: Apanhar o autocarro is the standard European Portuguese collocation. Pegar and tomar are Brazilian or belong to other meanings, and agarrar means to grab physically.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Se ___ greve amanhã, vou a pé.'
questions.explanation: Se pointing to a real future situation takes the future subjunctive, and haver gives houver. Houvesse would set up an unreal condition.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to take an amount of time: 'O percurso ___ vinte minutos a pé.'
questions.explanation: Both demorar and levar express duration of a journey: o percurso demora vinte minutos.

questions.prompt: Complete with the transport word used for the underground railway: 'Vou de ___ até ao centro.'
questions.explanation: In Portugal the underground is o metro, and the means of transport takes de with no article: de metro, de comboio, de autocarro.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'I missed the train and arrived late.'
questions.explanation: Perder is the verb for missing a service, and chegar atrasado is the fixed way to say you arrived late.

activities.title: No balcão da estação
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are buying a ticket and checking the timetable, using apanhar, demorar and se plus the future subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.en: Good afternoon. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Queria apanhar o comboio para o Porto ainda esta manhã.
lines.en: I would like to catch the train to Porto still this morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Há um às dez e cinco e outro às onze e meia.
lines.en: There is one at five past ten and another at half past eleven.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E quanto tempo demora a viagem?
lines.en: And how long does the journey take?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cerca de três horas, se não houver obras na linha.
lines.en: About three hours, if there are no works on the line.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então levo o das dez e cinco. Sai mais barato com passe?
lines.en: Then I will take the five past ten one. Is it cheaper with a pass?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Só se viajar todas as semanas. Para uma vez, o bilhete avulso chega.
lines.en: Only if you travel every week. For one trip, a single ticket is enough.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito. Se perder este, apanho o seguinte.
lines.en: Perfect. If I miss this one, I will catch the next one.

days.title: A gastronomia portuguesa: falar de cozinha e de gostos
days.objective: Describe a dish, say what it contains and what it tastes of, and express your food preferences using levar, saber a and the passive with ser.
days.paretoFocus: Food is the fastest route into real conversation in Portugal, and levar plus saber a let you talk about any dish you have never seen before.
drills: Describe two dishes from your own country using levar for the ingredients.
Write three sentences with saber a about flavours you like and dislike.
Rewrite three cooking steps in the passive with ser plus a past participle.

items: Which verb lists what a dish contains?
How do you say 'this soup tastes of garlic'?
What is the first person of trazer in the present?

activities.title: Falar de cozinha e de gostos
activities.intro: When Portuguese speakers describe a recipe they say what the dish leva, not what it has. Flavour is expressed with saber a plus a noun. Cooking methods are often given in the passive with ser plus a past participle that agrees with the subject.

items.hr: O caldo verde leva couve, batata e um pouco de chouriço.
items.en: Caldo verde has cabbage, potato and a little chouriço in it.
items.note: levar = to contain (ingredients)

items.hr: Este bacalhau sabe um bocadinho a alho.
items.en: This cod tastes slightly of garlic.
items.note: saber a = to taste of

items.hr: Não sou grande adepto de doces, prefiro qualquer coisa salgada.
items.en: I am not a big fan of sweet things, I prefer something savoury.
items.note: ser adepto de = to be a fan of

items.hr: O leitão é assado no forno durante várias horas.
items.en: The suckling pig is roasted in the oven for several hours.
items.note: passive: ser + particípio, agreeing with the subject

items.hr: Sempre que vou ao Norte, trago queijo da serra.
items.en: Whenever I go up north, I bring back mountain cheese.
items.note: trazer: eu trago

activities.title: Pratos e sabores
activities.intro: Practise describing dishes and stating preferences.

questions.prompt: 'Este molho sabe a limão' means:
options: This sauce is served with lemon.
questions.explanation: Saber a is the fixed expression for flavour. Saber alone means to know, but with the preposition a it means to taste of.

questions.prompt: Which verb do Portuguese speakers use to list what a dish contains?
questions.explanation: O bolo leva três ovos is the everyday way to say what goes into a dish. Trazer means to bring and pôr means to put.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past participle in the passive, agreeing with the subject: 'A carne é ___ no forno.'
questions.explanation: In the passive with ser, the participle agrees in gender and number with the subject, so a carne takes the feminine assada.

questions.prompt: Complete with the irregular first person of the verb trazer: 'Eu ___ sempre vinho do Douro.'
questions.explanation: Trazer is irregular in the present: eu trago, tu trazes, ele traz.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'I am not a big fan of sweet things.'
questions.explanation: Ser grande adepto de is a fixed way of softening a dislike, and the preposition de always follows adepto.

activities.title: À mesa com amigos
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend walks you through a dish and you react with questions and opinions of your own.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Prova lá. Fiz uma receita da minha avó.
lines.en: Try it. I made a recipe of my grandmother's.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está muito bom. Isto leva o quê?
lines.en: It is very good. What is in this?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Leva grão, espinafres e bacalhau desfiado.
lines.en: It has chickpeas, spinach and shredded cod in it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sabe um bocadinho a alho, não é?
lines.en: It tastes slightly of garlic, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sabe, sim. O alho é frito à parte e juntado no fim.
lines.en: It does. The garlic is fried separately and added at the end.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Depois quero a receita. Não sou grande adepto de peixe, mas isto vai bem.
lines.en: I want the recipe afterwards. I am not a big fan of fish, but this works.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E de sobremesa? Fiz arroz-doce.
lines.en: And for dessert? I made rice pudding.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Só um bocadinho, que os doces ficam sempre muito doces para mim.
lines.en: Just a little, because desserts are always too sweet for me.

days.title: A educação e a formação: percursos e diplomas
days.objective: Narrate your education and training path and your current studies, using licenciar-se, tirar um curso, estar a plus infinitive and the future subjunctive after quando.
days.paretoFocus: Your study path comes up in every introduction, and the same sentences go straight into a job interview or an official form.
drills: Write your own study path in five sentences, in chronological order.
Make three sentences with quando plus the future subjunctive about what you will do after finishing something.
Note down the Portuguese names for five qualifications and say who has each one.

items: Which word do Portuguese universities use for an individual course subject?
How do you say 'I am doing a master's degree' in European Portuguese?
What form follows quando when you talk about the future?

activities.title: Percursos, cursos e diplomas
activities.intro: Portuguese talks about qualifications with tirar um curso and licenciar-se em. Ongoing study uses estar a plus an infinitive, which is the European Portuguese progressive. After quando referring to the future, use the future subjunctive, never the present indicative.

items.hr: Licenciei-me em Engenharia e depois tirei um mestrado.
items.en: I got my degree in Engineering and then did a master's.
items.note: licenciar-se em; tirar um curso, um mestrado

items.hr: Estou a fazer uma pós-graduação em gestão à noite.
items.en: I am doing a postgraduate course in management in the evenings.
items.note: estar a + infinitivo, the European Portuguese progressive

items.hr: Fiz o secundário na minha terra e só depois vim para a cidade.
items.en: I did secondary school in my home town and only then came to the city.
items.note: o secundário = upper secondary school

items.hr: Ainda me faltam duas cadeiras para acabar o curso.
items.en: I still have two subjects left to finish the degree.
items.note: cadeira = a university subject; faltar agrees with what is missing

items.hr: Quando acabar a formação, quero candidatar-me a um estágio.
items.en: When I finish the training, I want to apply for an internship.
items.note: quando + futuro do conjuntivo: acabar

activities.title: Estudos e diplomas
activities.intro: Practise talking about study paths and future plans.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the European Portuguese progressive correctly?
options: Estou a estudar para os exames.
Sou a estudar para os exames.
Estou de estudar para os exames.
questions.answer: Estou a estudar para os exames.
questions.explanation: European Portuguese forms the progressive with estar a plus the infinitive; the gerund version is Brazilian.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form for a future action: 'Quando ___ o curso, vou trabalhar.'
questions.explanation: Quando pointing to the future requires the future subjunctive, which for regular -ar verbs looks identical to the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word Portuguese universities use for an individual subject: 'Este semestre tenho seis ___.'
questions.explanation: A university subject is uma cadeira in Portugal, also called uma unidade curricular in official documents.

questions.prompt: Complete with the reflexive verb meaning to graduate with a first degree: 'Ela ___ em Direito no ano passado.'
questions.explanation: Licenciar-se em is the verb for completing a licenciatura, and the pronoun follows the verb in an affirmative clause.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'I still have two subjects left to finish.'
questions.explanation: Faltar agrees with what is missing, duas cadeiras, and the person affected appears as the pronoun me before the verb after ainda.

activities.title: Conversa sobre percursos
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone asks about your studies and you narrate your path and your plans.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E tu, o que é que estudaste?
lines.en: And you, what did you study?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Licenciei-me em Economia e depois tirei um curso de contabilidade.
lines.en: I did a degree in Economics and then took an accountancy course.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E paraste por aí ou continuaste?
lines.en: And did you stop there or carry on?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estou a fazer uma pós-graduação à noite, duas vezes por semana.
lines.en: I am doing a postgraduate course in the evenings, twice a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Deve ser puxado com o trabalho.
lines.en: That must be tough alongside work.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É, mas só me faltam três cadeiras para acabar.
lines.en: It is, but I only have three subjects left to finish.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E depois? Já pensaste no que vem a seguir?
lines.en: And afterwards? Have you thought about what comes next?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quando terminar, quero candidatar-me a uma vaga na área financeira.
lines.en: When I finish, I want to apply for a position in the financial area.

days.title: Procurar emprego: a carta de apresentação
days.objective: Write a short Portuguese cover letter using the standard formal opening, body and closing formulas.
days.paretoFocus: Portuguese formal writing runs on a handful of fixed formulas: learn six of them and you can produce a correct letter for any application.
drills: Write a five-line cover letter for a job you would like, using one opening and one closing formula.
List the documents you would attach and introduce each with junto envio or envio em anexo.
Rewrite two casual sentences of yours in formal register, replacing tu forms with impersonal ones.

items: Give the standard opening for a Portuguese job application letter.
How do you sign off a formal letter in Portugal?
How do you say you remain available for further information?

activities.title: Escrever uma carta de apresentação
activities.intro: Portuguese cover letters use a fixed formal register with set phrases. Venho por este meio opens the letter, junto envio introduces attachments, and fico ao vosso dispor closes the body before the greeting. The company is addressed as vós, so possessives are vosso and vossa.

items.hr: Venho por este meio candidatar-me à vaga de assistente comercial.
items.en: I am writing to apply for the position of sales assistant.
items.note: venho por este meio = fixed formal opening

items.hr: Tomei conhecimento da vossa oferta e gostaria de apresentar a minha candidatura.
items.en: I became aware of your vacancy and would like to submit my application.
items.note: tomar conhecimento de = to learn of

items.hr: Ao longo dos últimos cinco anos, trabalhei na área do apoio ao cliente.
items.en: Over the last five years, I have worked in customer support.
items.note: ao longo de = over the course of

items.hr: Junto envio o meu currículo, bem como duas cartas de recomendação.
items.en: I am attaching my CV, as well as two letters of recommendation.
items.note: bem como = as well as

items.hr: Fico ao vosso dispor para qualquer esclarecimento. Com os melhores cumprimentos,
items.en: I remain available for any clarification. Kind regards,
items.note: com os melhores cumprimentos = standard formal sign-off

activities.title: Registo formal na candidatura
activities.intro: Practise the fixed formulas of a Portuguese cover letter.

questions.prompt: Which opening is appropriate for a formal Portuguese cover letter?
options: Venho por este meio candidatar-me à vaga anunciada.
Boas, vi o anúncio e gostei.
questions.answer: Venho por este meio candidatar-me à vaga anunciada.
questions.explanation: The other three use informal register and tu forms, which are never used in a written application to a company.

questions.prompt: Which formula closes a formal letter in Portugal?
questions.explanation: The other three belong to messages between friends and family and would be out of place in a professional letter.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun in the formula for offering yourself for further information: 'Fico ao vosso ___ para qualquer esclarecimento.'
questions.explanation: Ficar ao dispor de alguém is the fixed formal phrase meaning to remain at someone's disposal.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for the document that lists your experience: 'Envio em anexo o meu ___.'
questions.explanation: In Portugal the document is o currículo, from curriculum vitae, and it keeps the accent on the second syllable.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'I am attaching my cover letter.'
questions.explanation: Junto envio is the formal way of introducing an attachment, and carta de apresentação is the Portuguese name for a cover letter.

activities.title: Rever a candidatura
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague checks your draft letter and you defend your formal choices.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Deixa-me ver a carta antes de a enviares.
lines.en: Let me see the letter before you send it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Começa assim: venho por este meio candidatar-me à vaga de técnico.
lines.en: It starts like this: I am writing to apply for the technician position.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa. E dizes onde viste o anúncio?
lines.en: Good. And do you say where you saw the advert?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Digo: tomei conhecimento da vossa oferta na semana passada.
lines.en: I do: I became aware of your vacancy last week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Falta falar da tua experiência concreta.
lines.en: You still need to mention your actual experience.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Escrevi que ao longo dos últimos quatro anos trabalhei na manutenção.
lines.en: I wrote that over the last four years I worked in maintenance.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o fecho? Não acabes de forma seca.
lines.en: And the ending? Do not finish it abruptly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fico ao vosso dispor para qualquer esclarecimento, e depois os cumprimentos.
lines.en: I remain available for any clarification, and then the greetings.

days.title: A entrevista de emprego: apresentar-se profissionalmente
days.objective: Present your professional experience in an interview, using há plus a time with the present, the compound perfect tenho trabalhado and the conditional for polite availability.
days.paretoFocus: The compound perfect is the tense Portuguese uses for what you have been doing recently, and it is exactly what an interviewer asks about.
drills: Write three sentences with há plus a time about how long you have done things.
Write three sentences with tenho plus a past participle about what you have been doing lately.
Prepare a sixty second spoken self-presentation and time yourself.

items: What does tenho trabalhado express that trabalhei does not?
How do you say you have worked in a field for eight years?
Which tense makes an offer of availability sound polite?

activities.title: Apresentar-se numa entrevista
activities.intro: Portuguese states duration with há plus a time and the verb in the present: trabalho aqui há oito anos. The compound perfect, tenho plus the past participle, describes a repeated action running from the recent past up to now, which is different from the simple preterite. The conditional softens statements about availability.

items.hr: Trabalho nesta área há oito anos.
items.en: I have worked in this field for eight years.
items.note: há + time with the present tense

items.hr: Ultimamente, tenho trabalhado sobretudo com clientes estrangeiros.
items.en: Lately I have been working mainly with foreign clients.
items.note: pretérito perfeito composto = repeated action up to now

items.hr: Considero-me uma pessoa organizada e habituada a prazos apertados.
items.en: I consider myself an organised person, used to tight deadlines.
items.note: estar habituado a = to be used to

items.hr: O que me atrai nesta vaga é a possibilidade de evoluir.
items.en: What attracts me about this position is the chance to grow.
items.note: o que me atrai ... é = cleft sentence for emphasis

items.hr: Estaria disponível para começar no início do mês.
items.en: I would be available to start at the beginning of the month.
items.note: condicional for polite statements

activities.title: Falar da experiência profissional
activities.intro: Practise duration, the compound perfect and polite forms.

questions.prompt: 'Tenho trabalhado em projetos internacionais' means:
options: I have been working on international projects lately.
I worked on international projects once.
I will work on international projects.
I used to work on international projects years ago.
questions.answer: I have been working on international projects lately.
questions.explanation: The compound perfect covers a repeated or continuing action from the recent past up to the present moment, unlike the simple preterite which closes the action off.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct way to say you have done a job for six years:
options: Faço este trabalho há seis anos.
Faço este trabalho por seis anos.
Fiz este trabalho há seis anos até agora.
Estou a fazer este trabalho desde seis anos.
questions.answer: Faço este trabalho há seis anos.
questions.explanation: Duration up to the present is expressed with the present tense plus há; desde is followed by a starting point, not a length of time.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of estar to sound polite: 'Eu ___ disponível a partir de setembro.'
questions.explanation: The conditional estaria presents availability as an offer rather than a demand, which is the expected register in an interview.

questions.prompt: Complete with the reflexive verb meaning that you see yourself as something: '___ uma pessoa metódica e paciente.'
questions.explanation: Considerar-se plus a noun phrase is the standard way to describe your own qualities, with the pronoun attached after the verb.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'Lately I have been studying English.'
questions.explanation: Ultimamente signals the recent period, which is exactly when the compound perfect tenho estudado is required.

activities.title: A entrevista
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An interviewer asks about your experience and you answer with há, tenho plus participle and the conditional.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fale-me um pouco do seu percurso profissional.
lines.en: Tell me a little about your professional background.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trabalho na área da logística há sete anos.
lines.en: I have worked in logistics for seven years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o que tem feito no último ano?
lines.en: And what have you been doing over the last year?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tenho coordenado uma equipa de seis pessoas e tenho dado formação.
lines.en: I have been coordinating a team of six people and giving training.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Como se descreve a trabalhar sob pressão?
lines.en: How would you describe yourself working under pressure?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Considero-me calmo e já estou habituado a prazos apertados.
lines.en: I consider myself calm and I am already used to tight deadlines.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A partir de quando poderia começar?
lines.en: From when could you start?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estaria disponível dentro de um mês, depois do aviso prévio.
lines.en: I would be available within a month, after my notice period.

days.title: O dinheiro e o orçamento: gerir as despesas
days.objective: Talk about spending, saving and prices, using gastar em, ficar por, dar para and the imperfect subjunctive for unreal money situations.
days.paretoFocus: Money talk is constant and concrete, and se plus the imperfect subjunctive is the structure that lets you say what you would do differently.
drills: Write out your monthly budget in five sentences using gastar em.
Ask three prices out loud and answer each with ficou por plus an amount.
Write three sentences with se plus the imperfect subjunctive about money choices.

items: What does ficou por trinta euros mean?
How do you say 'there isn't enough left for holidays'?
Which form of poupar follows se in an unreal condition?

activities.title: Gerir as despesas do mês
activities.intro: Portuguese spends money em something, not on it. Ficar por gives the final price of a meal or purchase, and dar para says whether an amount is enough. For unreal situations, se takes the imperfect subjunctive, and speakers usually answer with the imperfect indicative rather than the conditional.

items.hr: Gasto quase um terço do ordenado em renda.
items.en: I spend almost a third of my salary on rent.
items.note: gastar em, not gastar sobre

items.hr: O jantar ficou por vinte euros por pessoa.
items.en: Dinner came to twenty euros per person.
items.note: ficar por = to come to (a price)

items.hr: Este mês não me dá para poupar nada.
items.en: This month I cannot manage to save anything.
items.note: dar para = to be enough to

items.hr: Ando a pôr algum dinheiro de parte todos os meses.
items.en: I have been putting some money aside every month.
items.note: pôr de parte = to set aside

items.hr: Se gastasse menos em cafés, chegava melhor ao fim do mês.
items.en: If I spent less on coffees, I would get to the end of the month more easily.
items.note: se + imperfeito do conjuntivo, answer in the imperfect indicative

activities.title: Despesas e poupança
activities.intro: Practise price expressions and unreal conditions about money.

questions.prompt: 'A reparação ficou por trinta euros' means:
options: The repair came to thirty euros.
The repair stayed for thirty days.
The repair was worth thirty euros less.
The repair was left at thirty per cent.
questions.answer: The repair came to thirty euros.
questions.explanation: Ficar por plus an amount states the final price something worked out at.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form for an unreal condition: 'Se ___ mais, comprava um carro.'
questions.explanation: An imagined, unreal condition after se takes the imperfect subjunctive; the future subjunctive poupar would make it a real future possibility.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb in the expression meaning to be enough for something: 'O que sobra não ___ para férias.'
questions.explanation: Dar para plus a noun or infinitive means to be enough for something, used impersonally in the third person.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to put money aside for the future: 'Tento ___ cem euros por mês.'
questions.explanation: Poupar is the everyday verb for saving money in Portugal; economizar exists but sounds more formal.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'I spend too much on eating out.'
questions.explanation: Gastar takes em before what the money goes on, and refeições fora de casa is the usual phrase for eating out.

activities.title: Contas ao fim do mês
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend and you compare budgets, using gastar em, dar para and unreal conditions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Este mês foi mau. Não sei para onde foi o dinheiro.
lines.en: This month was bad. I don't know where the money went.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comigo é igual. Gasto quase metade do ordenado em renda e contas.
lines.en: Same with me. I spend almost half my salary on rent and bills.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E consegues poupar alguma coisa?
lines.en: And do you manage to save anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ando a pôr cinquenta euros de parte, mas há meses em que não dá.
lines.en: I have been putting fifty euros aside, but there are months when it is not possible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O pior para mim são os jantares fora.
lines.en: The worst thing for me is eating out.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se cozinhasse mais em casa, sobrava-me bem mais ao fim do mês.
lines.en: If I cooked more at home, I would have a lot more left at the end of the month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ontem o jantar ficou por trinta euros só para mim.
lines.en: Yesterday dinner came to thirty euros just for me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nessa altura já dava para as compras de uma semana.
lines.en: For that you could already do a week's shopping.

days.title: As redes sociais: usos e limites
days.objective: Discuss your own social media habits and limits, using the verbal periphrases passar a vida a, deixar de, acabar por and dar por si a.
days.paretoFocus: These four periphrases carry a huge amount of everyday meaning that no single verb can express, and they work far beyond this topic.
drills: Write four sentences about your phone use, one with each periphrasis from the lesson.
Say three things you have decided to stop doing, using deixar de.
Describe a habit you fell into without noticing, using dar por si a.

items: What does acabar por plus an infinitive express?
How do you say 'I spend all my time scrolling'?
Which structure means to stop doing something you used to do?

activities.title: Usos e limites das redes sociais
activities.intro: Portuguese expresses shades of habit and change with verb plus preposition plus infinitive. Dar por si a marks catching yourself doing something, deixar de marks stopping, acabar por marks an unplanned outcome, and passar a vida a marks excess. The impersonal se lets you talk about people in general without naming them.

items.hr: Passo a vida a olhar para o telemóvel sem razão nenhuma.
items.en: I spend all my time looking at my phone for no reason at all.
items.note: passar a vida a + infinitivo = to spend all one's time doing

items.hr: Decidi deixar de ver notícias antes de dormir.
items.en: I decided to stop reading the news before sleeping.
items.note: deixar de + infinitivo = to stop doing

items.hr: Acabei por apagar duas aplicações do telemóvel.
items.en: I ended up deleting two apps from my phone.
items.note: acabar por + infinitivo = to end up doing

items.hr: Dou por mim a perder horas sem dar conta.
items.en: I catch myself losing hours without realising.
items.note: dar por si a + infinitivo; dar conta = to notice

items.hr: Nem tudo o que se publica nas redes é verdade.
items.en: Not everything that gets posted on social media is true.
items.note: impersonal se: publica-se, o que se publica

activities.title: O telemóvel e o tempo
activities.intro: Practise the verbal periphrases and the impersonal se.

questions.prompt: 'Acabei por concordar com ele' means:
options: I ended up agreeing with him.
I refused to agree with him.
questions.answer: I ended up agreeing with him.
questions.explanation: Acabar por plus an infinitive marks the final, often unplanned outcome of a process.

questions.prompt: Which structure means to stop doing something you used to do?
questions.explanation: Passar a means to start doing, voltar a means to do again and estar a marks an action in progress.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb that builds the expression for spending all your time on something: 'Ele ___ a vida a comentar publicações.'
questions.explanation: Passar a vida a plus an infinitive is a fixed expression criticising excess.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to delete something from a phone: 'Acabei por ___ duas aplicações que só me roubavam tempo.'
questions.explanation: Apagar is the everyday verb for deleting a file or an application, and after acabar por the verb stays in the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'I decided to stop using that app.'
questions.explanation: Deixar de keeps its preposition even when it follows another verb such as decidir.

activities.title: Demasiado tempo no ecrã
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend complains about screen time and you answer using the lesson's periphrases.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Viste o relatório do tempo de ecrã desta semana? O meu foi assustador.
lines.en: Did you see this week's screen time report? Mine was frightening.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O meu também. Passo a vida a olhar para o telemóvel sem motivo.
lines.en: Mine too. I spend all my time looking at my phone for no reason.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E fazes alguma coisa para mudar isso?
lines.en: And are you doing anything to change that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Deixei de levar o telemóvel para o quarto à noite.
lines.en: I stopped taking my phone into the bedroom at night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso resulta mesmo?
lines.en: Does that really work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Resulta, mas às vezes dou por mim a ver vídeos até tarde na mesma.
lines.en: It does, but sometimes I still catch myself watching videos until late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as aplicações? Ainda tens todas?
lines.en: And the apps? Do you still have them all?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, acabei por apagar duas. Nem tudo o que lá se lê vale o tempo.
lines.en: No, I ended up deleting two. Not everything you read there is worth the time.

days.title: Os tempos livres e a cultura
days.objective: Suggest and react to cultural plans, using vale a pena, estar a dar, apetecer and dar um salto a.
days.paretoFocus: Making and answering suggestions is what free time conversation actually consists of, and apetecer is the verb Portuguese uses instead of 'feel like'.
drills: Write three suggestions with vale a pena plus an infinitive and a reason for each.
Answer three invitations, one accepting and two refusing politely with apetecer.
Describe your last cultural outing in four sentences.

items: How does apetecer work grammatically compared with the English 'feel like'?
Which expression says a film is currently showing?
How do you say it is worth going early?

activities.title: Tempos livres e programas culturais
activities.intro: Apetecer works like gostar in reverse: the thing you fancy is the subject and you appear as an indirect pronoun, apetece-me. Dar um salto a means to pop over somewhere briefly, vale a pena plus an infinitive recommends something, and estar a dar says a film or show is on.

items.hr: Vale a pena ver a exposição, está lá até ao fim do mês.
items.en: The exhibition is worth seeing, it is there until the end of the month.
items.note: valer a pena + infinitivo

items.hr: Está a dar um filme português no cinema aqui ao pé.
items.en: There is a Portuguese film on at the cinema nearby.
items.note: estar a dar = to be showing

items.hr: Apetece-me sair, mas não me apetece nada gastar muito.
items.en: I feel like going out, but I really don't feel like spending much.
items.note: apetecer a alguém; negation moves the pronoun forward

items.hr: Ao sábado, damos sempre um salto à feira do livro.
items.en: On Saturdays we always pop over to the book fair.
items.note: dar um salto a = to pop over to

items.hr: Se calhar marcamos para as nove, assim dá tempo de jantar antes.
items.en: Maybe we set it for nine, that way there is time to have dinner first.
items.note: se calhar = maybe, followed by the indicative

activities.title: Fazer e recusar convites
activities.intro: Practise suggesting plans and reacting to them.

questions.prompt: 'Apetece-me um café' means:
questions.explanation: With apetecer the thing wanted is the subject and the person appears as an indirect pronoun, so the literal structure is 'a coffee appeals to me'.

questions.prompt: Which expression says a film is currently showing?
questions.explanation: Dar is used impersonally for what is on at the cinema or on television: o que está a dar hoje?

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun in the expression meaning that something is worth doing: 'Vale a ___ ir cedo para apanhar lugar.'
questions.explanation: Valer a pena is a fixed expression and the noun never changes, whatever the tense of valer.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb in the expression meaning to pop over somewhere: 'Vamos ___ um salto ao museu depois do almoço.'
questions.explanation: Dar um salto a plus a place means to go there briefly, and the preposition a contracts with the article: ao museu.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'It is worth booking the tickets in advance.'
questions.explanation: Valer a pena is followed directly by the infinitive, and com antecedência is the fixed phrase for doing something in advance.

activities.title: Planos para o fim de semana
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend proposes plans and you accept, refuse and counter-suggest using the lesson's expressions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estás livre no sábado? Podíamos fazer alguma coisa.
lines.en: Are you free on Saturday? We could do something.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estou. Apetece-me sair, mas não me apetece nada uma noite longa.
lines.en: I am. I feel like going out, but I really don't feel like a long night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então vamos ao cinema. Está a dar um filme novo.
lines.en: Then let's go to the cinema. There is a new film on.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa ideia. Vale a pena reservar os bilhetes com antecedência.
lines.en: Good idea. It is worth booking the tickets in advance.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E antes? Jantamos ou comemos qualquer coisa rápida?
lines.en: And before? Shall we have dinner or grab something quick?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se calhar damos um salto àquela tasca ao pé do teatro.
lines.en: Maybe we pop over to that little place next to the theatre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Combinado. A que horas te dá jeito?
lines.en: Agreed. What time suits you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Às sete e meia, assim dá tempo de jantar antes da sessão.
lines.en: At half past seven, that way there is time to eat before the showing.

days.title: O voluntariado e a solidariedade
days.objective: Talk about volunteering and helping others, using the present subjunctive after impersonal expressions and after para que.
days.paretoFocus: É importante que and para que force the subjunctive, and these two frames alone let you express purpose and social values in any discussion.
drills: Write three sentences beginning with É importante que about your community.
Write three purpose clauses with para que plus the present subjunctive.
Conjugate dar, ser and estar in the present subjunctive out loud, then use each in a sentence.

items: Which mood follows para que and why?
Give the present subjunctive of dar for eu, tu and eles.
How do you say you do volunteer work at a local association?

activities.title: Voluntariado e ajuda aos outros
activities.intro: Impersonal expressions of value such as é importante, é preciso and basta are followed by que plus the present subjunctive. The same mood follows para que, which introduces a purpose when the two clauses have different subjects. If the subject is the same, use para plus the infinitive instead.

items.hr: É importante que as pessoas deem um pouco do seu tempo.
items.en: It is important that people give a little of their time.
items.note: dar in the present subjunctive: que eles deem

items.hr: Faço voluntariado numa associação de bairro aos sábados de manhã.
items.en: I do volunteer work at a neighbourhood association on Saturday mornings.
items.note: fazer voluntariado; associação de bairro

items.hr: Ajudamos para que ninguém fique sozinho no Natal.
items.en: We help so that nobody is left alone at Christmas.
items.note: para que + presente do conjuntivo: fique

items.hr: Basta que cada um dê uma ajuda de vez em quando.
items.en: It is enough for each person to lend a hand every now and then.
items.note: dar uma ajuda = to lend a hand

items.hr: Recolhemos roupa e bens alimentares para famílias carenciadas.
items.en: We collect clothes and food for families in need.
items.note: carenciado = in need, deprived

activities.title: Ajudar e participar
activities.intro: Practise the present subjunctive in value and purpose clauses.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'É preciso que tu ___ mais atenção aos outros.'
questions.explanation: É preciso que requires the present subjunctive, and the tu form of dar in that mood is dês, written with a circumflex.

questions.prompt: Which conjunction expresses purpose and requires the subjunctive?
questions.explanation: The other three introduce a cause and are followed by the indicative, since a cause is presented as a fact.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to gather donated items: 'A associação ___ roupa usada todos os meses.'
questions.explanation: Recolher is the verb used for collecting donations, distinct from apanhar, which is about picking things up physically.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of ficar: 'Trabalhamos para que ninguém ___ sem apoio este inverno.'
questions.explanation: After para que the verb goes into the present subjunctive, and ficar changes its c to qu before e: fique.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'I do volunteer work twice a month.'
questions.explanation: Fazer voluntariado takes no article, and frequency is given with duas vezes por mês.

activities.title: Inscrever-se como voluntário
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You offer to volunteer at a local association, using impersonal expressions and purpose clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Soube que quer dar uma ajuda por cá.
lines.en: Good afternoon. I hear you want to lend a hand here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quero, sim. Acho importante que cada um dê algum tempo.
lines.en: Yes, I do. I think it is important that everyone gives some time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que dias é que lhe dão jeito?
lines.en: Which days suit you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aos sábados de manhã, para que não choque com o trabalho.
lines.en: Saturday mornings, so that it does not clash with work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Temos a recolha de bens alimentares e o apoio a idosos.
lines.en: We have the food collection and support for elderly people.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prefiro o apoio a idosos, basta que me expliquem o que fazer.
lines.en: I prefer support for elderly people, as long as someone explains what to do.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Damos formação no primeiro dia. Traga um documento de identificação.
lines.en: We give training on the first day. Bring an identification document.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. Espero que a equipa precise de mais mãos.
lines.en: Agreed. I hope the team needs more hands.

days.title: A família moderna e as relações
days.objective: Describe family relationships and how people get on, using dar-se bem com, reciprocal verbs and voltar a plus infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: Reciprocal verbs and dar-se bem com are how Portuguese actually describes relationships, and the vemo-nos spelling rule shows up constantly in speech.
drills: Write five sentences with dar-se bem or dar-se mal about people you know.
Write three reciprocal sentences in the nós form and check the dropped s.
Describe two changes in your family over the last ten years using cada vez mais.

items: Why is it vemo-nos and not vemos-nos?
How do you say 'they started talking to each other again'?
Give two ways to say you get on badly with someone.

activities.title: A família e as relações
activities.intro: Dar-se bem com is the standard way to say you get on with someone. Reciprocal actions use the nós form with the pronoun nos, and the verb drops its final s before that pronoun: vemos plus nos becomes vemo-nos. Voltar a plus an infinitive says an action started again.

items.hr: Dou-me muito bem com a minha cunhada.
items.en: I get on very well with my sister-in-law.
items.note: dar-se bem / mal com alguém

items.hr: Os meus pais divorciaram-se, mas continuam a dar-se bem.
items.en: My parents got divorced, but they still get on well.
items.note: continuar a + infinitivo = to keep on doing

items.hr: Hoje em dia, cada vez mais casais vivem juntos sem casar.
items.en: Nowadays more and more couples live together without marrying.
items.note: cada vez mais = increasingly

items.hr: Vemo-nos uma vez por mês, nem que seja para um café.
items.en: We see each other once a month, even if it is only for a coffee.
items.note: vemos + nos gives vemo-nos; nem que + conjuntivo

items.hr: A minha irmã voltou a viver com os meus pais por causa das rendas.
items.en: My sister went back to living with my parents because of the rents.
items.note: voltar a + infinitivo = to do again

activities.title: Relações familiares
activities.intro: Practise reciprocal verbs and relationship expressions.

questions.prompt: Which form is correct in European Portuguese?
questions.explanation: The nós ending loses its final s before the pronoun nos, giving encontramo-nos; fronting the pronoun with no trigger word is not European usage.

questions.prompt: 'Dou-me bem com ele' means:
options: I get on well with him.
questions.answer: I get on well with him.
questions.explanation: Dar-se bem com is a fixed reflexive expression about relationships and has nothing to do with giving.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb that builds the structure meaning to do something again: 'Eles ___ a falar-se depois de dois anos.'
questions.explanation: Voltar a plus the infinitive expresses repetition, and in the preterite the eles form is voltaram.

questions.prompt: Complete with the reflexive verb meaning to end a marriage legally: 'Os meus tios ___ o ano passado.'
questions.explanation: Divorciar-se is reflexive, and in an affirmative clause the pronoun attaches to the end of the verb with a hyphen.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'We see each other once a month.'
questions.explanation: The reciprocal nós form drops the final s of the verb before nos, giving vemo-nos.

activities.title: Falar da família
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your family and you answer with reciprocal verbs and dar-se bem com.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A tua família é grande?
lines.en: Is your family big?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nem por isso. Somos quatro, mas damo-nos todos muito bem.
lines.en: Not really. There are four of us, but we all get on very well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E vês os teus pais muitas vezes?
lines.en: And do you see your parents often?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vemo-nos uma vez por mês, nem que seja para um almoço rápido.
lines.en: We see each other once a month, even if it is only for a quick lunch.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A minha irmã voltou a viver com os meus pais.
lines.en: My sister went back to living with my parents.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hoje em dia acontece cada vez mais, com as rendas como estão.
lines.en: That happens more and more nowadays, with rents as they are.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E dão-se bem, os três?
lines.en: And do the three of them get on?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ao início discutiam, mas agora entendem-se bem melhor.
lines.en: At first they used to argue, but now they understand each other much better.

days.title: As formalidades e os documentos
days.objective: Handle documents and their requirements, using ter de, bastar, caducar and the vocabulary of official paperwork in Portugal.
days.paretoFocus: Renewals, proofs and deadlines follow the same three verbs, so one set of sentences covers every document you will ever need.
drills: List four documents you own and write when each one expires.
Write three sentences with basta plus an infinitive about what is enough to bring.
Write the requirements for one renewal as a numbered list in Portuguese.

items: What does caducar mean and what nouns go with it?
Which verb do you use for picking up a document that is ready?
How do you say 'five working days'?

activities.title: Tratar de documentos
activities.intro: Obligation about paperwork is expressed with ter de plus an infinitive, and bastar says what is sufficient. Documents caducam rather than expiram in everyday Portuguese, and you levantar a document once it is ready. Deadlines are counted in dias úteis, working days.

items.hr: Tem de renovar o cartão de cidadão antes de este caducar.
items.en: You have to renew your citizen card before it expires.
items.note: caducar = to expire

items.hr: Basta levar um comprovativo de morada e uma fotografia.
items.en: It is enough to bring proof of address and a photograph.
items.note: bastar = to be enough; comprovativo = proof document

items.hr: É preciso marcar atendimento com bastante antecedência.
items.en: You need to book an appointment well in advance.
items.note: marcar atendimento = to book a service appointment

items.hr: O certificado demora cerca de dez dias úteis a ser emitido.
items.en: The certificate takes about ten working days to be issued.
items.note: dias úteis = working days; emitir = to issue

items.hr: Já levantei a carta de condução nova ao balcão.
items.en: I have already picked up my new driving licence at the counter.
items.note: levantar = to collect something that is ready

activities.title: Documentos e prazos
activities.intro: Practise the verbs and nouns of official paperwork.

questions.prompt: 'O passaporte caduca em março' means:
options: The passport is issued in March.
The passport is renewed in March.
The passport is cancelled in March.
questions.explanation: Caducar is the standard verb for a document reaching the end of its validity in Portugal.

questions.prompt: Which verb means to collect a document that is ready for you?
questions.explanation: Levantar um documento means to pick it up at a counter; tirar means to obtain a document for the first time and apanhar is for catching things or transport.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning that something is sufficient: '___ apresentar um documento de identificação.'
questions.explanation: Bastar is used impersonally in the third person singular before an infinitive to say what is enough.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adjective used for days on which offices are open: 'A resposta chega em cinco dias ___.'
questions.explanation: Dias úteis are working days; the plural of útil is úteis, keeping the accent.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'You have to renew your identity card.'
questions.explanation: Obligation uses ter de plus an infinitive, and the Portuguese identity document is o cartão de cidadão.

activities.title: Ao balcão do atendimento
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You renew a document and ask about requirements and deadlines.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia. Diga, faz favor.
lines.en: Good morning. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. O meu cartão caduca no mês que vem e queria renová-lo.
lines.en: Good morning. My card expires next month and I would like to renew it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trouxe algum comprovativo de morada?
lines.en: Did you bring any proof of address?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trouxe uma fatura da água. Basta isso ou é preciso mais?
lines.en: I brought a water bill. Is that enough or is more needed?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Chega. A fotografia é tirada aqui mesmo.
lines.en: That is fine. The photograph is taken right here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E quanto tempo demora até estar pronto?
lines.en: And how long does it take until it is ready?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cerca de dez dias úteis. Depois vem levantá-lo ao balcão.
lines.en: About ten working days. Then you come and pick it up at the counter.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muito obrigado. Então tenho de voltar cá no fim do mês.
lines.en: Thank you very much. So I have to come back here at the end of the month.

days.title: A publicidade e o consumo
days.objective: Discuss advertising and consumer habits, using fazer com que plus the subjunctive, levar alguém a plus infinitive and deixar-se levar por.
days.paretoFocus: Fazer com que and levar a are the two structures Portuguese uses to talk about causes and influence, which is the heart of any opinion discussion.
drills: Write three sentences with fazer com que about the effects of advertising.
Write two sentences about your own purchases: what led you to buy them? Use levar alguém a.
Describe a purchase you regret in four sentences.

items: Which mood follows fazer com que and why?
How do you say an advert made you buy on impulse?
What does deixar-se levar por mean?

activities.title: Publicidade e consumo
activities.intro: Fazer com que expresses causing something and always takes the present subjunctive. Levar alguém a followed by an infinitive says that something pushes a person into an action. Deixar-se levar por means to be swayed by something, and the pronoun moves in front of the verb after não.

items.hr: A publicidade faz com que a gente compre coisas de que não precisa.
items.en: Advertising makes people buy things they do not need.
items.note: fazer com que + presente do conjuntivo: compre

items.hr: Os descontos levam-nos a gastar mais do que queríamos.
items.en: Discounts lead us to spend more than we wanted to.
items.note: levar alguém a + infinitivo

items.hr: Não me deixo levar por promoções agressivas.
items.en: I do not let myself be swayed by aggressive offers.
items.note: deixar-se levar por; não moves the pronoun forward

items.hr: Antes de comprar, comparo sempre preços e leio comentários.
items.en: Before buying, I always compare prices and read reviews.
items.note: antes de + infinitivo

items.hr: Aquilo é publicidade enganosa: prometem coisas que não cumprem.
items.en: That is misleading advertising: they promise things they do not deliver.
items.note: enganoso = misleading; cumprir = to deliver on

activities.title: Anúncios e compras
activities.intro: Practise causative structures and consumer vocabulary.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'A campanha faz com que muita gente ___ produtos inúteis.'
questions.explanation: Fazer com que always introduces a subordinate clause in the present subjunctive, so comprar becomes compre.

questions.prompt: 'Deixei-me levar pela promoção' means:
options: I let myself be swayed by the offer.
questions.answer: I let myself be swayed by the offer.
questions.explanation: Deixar-se levar por is a fixed reflexive expression meaning to be carried along by something, here an offer.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adjective describing advertising that misleads the public: 'A associação de consumidores queixou-se de publicidade ___.'
questions.explanation: Enganoso comes from enganar, to deceive, and agrees with the feminine noun publicidade.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb that means to push someone into doing something: 'Os anúncios ___-nos a comprar por impulso.'
questions.explanation: Levar alguém a plus an infinitive expresses influence, and here the eles form levam agrees with os anúncios.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'I always compare prices before buying.'
questions.explanation: Sempre normally sits right after the verb, and antes de is followed by the infinitive when the subject stays the same.

activities.title: Uma compra por impulso
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend shows off a purchase and you discuss the influence of advertising.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Olha o que comprei ontem. Estava com cinquenta por cento de desconto.
lines.en: Look what I bought yesterday. It was fifty per cent off.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Precisavas mesmo disso ou deixaste-te levar pela promoção?
lines.en: Did you really need it or did you let yourself be swayed by the offer?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Confesso que foi o anúncio que me convenceu.
lines.en: I admit it was the advert that convinced me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É isso: a publicidade faz com que compremos o que não nos faz falta.
lines.en: That is it: advertising makes us buy what we do not need.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E tu nunca cais nessa?
lines.en: And you never fall for that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Caio, mas antes de comprar comparo sempre preços noutro sítio.
lines.en: I do, but before buying I always compare prices somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se calhar tinha encontrado mais barato.
lines.en: Maybe I would have found it cheaper.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Provavelmente. Os descontos levam-nos a decidir depressa de mais.
lines.en: Probably. Discounts lead us to decide far too quickly.

days.title: As notícias: compreender um artigo curto
days.objective: Understand and summarise a short news item, recognising the passive with ser, source phrases such as segundo, and the future perfect used for unconfirmed information.
days.paretoFocus: Portuguese news writing recycles the same few structures, so learning them unlocks almost any short article or bulletin.
drills: Rewrite three active sentences of your own in the passive with ser.
Write two sentences with terá plus a participle about something not yet confirmed.
Summarise a short news story you know in five Portuguese sentences, naming the source.

items: What does terá começado mean in a news report?
Which preposition introduces the agent in a passive sentence?
Give two phrases that introduce the source of information.

activities.title: Ler uma notícia
activities.intro: News Portuguese leans on the passive with ser plus a past participle, with the agent introduced by por and its contractions. Sources are marked with segundo or de acordo com. When information is not confirmed, journalists use the future perfect, terá acontecido, which means it apparently happened.

items.hr: Segundo o jornal, a greve foi adiada para a próxima semana.
items.en: According to the newspaper, the strike was postponed to next week.
items.note: segundo = according to, with no preposition after it

items.hr: De acordo com a autarquia, as obras estarão concluídas em junho.
items.en: According to the local council, the works will be finished in June.
items.note: autarquia = local council

items.hr: O acordo foi assinado esta manhã pelos dois governos.
items.en: The agreement was signed this morning by the two governments.
items.note: passiva: ser + particípio + por, contracted to pelos

items.hr: O incêndio terá começado por volta das três da madrugada.
items.en: The fire apparently started at around three in the morning.
items.note: futuro composto for unconfirmed facts: terá começado

items.hr: A notícia refere ainda que os prejuízos ascendem a milhares de euros.
items.en: The report also states that the damage amounts to thousands of euros.
items.note: prejuízos = damage, losses; ascender a = to amount to

activities.title: A linguagem das notícias
activities.intro: Practise the passive, source phrases and unconfirmed information.

questions.prompt: 'O suspeito terá fugido a pé' in a news report means:
options: The suspect apparently fled on foot.
The suspect will flee on foot.
The suspect had to flee on foot.
The suspect wanted to flee on foot.
questions.answer: The suspect apparently fled on foot.
questions.explanation: In journalism the future perfect signals information that is likely but not confirmed, not a future action.

questions.prompt: Which phrase introduces the source of a piece of news?
questions.explanation: De acordo com and segundo attribute information to a source; the others express contrast, concession and cause.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past participle of anunciar in the passive: 'A decisão foi ___ ontem à noite.'
questions.explanation: In the passive with ser the participle agrees with the subject, and a decisão is feminine singular.

questions.prompt: Complete with the contracted preposition that introduces the agent: 'O relatório foi divulgado ___ ministério.'
questions.explanation: The agent of a passive sentence is introduced by por, which contracts with o to give pelo.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'According to the newspaper, the meeting was postponed.'
questions.explanation: Segundo takes no preposition before the source, and the passive foi adiada agrees with a reunião.

activities.title: Comentar uma notícia
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks what a short article said and you report it using passives and source phrases.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Leste a notícia sobre a ponte? O que é que dizia?
lines.en: Did you read the news about the bridge? What did it say?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Segundo o jornal, as obras foram adiadas outra vez.
lines.en: According to the newspaper, the works have been postponed again.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Outra vez? E dão alguma razão?
lines.en: Again? And do they give any reason?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O atraso terá sido causado por um problema no financiamento.
lines.en: The delay was apparently caused by a funding problem.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quem é que anunciou isso?
lines.en: And who announced that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: De acordo com o artigo, foi anunciado pela autarquia na sexta-feira.
lines.en: According to the article, it was announced by the local council on Friday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dizem quando é que aquilo fica pronto?
lines.en: Do they say when it will be ready?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A notícia refere ainda que estará concluída no próximo verão.
lines.en: The report also states that it will be finished next summer.

days.title: A lusofonia: o português no mundo
days.objective: Talk about where Portuguese is spoken and compare its varieties using connectors of addition and contrast.
days.paretoFocus: Portuguese is spread over four continents, so learners meet several accents daily and need language to compare them without sounding confused.
drills: List the nine countries where Portuguese is official, saying each name out loud in Portuguese.
Write three sentences using 'tanto ... como', 'além de' and 'ao passo que' about the language.
Say aloud two words you know that differ between European and other varieties, and use each in a sentence.

items: How do you say 'besides Portugal' in Portuguese?
Which connector means 'whereas' and introduces a contrast?
Which mood follows 'embora'?

activities.title: O português pelo mundo
activities.intro: Portuguese is an official language on four continents, and each region has its own accent and some of its own words. To describe this you need connectors that add ('além de', 'tanto ... como') and connectors that contrast ('ao passo que', 'embora'). Note that 'embora' is always followed by the subjunctive.

items.hr: O português é língua oficial em nove países, tanto na Europa como em África.
items.en: Portuguese is an official language in nine countries, both in Europe and in Africa.
items.note: tanto ... como = both ... and

items.hr: Além de Portugal e do Brasil, fala-se português em Angola, Moçambique e Timor-Leste.
items.en: Besides Portugal and Brazil, Portuguese is spoken in Angola, Mozambique and East Timor.
items.note: além de + noun = besides, in addition to

items.hr: Em Portugal diz-se 'comboio', ao passo que noutras variedades se usa outra palavra.
items.en: In Portugal people say 'comboio', whereas in other varieties another word is used.
items.note: ao passo que = whereas

items.hr: Embora a ortografia seja quase igual, a pronúncia varia bastante de país para país.
items.en: Although the spelling is almost the same, the pronunciation varies a lot from country to country.
items.note: embora + conjuntivo: ser becomes seja

items.hr: Cada variedade tem o seu sotaque próprio, o que não impede que nos entendamos.
items.en: Each variety has its own accent, which does not stop us from understanding each other.
items.note: impedir que + conjuntivo

activities.title: Comparar variedades
activities.intro: Practise the connectors used to add and to contrast information about the language.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'O português é falado ___ na Europa como em África.'
questions.explanation: The pair is 'tanto ... como', the Portuguese equivalent of 'both ... and'.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a contrast between two varieties of the language?
questions.explanation: 'Ao passo que' means 'whereas'. 'Por isso' gives a consequence, 'visto que' a cause and 'assim que' a time relation.

questions.prompt: Complete so that the sentence means 'Besides Portugal, Portuguese is spoken in Angola': '___ Portugal, fala-se português em Angola.'
questions.explanation: 'Além de' is followed directly by a noun and adds a further element to the list.

questions.prompt: Give the correct form of 'ser' after 'embora': 'Embora a ortografia ___ quase igual, a pronúncia muda.'
questions.explanation: 'Embora' always takes the present subjunctive, so 'ser' becomes 'seja'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Portuguese is an official language in nine countries.'
questions.explanation: In this fixed expression 'língua oficial' takes no article: 'é língua oficial em'.

activities.title: Quantos portugueses existem?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks you about the varieties of Portuguese, and you answer, linking your points and marking the differences.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ouvi dizer que o português se fala em muitos países. É mesmo assim?
lines.en: I heard that Portuguese is spoken in many countries. Is that really so?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É, sim. Além de Portugal e do Brasil, é língua oficial em Angola, em Moçambique e em Timor-Leste.
lines.en: Yes, it is. Besides Portugal and Brazil, it is an official language in Angola, in Mozambique and in East Timor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as pessoas percebem-se umas às outras?
lines.en: And do people understand each other?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Percebem. Embora o sotaque seja muito diferente, a gramática é praticamente a mesma.
lines.en: They do. Although the accent is very different, the grammar is practically the same.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o vocabulário? Dá-me um exemplo.
lines.en: And the vocabulary? Give me an example.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aqui apanhamos o comboio, ao passo que noutros sítios usam outra palavra para o mesmo transporte.
lines.en: Here we take the train, whereas in other places they use another word for the same transport.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então tenho de habituar o ouvido a tudo isso.
lines.en: So I have to get my ear used to all of that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tanto na rádio como nas séries ouves vários sotaques, e ao fim de umas semanas já distingues.
lines.en: Both on the radio and in series you hear several accents, and after a few weeks you can already tell them apart.

days.title: Viver no estrangeiro: instalar-se e adaptar-se
days.objective: Describe settling into a new country using reflexive verbs of adaptation and the prepositions they require.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every conversation with a new neighbour or colleague abroad starts with how long you have been there and how you are adapting.
drills: Say three things you are still getting used to, each with 'habituar-se a'.
Describe two people you get on well with using 'dar-se bem com'.
Name three things from home that you miss, using 'fazer falta'.

items: Which preposition follows 'habituar-se'?
How do you say 'I find it hard to understand the accent'?
How do you say 'I miss the sea' in Portuguese?

activities.title: Instalar-se num país novo
activities.intro: Portuguese describes adaptation with reflexive verbs that each take a fixed preposition: 'habituar-se a', 'dar-se bem com', 'adaptar-se a'. Two other patterns are very frequent here: 'custar a + infinitive' for something you find hard, and 'fazer falta a alguém' for missing something.

items.hr: Mudei-me para Lisboa há seis meses e ainda me estou a habituar ao ritmo.
items.en: I moved to Lisbon six months ago and I am still getting used to the pace.
items.note: habituar-se a; estar a + infinitivo is the European Portuguese progressive

items.hr: No início, custou-me a perceber o sotaque das pessoas na rua.
items.en: At the start, I found it hard to understand people's accent in the street.
items.note: custar a + infinitivo = to find something hard

items.hr: Já me dou bem com os vizinhos, sobretudo com a senhora do quarto andar.
items.en: I already get on well with the neighbours, especially with the lady on the fourth floor.
items.note: dar-se bem com alguém

items.hr: Aos poucos, fui-me adaptando à maneira de viver daqui.
items.en: Little by little, I gradually adapted to the way of living here.
items.note: ir + gerúndio expresses a gradual process

items.hr: O que mais me faz falta é a comida da minha mãe.
items.en: What I miss most is my mother's cooking.
items.note: fazer falta a alguém: the thing missed is the subject

activities.title: Adaptar-se à vida nova
activities.intro: Practise the reflexive verbs and prepositions used to talk about settling in.
