questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to get used to' and is followed by 'a'?
questions.explanation: 'Habituar-se a' means to get used to. 'Despedir-se de' is to say goodbye, 'queixar-se de' to complain and 'aproximar-se de' to come closer.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Dou-me muito ___ com os meus colegas de trabalho.'
questions.explanation: 'Dar-se bem com' uses the adverb 'bem', which never agrees with the subject.

questions.prompt: Supply the missing preposition plus article: 'Ainda me estou a habituar ___ horário das lojas.'
questions.explanation: 'Habituar-se a' plus the masculine article 'o' contracts to 'ao'.

questions.prompt: Supply the verb so the sentence means 'What I miss most is the sea': 'O que mais me ___ falta é o mar.'
questions.explanation: In 'fazer falta' the verb agrees with the thing missed, here 'o mar', so it is third person singular.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I moved to Lisbon six months ago.'
questions.explanation: 'Mudar-se para' takes 'para' for the destination, and elapsed time uses 'há'.

activities.title: Já te sentes em casa?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour asks how you are settling in and you answer with reflexive verbs of adaptation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, já te sentes em casa cá no bairro?
lines.en: So, do you feel at home here in the neighbourhood yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aos poucos. Ainda me estou a habituar aos horários, mas já não me perco.
lines.en: Little by little. I am still getting used to the timetables, but I no longer get lost.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o que foi mais difícil no início?
lines.en: And what was hardest at the beginning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Custou-me a perceber as pessoas ao telefone. Ao vivo é muito mais fácil.
lines.en: I found it hard to understand people on the phone. In person it is much easier.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E com os vizinhos, tens-te dado bem?
lines.en: And with the neighbours, have you been getting on well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dou-me bem com toda a gente, sobretudo com o senhor do rés do chão.
lines.en: I get on well with everybody, especially with the gentleman on the ground floor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nunca tens saudades de nada?
lines.en: Don't you ever miss anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tenho. O que mais me faz falta são os jantares longos com a família.
lines.en: I do. What I miss most are the long dinners with my family.

days.title: O turismo responsável
days.objective: Give impersonal recommendations about travelling responsibly using 'deve-se', 'convém que' and 'valer a pena'.
days.paretoFocus: Impersonal recommendation structures let you give advice about any topic without pointing at the person you are speaking to.
drills: Write three rules for visitors to your town, each starting with 'Deve-se'.
Turn two of those rules into 'Convém que os visitantes ...' with the subjunctive.
Say three things that are worth doing off season, using 'vale a pena'.

items: How do you say 'one should respect the silence'?
Which mood follows 'convém que'?
What is the European Portuguese word for 'train'?

activities.title: Viajar com respeito
activities.intro: To recommend something without naming who must do it, Portuguese uses impersonal structures. 'Deve-se + infinitive' and 'evita-se' use the impersonal 'se'; 'convém que' and 'é preferível que' are followed by the subjunctive. 'Valer a pena + infinitive' says something is worth doing.

items.hr: Deve-se respeitar o silêncio nas ruas dos bairros antigos.
items.en: One should respect the silence in the streets of the old neighbourhoods.
items.note: deve-se + infinitivo = impersonal 'one should'

items.hr: Convém que os visitantes comprem produtos locais em vez de recordações importadas.
items.en: It is advisable for visitors to buy local products instead of imported souvenirs.
items.note: convém que + conjuntivo: comprar becomes comprem

items.hr: É preferível andar de comboio do que ir de avião em distâncias curtas.
items.en: It is preferable to travel by train rather than to fly over short distances.
items.note: comboio is the European Portuguese word for train

items.hr: Evita-se assim o excesso de pessoas nos mesmos sítios e à mesma hora.
items.en: That way you avoid the excess of people in the same places at the same time.
items.note: evita-se: impersonal se with a plural or singular object

items.hr: Vale a pena visitar aldeias pequenas fora da época alta.
items.en: It is worth visiting small villages outside the high season.
items.note: valer a pena + infinitivo

activities.title: Conselhos para quem visita
activities.intro: Deve-se or convém que? Give travel advice without naming anyone in particular.

questions.prompt: Which form gives an impersonal recommendation rather than a personal one?
questions.explanation: The clitic 'se' removes the subject, so the advice applies to anyone. The other options all name a specific speaker.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Convém que os turistas ___ os horários dos locais de culto.'
questions.explanation: 'Convém que' triggers the present subjunctive, so 'respeitar' becomes 'respeitem'.

questions.prompt: Supply the verb so the sentence means 'It is worth visiting the villages in autumn': '___ a pena visitar as aldeias no outono.'
questions.explanation: The idiom is 'valer a pena', used impersonally in the third person singular.

questions.prompt: Supply the European Portuguese noun for the transport that runs on rails: 'Em vez de ir de avião, fui de ___.'
questions.explanation: European Portuguese uses 'comboio' for train; other varieties use a different word.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It is preferable to travel outside the high season.'
questions.explanation: With no named subject, 'é preferível' is followed straight by the infinitive.

activities.title: Planear uma viagem com cabeça
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is planning a trip and you suggest a more responsible way of doing it, using impersonal recommendations.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estou a pensar ir três dias ao sul em agosto. O que achas?
lines.en: I am thinking of going south for three days in August. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vale mais a pena ir em setembro. Fora da época alta, está tudo mais calmo e mais barato.
lines.en: It is more worth going in September. Outside the high season, everything is calmer and cheaper.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Faz sentido. E vou de avião ou de carro?
lines.en: That makes sense. And should I fly or drive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Em distâncias curtas, é preferível ir de comboio. Gasta-se menos e chega-se ao centro da cidade.
lines.en: Over short distances, it is preferable to go by train. You spend less and you arrive in the city centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E onde é que devo ficar?
lines.en: And where should I stay?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Convém que escolhas um sítio fora do centro histórico, para não ocupar casas de quem lá vive.
lines.en: It is advisable that you choose a place outside the historic centre, so as not to take up homes from the people who live there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E à noite, há alguma regra?
lines.en: And at night, is there any rule?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Deve-se respeitar o silêncio depois das dez. As casas são antigas e ouve-se tudo.
lines.en: One should respect the silence after ten. The houses are old and you hear everything.

days.title: O clima e os gestos do dia a dia
days.objective: Link everyday habits to their causes and consequences using 'devido a', 'graças a', 'por isso', 'como' and 'de modo que'.
days.paretoFocus: Cause and consequence connectors are the backbone of any explanation, from why you changed a habit to why a plan failed.
drills: Write three sentences about your habits, each with a different cause connector.
Turn one cause sentence around so the consequence comes second with 'por isso'.
Say aloud one change you made and what it saved, using 'graças a'.

items: Which expression introduces a positive cause?
Which connector opens a sentence and means 'since'?
How do you contract 'devido a' with a feminine plural noun?

activities.title: Pequenos gestos, grande efeito
activities.intro: Portuguese separates connectors that introduce a cause from those that introduce a consequence. 'Devido a', 'graças a' and 'por causa de' take a noun; 'como' opens the sentence with the cause. 'Por isso' and 'de modo que' introduce the result.

items.hr: Devido às ondas de calor, muita gente mudou os horários das compras.
items.en: Because of the heatwaves, many people changed their shopping times.
items.note: devido a + noun; a + as contracts to às

items.hr: Como o verão está cada vez mais seco, poupamos água em casa.
items.en: Since the summer is getting drier and drier, we save water at home.
items.note: como at the start of a sentence means 'since', never 'how'

items.hr: Uso menos o carro, por isso gasto muito menos combustível.
items.en: I use the car less, so I spend much less on fuel.
items.note: por isso introduces the consequence

items.hr: Graças à reciclagem do prédio, produzimos metade do lixo do ano passado.
items.en: Thanks to the building's recycling, we produce half the rubbish of last year.
items.note: graças a is only used for a cause with a good result

items.hr: Desliguei os aparelhos à noite, de modo que a conta desceu quase um terço.
items.en: I switched off the appliances at night, so that the bill went down almost a third.
items.note: de modo que + indicativo when the result really happened

activities.title: Causas e consequências
activities.intro: Practise choosing between cause connectors and consequence connectors.

questions.prompt: Which expression introduces a cause with a good result?
questions.explanation: 'Graças a' means 'thanks to' and is only used when the outcome is positive. The others express concession or contrast.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ o verão está mais seco, poupamos água em casa.'
questions.explanation: At the start of a sentence 'como' introduces the cause. 'Por isso' and 'portanto' introduce results and cannot open this clause.

questions.prompt: Supply the two words that introduce the result: 'Uso menos o carro, ___ ___ gasto menos combustível.'
questions.explanation: 'Por isso' links a fact to the consequence that follows from it.

questions.prompt: Supply the contraction of 'a' with the feminine plural article: 'Devido ___ ondas de calor, mudámos os horários.'
questions.explanation: 'Devido a' plus 'as' contracts to 'às', with the grave accent marking the contraction.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I separate the rubbish every day.'
questions.explanation: 'Todos os dias' is the fixed adverbial for 'every day' and goes after the object.

activities.title: A conta da luz desceu
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend notices your bills went down and you explain the causes and the consequences of your new habits.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Disseste que a tua conta da luz desceu. Como é que conseguiste?
lines.en: You said your electricity bill went down. How did you manage that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comecei a desligar tudo à noite, de modo que a conta desceu quase um terço.
lines.en: I started switching everything off at night, so the bill went down almost a third.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Só isso? Parece pouco para tanta diferença.
lines.en: Just that? It seems little for such a difference.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Também uso menos o carro, por isso gasto bastante menos ao fim do mês.
lines.en: I also use the car less, so I spend considerably less at the end of the month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a água? Aqui em casa é o que mais pesa.
lines.en: And water? Here at home that is what weighs most.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Como o verão está cada vez mais seco, passei a apanhar a água fria do duche num balde.
lines.en: Since the summer is getting drier and drier, I started collecting the cold shower water in a bucket.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nunca me tinha lembrado disso. E funciona mesmo?
lines.en: I had never thought of that. And does it really work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Funciona. Graças a esses gestos pequenos, este ano quase não notei o aumento dos preços.
lines.en: It does. Thanks to those small gestures, this year I hardly noticed the price increase.

days.title: A imprensa e as fontes de informação
days.objective: Attribute information to a source and mark what is unconfirmed using 'segundo', 'de acordo com', 'ao que parece' and 'diz-se que'.
days.paretoFocus: Marking where information came from is what separates repeating a rumour from reporting a fact, in speech and in writing alike.
drills: Report three headlines you saw this week, each starting with 'Segundo'.
Rewrite one of them with 'De acordo com' and one with 'Ao que parece'.
Say one thing you heard but cannot confirm, using 'diz-se que'.

items: Which one-word expression means 'according to'?
How do you say 'apparently' at the start of a sentence?
How do you build the impersonal 'it is said that'?

activities.title: Citar uma fonte
activities.intro: When you report something you did not witness, Portuguese asks you to name the source or to flag the doubt. 'Segundo' and 'de acordo com' introduce the source; 'diz-se que' and 'ao que parece' signal that the information is not confirmed.

items.hr: Segundo o jornal de hoje, o tempo vai mudar no fim de semana.
items.en: According to today's newspaper, the weather is going to change at the weekend.
items.note: segundo takes no preposition after it

items.hr: De acordo com os dados divulgados ontem, o número de leitores subiu.
items.en: According to the data released yesterday, the number of readers went up.
items.note: de acordo com is the slightly more formal equivalent

items.hr: Ao que parece, a notícia ainda não foi confirmada por ninguém.
items.en: Apparently, the news has not been confirmed by anyone yet.
items.note: ao que parece = apparently, from what it seems

items.hr: Diz-se que a decisão será anunciada amanhã de manhã.
items.en: It is said that the decision will be announced tomorrow morning.
items.note: diz-se que: impersonal se, no named source

items.hr: Antes de partilhar uma notícia, verifico sempre quem a escreveu e quando.
items.en: Before sharing a piece of news, I always check who wrote it and when.
items.note: notícia is countable: uma notícia = one news item

activities.title: De onde vem a informação?
activities.intro: Practise attributing information and flagging what is still unconfirmed.

questions.prompt: Which expression tells the listener that the information is unconfirmed?
questions.explanation: 'Ao que parece' means apparently. 'Por acaso' means by chance, 'de vez em quando' occasionally and 'ainda por cima' on top of that.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ com o relatório publicado ontem, os preços vão descer.'
questions.explanation: The fixed phrase is 'de acordo com'. 'Segundo' is used alone, without 'com'.

questions.prompt: Supply the single word that attributes the statement to a named source: '___ o jornal de ontem, a greve foi adiada.'
questions.explanation: 'Segundo' is followed directly by the source, with no preposition in between.

questions.prompt: Supply the missing verb form in this impersonal report: '___-se que a notícia é falsa.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal 'se' attaches to the third person singular 'diz', giving 'diz-se que'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The news has not been confirmed yet.'
questions.explanation: In the passive the participle agrees with the subject, so 'confirmada' is feminine singular, and 'ainda' comes before 'não'.

activities.title: Isso é verdade?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague repeats something they read and you push them to say where it came from.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ouvi dizer que vão fechar a biblioteca do bairro.
lines.en: I heard they are going to close the neighbourhood library.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ouviste onde? Ao que parece, ainda não há nada decidido.
lines.en: Where did you hear that? Apparently, nothing has been decided yet.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estava numa mensagem que me enviaram esta manhã.
lines.en: It was in a message someone sent me this morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Segundo o jornal local, a obra é só para arranjar o telhado.
lines.en: According to the local paper, the work is only to fix the roof.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então a mensagem estava errada?
lines.en: So the message was wrong?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: De acordo com os dados que li, sim. E a mensagem não dizia quem a tinha escrito.
lines.en: According to the data I read, yes. And the message did not say who had written it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tens razão, eu nem olhei para isso.
lines.en: You are right, I did not even look at that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Antes de reenviar, vejo sempre quem escreveu e em que dia. Poupa muitos sustos.
lines.en: Before forwarding, I always check who wrote it and on what day. It saves a lot of scares.

days.title: O desporto e a competição: comentar um acontecimento
days.objective: Comment on a sports event by combining the pretérito perfeito and imperfeito and the near-miss expressions 'por pouco não' and 'estar quase a'.
days.paretoFocus: The same tense contrast and near-miss expressions are used to narrate any event, from a match to an accident or a job interview.
drills: Describe a match or race in five sentences, using the imperfect for the background and the perfect for the events.
Write two sentences with 'por pouco não' about things that almost happened to you.
Say aloud three Portuguese words for people in a match: player, goalkeeper, referee.

items: Which tense sets the scene and which reports the events?
How do you say 'we almost scored'?
What is the Portuguese word for goalkeeper?

activities.title: Comentar um jogo
activities.intro: To tell someone about a match you need two past tenses at once: the imperfeito for what was going on, and the pretérito perfeito for what happened. Two near-miss patterns are essential in commentary: 'por pouco não' plus the perfect, and 'estar quase a' plus the infinitive.

items.hr: O jogo estava equilibrado até que o guarda-redes falhou uma bola fácil.
items.en: The match was balanced until the goalkeeper missed an easy ball.
items.note: estava sets the scene, falhou reports the event

items.hr: Por pouco não marcámos nos últimos minutos.
items.en: We almost scored in the last minutes.
items.note: por pouco não: the negative is not translated into English

items.hr: Enquanto a equipa da casa atacava, a outra defendia-se muito bem.
items.en: While the home team was attacking, the other one was defending itself very well.
items.note: two simultaneous backgrounds, both imperfeito

items.hr: Estivemos quase a empatar, mas o árbitro apitou o fim.
items.en: We were about to draw, but the referee blew for the end.
items.note: estar quase a + infinitivo

items.hr: Foi um jogo renhido, ganharam por um golo de diferença.
items.en: It was a closely fought match, they won by one goal.
items.note: renhido = closely fought, hard-fought

activities.title: Contar o que se passou
activities.intro: Perfeito or imperfeito, and how do you say something almost happened? Commentary practice.

questions.prompt: Which word is European Portuguese for the player who defends the goal?
questions.explanation: 'Guarda-redes' is the European Portuguese term. 'Porteiro' is a caretaker and 'avançado' is a forward.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Por pouco não ___ o golo do empate no último minuto.'
questions.explanation: 'Por pouco não' refers to a completed near miss, so it takes the pretérito perfeito.

questions.prompt: Supply the imperfeito of 'estar': 'O resultado ___ empatado quando saí do café.'
questions.explanation: The score is background information that was ongoing, so the imperfeito is required.

questions.prompt: Supply the adjective that describes a match decided by the smallest of margins: 'Foi um jogo muito ___, decidido nos descontos.'
questions.explanation: 'Renhido' describes a hard-fought, very close contest and is the usual word in sports commentary.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'We were about to draw the match.'
questions.explanation: 'Estar quase a' keeps 'quase' between the verb and 'a', and the infinitive follows.

activities.title: Viste o jogo de ontem?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend missed the match and you narrate it, mixing the imperfeito for the scene and the perfeito for the events.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perdi o jogo de ontem. Valeu a pena?
lines.en: I missed yesterday's match. Was it worth it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Foi um jogo renhido. Estava tudo empatado até aos oitenta minutos.
lines.en: It was a closely fought match. Everything was level until the eightieth minute.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E depois? Quem é que marcou?
lines.en: And then? Who scored?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Enquanto nós atacávamos, eles saíram num contra-ataque e marcaram.
lines.en: While we were attacking, they broke away on a counter-attack and scored.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que azar. Não houve resposta?
lines.en: What bad luck. Was there no reply?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Por pouco não empatámos. A bola bateu no poste aos noventa.
lines.en: We almost drew. The ball hit the post in the ninetieth minute.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o guarda-redes deles esteve bem?
lines.en: And was their goalkeeper good?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esteve enorme. Estivemos quase a marcar três vezes e ele defendeu tudo.
lines.en: He was huge. We were about to score three times and he saved everything.

days.title: A música portuguesa: compreender uma letra
days.objective: Understand sung Portuguese by recognising reduced forms, figurative images and inverted word order in lyrics.
days.paretoFocus: The reductions singers use are exactly the ones fast speakers use, so decoding lyrics trains you for real conversation speed.
drills: Say aloud five full forms and their sung reductions, for example 'está' and 'tá'.
Choose a Portuguese song you like and write down three words you catch without help.
Write two sentences about feelings using an image rather than a plain adjective.

items: What full word is hidden behind the sung form 'tá'?
Which noun names the feeling of longing so common in Portuguese songs?
What can you use to guess a word you cannot hear at the end of a line?

activities.title: Perceber uma letra
activities.intro: Sung Portuguese drops unstressed sounds and reorders words to fit the melody, which is why a familiar sentence can sound unrecognisable. Learning the common reductions and listening for the rhyme lets you reconstruct what you did not catch. Lyrics also lean heavily on images rather than plain description.

items.hr: Nas canções, 'está' aparece muitas vezes como 'tá' e 'para' como 'pra'.
items.en: In songs, 'está' often appears as 'tá' and 'para' as 'pra'.
items.note: unstressed first syllables drop in fast or sung speech

items.hr: O fado fala muitas vezes de saudade, de mar e de despedidas.
items.en: Fado often speaks of longing, of the sea and of farewells.
items.note: saudade: the feeling of missing someone or something

items.hr: A letra diz que o coração dela ficou preso ao cais.
items.en: The lyrics say that her heart stayed tied to the quay.
items.note: images replace plain adjectives: preso ao cais = unable to move on

items.hr: Quando não percebo uma palavra, uso a rima para adivinhar o som.
items.en: When I do not catch a word, I use the rhyme to guess the sound.
items.note: a rima = the rhyme; adivinhar = to guess

items.hr: Muitas letras trocam a ordem das palavras: 'ao mar voltei' em vez de 'voltei ao mar'.
items.en: Many lyrics swap the word order: 'ao mar voltei' instead of 'voltei ao mar'.
items.note: the complement can move in front of the verb for rhythm

activities.title: Da letra ao sentido
activities.intro: Practise recognising sung reductions, images and inverted order in lyrics.

questions.prompt: In sung Portuguese, the form 'tá' is a reduction of which full word?
questions.explanation: The unstressed first syllable of 'está' disappears, leaving 'tá'. The other words keep their first syllable.

questions.prompt: Which noun names the feeling of longing that so many Portuguese songs describe?
questions.explanation: 'Saudade' is missing someone or something absent. 'Vontade' is a wish, 'novidade' news and 'liberdade' freedom.

questions.prompt: Supply the sung reduction of 'para a' that you would hear in a song: 'Vou ___ beira-mar contigo.'
questions.explanation: 'Para a' collapses to 'pra' in sung and informal speech, keeping only one syllable.

questions.prompt: Supply the noun for the sound match at the end of two lines: 'Quando não apanho a palavra, sigo a ___ do verso anterior.'
questions.explanation: 'A rima' is the rhyme, and it narrows down the possible endings of the missing word.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The lyrics speak of the sea and of farewells.'
questions.explanation: 'Falar de' contracts with the article: de + o gives 'do', de + as gives 'das'.

activities.title: O que diz aquela canção?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend cannot follow a song in Portuguese and you explain the reductions and the images in the lyrics.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gosto da música, mas não apanho metade das palavras. É normal?
lines.en: I like the song, but I do not catch half the words. Is that normal?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É normalíssimo. Quando ela canta 'tá', está a dizer 'está'. Come a primeira sílaba.
lines.en: It is completely normal. When she sings 'tá', she is saying 'está'. She swallows the first syllable.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E aquele 'pra' que aparece a toda a hora?
lines.en: And that 'pra' that comes up all the time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É 'para a'. Na fala rápida também dizemos assim, não é só nas canções.
lines.en: It is 'para a'. In fast speech we say it like that too, it is not only in songs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o refrão fala de um coração preso ao cais. Isso é literal?
lines.en: And the chorus speaks of a heart tied to the quay. Is that literal?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, é uma imagem. Quer dizer que ela ficou à espera de alguém que partiu.
lines.en: No, it is an image. It means she stayed waiting for someone who left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Há um verso que soa todo ao contrário.
lines.en: There is a line that sounds all back to front.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois é, 'ao mar voltei'. Trocam a ordem para a frase caber na melodia.
lines.en: That is right, 'ao mar voltei'. They swap the order so the phrase fits the melody.

days.title: O cinema: contar e criticar um filme
days.objective: Summarise a film's plot in the present tense and judge it using the vocabulary of directing, script, cast and soundtrack.
days.paretoFocus: Retelling a story in the present and giving a balanced judgement is exactly what conversations about books, series and news events also require.
drills: Summarise a film you know in three present-tense sentences without revealing the ending.
Write one sentence praising one aspect and criticising another with 'mas'.
List the Portuguese words for script, directing, cast and soundtrack.

items: Which tense do you use to summarise a plot?
What is the Portuguese word for the script of a film?
How do you say 'the soundtrack'?

activities.title: Contar e criticar um filme
activities.intro: A plot summary in Portuguese is told in the present tense, however old the film is. To judge it you separate the parts: 'a realização' is the directing, 'o argumento' the script, 'o elenco' the cast and 'a banda sonora' the soundtrack. A balanced review praises one part and criticises another.

items.hr: O filme conta a história de uma médica que volta à aldeia onde nasceu.
items.en: The film tells the story of a doctor who returns to the village where she was born.
items.note: contar a história de: standard opening for a summary

items.hr: A meio da história, ela descobre um segredo de família.
items.en: Halfway through the story, she discovers a family secret.
items.note: a meio de = halfway through

items.hr: A realização é excelente, mas o argumento é fraco na segunda parte.
items.en: The directing is excellent, but the script is weak in the second half.
items.note: realização = directing; argumento = script

items.hr: O que mais me agradou foi a banda sonora e o trabalho do elenco.
items.en: What pleased me most was the soundtrack and the work of the cast.
items.note: agradar a alguém = to please someone

items.hr: No fim, fica tudo em aberto, o que me deixou algo frustrado.
items.en: At the end, everything is left open, which left me somewhat frustrated.
items.note: ficar em aberto = to be left unresolved

activities.title: Falar de um filme
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of film criticism and the present tense used for plot summaries.

questions.prompt: Which Portuguese word names the written script of a film?
questions.explanation: 'O argumento' is the screenplay. 'A realização' is the directing, 'o elenco' the cast and 'a estreia' the premiere.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A ___ sonora deste filme é inesquecível.'
questions.explanation: The fixed term is 'banda sonora'. 'Faixa' is an individual track and the other two do not combine with 'sonora'.

questions.prompt: Supply the present-tense verb used to open a plot summary: 'O filme ___ a história de dois irmãos.'
questions.explanation: Plot summaries stay in the present, so 'contar' appears as 'conta' in the third person singular.

questions.prompt: Supply the noun for the work done by the director: 'A ___ é cuidada, cada plano parece um quadro.'
questions.explanation: In Portuguese the director's craft is 'a realização', from 'realizador'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'What pleased me most was the ending.'
questions.explanation: The cleft structure 'O que ... foi ...' highlights one element, and 'agradar' takes an indirect object pronoun.

activities.title: Vale a pena ver?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about a film you saw and you summarise it in the present and give a balanced judgement.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Viste aquele filme português de que toda a gente fala. É bom?
lines.en: You saw that Portuguese film everybody is talking about. Is it good?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Conta a história de uma médica que volta à aldeia onde nasceu.
lines.en: It tells the story of a doctor who returns to the village where she was born.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E acontece alguma coisa ou é só paisagem?
lines.en: And does anything happen or is it just scenery?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A meio da história, ela descobre um segredo de família. Não digo mais nada.
lines.en: Halfway through the story, she discovers a family secret. I will not say anything else.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E tecnicamente, está bem feito?
lines.en: And technically, is it well made?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A realização é excelente, mas o argumento perde força na segunda parte.
lines.en: The directing is excellent, but the script loses strength in the second half.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então saíste desiludido?
lines.en: So you came out disappointed?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nem por isso. O que mais me agradou foi a banda sonora, e por isso vale a ida ao cinema.
lines.en: Not really. What pleased me most was the soundtrack, and for that reason the trip to the cinema is worth it.

days.title: Ler um conto curto: estratégias de leitura
days.objective: Read a short story efficiently by guessing words from context and by recognising the pluperfect that signals an earlier past.
days.paretoFocus: Reading without stopping at every new word is the single habit that turns slow decoding into real reading speed.
drills: Read one page of any Portuguese text without a dictionary and underline unknown words instead of stopping.
Write two sentences with 'tinha' plus a past participle about things that had happened before another event.
Guess the meaning of three underlined words from context, then check them.

items: Which form places an action before another past action?
What do you do with a new word on a first reading?
What two things do you look for on a second reading?

activities.title: Ler um conto
activities.intro: Short stories jump backwards in time, and Portuguese marks that jump with 'tinha' plus a past participle, the compound pluperfect. Reading strategy matters as much as grammar: on a first pass you underline and keep going, and only on a second pass do you check words and identify the narrator and the setting.

items.hr: Antes de ler, olho para o título e imagino de que trata o conto.
items.en: Before reading, I look at the title and imagine what the story is about.
items.note: tratar de = to be about

items.hr: Quando o narrador diz que ela tinha partido, percebo que isso aconteceu antes.
items.en: When the narrator says that she had left, I understand that this happened earlier.
items.note: tinha + particípio = the earlier of two past events

items.hr: Não paro em cada palavra nova, sublinho e continuo até ao fim do parágrafo.
items.en: I do not stop at every new word, I underline it and carry on to the end of the paragraph.
items.note: sublinhar = to underline

items.hr: Pelo contexto, percebi que 'trémulo' queria dizer que ele tremia.
items.en: From the context, I understood that 'trémulo' meant that he was trembling.
items.note: pelo contexto = from the context; querer dizer = to mean

items.hr: Na segunda leitura, procuro quem conta a história e onde se passa.
items.en: On the second reading, I look for who tells the story and where it takes place.
items.note: passar-se = to take place

activities.title: Estratégias e tempos do conto
activities.intro: Practise the pluperfect and the vocabulary of reading strategies.

questions.prompt: Which form places one action before another action already in the past?
questions.explanation: 'Tinha' plus a past participle is the compound pluperfect, used for the earlier of two past events.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quando cheguei ao café, ele já ___ saído.'
questions.explanation: The auxiliary of the compound pluperfect is 'ter' in the imperfeito, so 'tinha saído'.

questions.prompt: Supply the noun for the surrounding words that help you guess a meaning: 'Percebi a palavra nova pelo ___ da frase.'
questions.explanation: 'Pelo contexto' is the standard way to say you worked the meaning out from the surrounding text.

questions.prompt: Supply the past participle of 'escrever': 'O conto tinha sido ___ há mais de cinquenta anos.'
questions.explanation: 'Escrever' has the irregular participle 'escrito', with no ending in 'ido'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Before reading, I look at the title.'
questions.explanation: 'Antes de' is followed by the infinitive, and 'olhar' requires 'para' before its object.

activities.title: Aquele conto é difícil?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is stuck on a short story and you share your reading strategy, using the pluperfect to explain the time jumps.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comecei o conto e desisti na primeira página. Havia palavras a mais.
lines.en: I started the story and gave up on the first page. There were too many words I did not know.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não pares em cada palavra. Sublinha e continua até ao fim do parágrafo.
lines.en: Do not stop at every word. Underline it and carry on to the end of the paragraph.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas assim não percebo nada.
lines.en: But that way I do not understand anything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Percebes mais do que pensas. Pelo contexto, quase sempre se adivinha o sentido.
lines.en: You understand more than you think. From the context, you can almost always guess the meaning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Há uma parte em que ele fala do passado e eu perco-me.
lines.en: There is a part where he talks about the past and I get lost.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Repara nos verbos. Quando diz que ela tinha partido, é porque isso aconteceu antes do resto.
lines.en: Look at the verbs. When it says that she had left, it is because that happened before the rest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E vale a pena ler outra vez do início?
lines.en: And is it worth reading again from the start?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vale. Na segunda leitura procura só quem conta a história e onde se passa.
lines.en: It is. On the second reading just look for who tells the story and where it takes place.

days.title: Desejos, arrependimentos e intenções
days.objective: Express wishes, regrets and intentions with 'quem me dera que', 'arrepender-se de ter', 'devia ter' and 'tencionar'.
days.paretoFocus: Wishes and regrets carry most of the emotional weight in personal conversation, and 'devia ter' is the standard way to apologise properly.
drills: Write two regrets about the past with 'devia ter' plus a participle.
Write two wishes using 'quem me dera que' followed by the imperfect subjunctive.
State three intentions for the next twelve months using 'tenciono'.

items: How do you say 'I should have warned you'?
Which mood follows 'quem me dera que'?
Which verb means 'to intend' in a slightly formal register?

activities.title: Quem me dera...
activities.intro: Portuguese has one pattern for each of these three ideas. A wish about an unreal present uses 'quem me dera que' plus the imperfect subjunctive; a regret uses 'arrepender-se de ter' or 'devia ter' plus the past participle; an intention uses 'tencionar' plus the infinitive.

items.hr: Gostaria de ter estudado português muito mais cedo.
items.en: I would like to have studied Portuguese much earlier.
items.note: gostaria de ter + particípio = a wish about the past

items.hr: Quem me dera que os dias tivessem mais duas horas.
items.en: I wish the days had two more hours.
items.note: quem me dera que + imperfeito do conjuntivo: ter becomes tivessem

items.hr: Arrependo-me de não ter aceitado aquele convite.
items.en: I regret not having accepted that invitation.
items.note: arrepender-se de + infinitivo composto

items.hr: Devia ter avisado, peço imensa desculpa.
items.en: I should have warned you, I am terribly sorry.
items.note: devia ter + particípio = should have done

items.hr: Tenciono mudar de cidade no próximo ano.
items.en: I intend to move to another city next year.
items.note: tencionar + infinitivo, slightly more formal than 'pensar em'

activities.title: O que faria diferente
activities.intro: Practise the structures for wishes, regrets and intentions.

questions.prompt: Which structure expresses regret about something you failed to do?
questions.explanation: 'Devia ter' plus a participle looks back at an unfulfilled obligation. The others refer to the present or the future.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quem me dera que ele ___ aqui connosco agora.'
questions.explanation: 'Quem me dera que' presents the wish as unreal, so it takes the imperfect subjunctive 'estivesse'.

questions.prompt: Supply the verb that states a plan in a formal register: '___ mudar de emprego no verão.'
questions.explanation: 'Tencionar' plus the infinitive announces an intention and is common in careful speech and writing.

questions.prompt: Supply the reflexive verb of regret in the first person: '___-me de não ter ido à festa dela.'
questions.explanation: 'Arrepender-se de' is reflexive, so the first person singular is 'arrependo-me de'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I would like to have studied more.'
questions.explanation: The conditional of 'gostar' takes 'de' plus the compound infinitive 'ter estudado' for a wish about the past.

activities.title: Se voltasse atrás
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks what you would change and you answer with wishes, regrets and intentions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se pudesses voltar atrás dez anos, o que farias de diferente?
lines.en: If you could go back ten years, what would you do differently?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gostaria de ter estudado línguas mais cedo. Agora custa-me muito mais.
lines.en: I would like to have studied languages earlier. Now it is much harder for me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais alguma coisa te ficou atravessada?
lines.en: Is there anything else that still bothers you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Arrependo-me de não ter aceitado um trabalho no estrangeiro em 2018.
lines.en: I regret not having accepted a job abroad in 2018.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ainda vais a tempo, não vais?
lines.en: You are still in time, are you not?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Talvez. Tenciono candidatar-me outra vez no início do ano.
lines.en: Perhaps. I intend to apply again at the start of the year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o que te falta para decidires?
lines.en: And what do you need in order to decide?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tempo. Quem me dera que os dias tivessem mais duas horas.
lines.en: Time. I wish the days had two more hours.

days.title: Matizar a opinião: atenuar e insistir
days.objective: Soften or reinforce an opinion using 'se calhar', 'talvez' with the subjunctive, 'de certo modo', 'sem dúvida' and 'fazer questão de'.
days.paretoFocus: Knowing how strongly to state an opinion decides whether you sound rude, vague or convincing, and it costs only a handful of set phrases.
drills: State one opinion three times: hedged, neutral and emphatic.
Write two sentences with 'talvez' plus the present subjunctive.
Say aloud one point you would insist on, using 'faço questão de'.

items: Which two-word phrase softens a statement at the start of a sentence?
Which mood follows 'talvez'?
How do you say 'I insist on saying' in Portuguese?

activities.title: Atenuar e insistir
activities.intro: Portuguese speakers rarely state a strong opinion flat. To soften they use 'se calhar', 'talvez' plus the subjunctive, and 'de certo modo'; to reinforce they use 'fazer questão de' and 'sem dúvida'. Note that 'talvez' before the verb requires the subjunctive.

items.hr: Se calhar estou enganado, mas parece-me que a ideia não resulta.
items.en: Maybe I am wrong, but it seems to me that the idea does not work.
items.note: se calhar = maybe; resultar = to work out

items.hr: Talvez seja melhor esperar mais uns dias antes de decidir.
items.en: Perhaps it is better to wait a few more days before deciding.
items.note: talvez before the verb takes the conjuntivo: seja, not é

items.hr: De certo modo, tens razão, embora eu veja as coisas de outra maneira.
items.en: In a way, you are right, although I see things differently.
items.note: de certo modo = in a way, up to a point

items.hr: Sem dúvida que este é o caminho certo, e insisto nisso.
items.en: This is without doubt the right path, and I insist on that.
items.note: insistir em: em + isso gives nisso

items.hr: Faço questão de dizer que não estou contra a proposta.
items.en: I make a point of saying that I am not against the proposal.
items.note: fazer questão de + infinitivo = to insist on doing

activities.title: Mais forte ou mais suave?
activities.intro: Practise choosing between hedging phrases and emphatic ones.

questions.prompt: Which expression signals that the speaker is certain rather than hesitant?
questions.explanation: 'Sem dúvida' asserts certainty; the other three all weaken the claim to different degrees.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Talvez ___ melhor falarmos disto amanhã.'
questions.explanation: 'Talvez' placed before the verb requires the present subjunctive, so 'ser' becomes 'seja'.

questions.prompt: Supply the missing noun: 'De certo ___, concordo contigo, mas tenho algumas reservas.'
questions.explanation: 'De certo modo' means 'in a way' and softens an agreement that is only partial.

questions.prompt: Supply the two words that hedge the opening of this claim: '___ ___ estou enganado, mas os números não batem certo.'
questions.explanation: 'Se calhar' is the everyday European Portuguese hedge and, unlike 'talvez', takes the indicative.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Perhaps it is better to wait.'
questions.explanation: 'Talvez' comes first, the subjunctive 'seja' follows, and the infinitive closes the phrase.

activities.title: Não concordo, mas com jeito
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague defends a plan and you disagree, first softening your objection and then insisting on one point.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Acho que devíamos avançar já com o projeto esta semana.
lines.en: I think we should go ahead with the project this week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se calhar estou enganado, mas parece-me cedo de mais.
lines.en: Maybe I am wrong, but it seems too early to me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cedo? Já estamos a discutir isto há um mês.
lines.en: Too early? We have been discussing this for a month already.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: De certo modo, tens razão. Ainda assim, talvez seja melhor ouvir a equipa toda.
lines.en: In a way, you are right. Even so, perhaps it is better to hear the whole team.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso atrasa-nos mais duas semanas.
lines.en: That delays us another two weeks.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Faço questão de ouvir quem vai executar o trabalho. Nisso não abro mão.
lines.en: I make a point of hearing the people who will do the work. On that I will not give way.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem. E depois disso avançamos?
lines.en: All right. And after that we go ahead?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sem dúvida. Com a equipa de acordo, o projeto anda muito mais depressa.
lines.en: Without doubt. With the team in agreement, the project moves much faster.

days.title: Estruturar uma apresentação oral de três minutos
days.objective: Structure a short spoken presentation with signposting phrases that announce, connect and close each part.
days.paretoFocus: Signposting makes a listener follow you even when your grammar slips, and the same phrases work in meetings, classes and interviews.
drills: Plan a three minute talk in three points and write the opening sentence for each.
Say the whole talk out loud once, timing yourself with a clock.
Rewrite your closing line so it ends with a question to the audience.

items: Which phrase announces that you are moving to the next point?
How do you say 'as I already mentioned'?
Which pronoun form attaches to 'deixo' when addressing a group?

activities.title: Estruturar uma apresentação
activities.intro: A short talk in Portuguese lives or dies by its signposts: they tell the listener where you are. 'Em primeiro lugar' opens, 'passo agora a' moves on, 'como já referi' refers back and 'para concluir' closes. Note the enclitic pronoun in 'deixo-vos', normal in careful spoken Portuguese.

items.hr: Em primeiro lugar, gostava de explicar porque escolhi este tema.
items.en: First of all, I would like to explain why I chose this topic.
items.note: gostava de is the everyday equivalent of gostaria de

items.hr: Vou dividir a minha apresentação em três partes muito curtas.
items.en: I am going to divide my presentation into three very short parts.
items.note: dividir em, never dividir a

items.hr: Passo agora ao segundo ponto, que tem a ver com os custos.
items.en: I now move on to the second point, which has to do with the costs.
items.note: passar a + noun; ter a ver com = to have to do with

items.hr: Como já referi, os dados que uso são deste ano.
items.en: As I already mentioned, the data I am using are from this year.
items.note: de + este contracts to deste

items.hr: Para concluir, deixo-vos uma pergunta para pensarem.
items.en: To conclude, I leave you a question to think about.
items.note: enclisis: the pronoun follows the verb in an affirmative main clause

activities.title: Os sinais da apresentação
activities.intro: Practise the phrases that signpost the parts of a spoken presentation.

questions.prompt: Which phrase announces that the speaker is moving to the next section?
questions.explanation: 'Passar a' plus the next point is the standard transition. The other options express chance, frequency and concession.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Para ___, queria deixar uma ideia final.'
questions.explanation: After the preposition 'para' Portuguese uses the infinitive, so 'concluir'.

questions.prompt: Supply the ordinal that opens the first section of a talk: 'Em ___ lugar, vou apresentar-me.'
questions.explanation: 'Em primeiro lugar' is the fixed opening formula, and the ordinal agrees with the masculine 'lugar'.

questions.prompt: Supply the enclitic pronoun for addressing the whole audience: 'Para terminar, deixo-___ uma pergunta.'
questions.explanation: 'Vos' is the second person plural object pronoun and attaches to the verb with a hyphen after an affirmative verb.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I am going to divide my presentation into three parts.'
questions.explanation: The possessive takes the article in European Portuguese, and 'dividir' requires 'em'.

activities.title: Ensaiar antes de apresentar
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague helps you rehearse and you answer using the signposting phrases of a short talk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tens três minutos. Como é que pensas organizar isso?
lines.en: You have three minutes. How are you planning to organise that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vou dividir a apresentação em três partes: o problema, os custos e a proposta.
lines.en: I am going to divide the presentation into three parts: the problem, the costs and the proposal.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E como começas?
lines.en: And how do you begin?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Em primeiro lugar, explico porque escolhi este tema, em duas frases apenas.
lines.en: First of all, I explain why I chose this topic, in two sentences only.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cuidado com a passagem para os números, é aí que as pessoas se perdem.
lines.en: Be careful with the transition to the figures, that is where people get lost.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Por isso digo em voz alta: passo agora ao segundo ponto, que tem a ver com os custos.
lines.en: That is why I say out loud: I now move on to the second point, which has to do with the costs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o fim? Não acabes a pedir desculpa.
lines.en: And the end? Do not finish by apologising.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Para concluir, deixo-vos uma pergunta. Assim a discussão começa logo ali.
lines.en: To conclude, I leave you a question. That way the discussion starts right there.

days.title: Compreensão do oral: identificar a informação essencial
days.objective: Apply a listening routine that captures the essential information of a recording before, during and after it plays.
days.paretoFocus: In any listening test the marks are lost between recordings, not during them, so the routine matters more than raw comprehension.
drills: Take any short Portuguese recording, read the questions first and underline the key words.
Listen once for topic and speakers only, writing nothing but two words.
Listen a second time and complete your answers, noting every number you hear.

items: What do you do before the recording starts?
What are you listening for on the first play?
What should you do if you miss one answer?

activities.title: Ouvir para captar o essencial
activities.intro: Listening tasks are won with a routine, not with speed. Before the audio you read the questions and underline the key words; on the first play you catch only the topic and the speakers; on the second you confirm and fill the gaps. Numbers and times are said fast, so write them the moment you hear them.

items.hr: Antes de ouvir, leio as perguntas e sublinho as palavras-chave.
items.en: Before listening, I read the questions and underline the key words.
items.note: palavras-chave: the plural falls on the first element

items.hr: Na primeira audição, procuro apenas o tema geral e quem está a falar.
items.en: On the first play, I look only for the general topic and who is speaking.
items.note: a audição = the listening, the play of a recording

items.hr: Os números e as horas são ditos depressa, por isso escrevo-os logo.
items.en: Numbers and times are said fast, so I write them down straight away.
items.note: escrevo-os: enclitic o becomes os agreeing with the plural

items.hr: Se perder uma resposta, não paro, continuo para não perder as seguintes.
items.en: If I miss an answer, I do not stop, I carry on so as not to miss the following ones.
items.note: se perder: future subjunctive after se

items.hr: Na segunda audição, confirmo o que escrevi e completo o que faltava.
items.en: On the second play, I confirm what I wrote and complete what was missing.
items.note: faltava: imperfeito for what was still missing at that moment

activities.title: A rotina da audição
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary and the sequence of an effective listening routine.

questions.prompt: What should you do in the seconds before a recording starts?
options: Ler as perguntas e sublinhar as palavras-chave
Traduzir tudo para a sua língua
questions.answer: Ler as perguntas e sublinhar as palavras-chave
questions.explanation: Reading the questions first tells you what to listen for, which is the only preparation that changes your score.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Na primeira ___, procuro só o tema geral.'
questions.explanation: 'Audição' is the act of listening to a recording; 'leitura' is reading and 'escrita' writing.

questions.prompt: Supply the second element of the compound noun for the important words in a question: 'Sublinho as palavras-___ antes de ouvir.'
questions.explanation: In 'palavras-chave' only the first element takes the plural, so the second stays 'chave'.

questions.prompt: Supply the future subjunctive of a verb meaning to lose or to miss something: 'Se ___ uma resposta, sigo em frente.'
questions.explanation: After 'se' referring to the future Portuguese uses the future subjunctive, which for this verb looks like the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I write the numbers down immediately on the paper.'
questions.explanation: The adverb sits after the object, and 'em' plus 'o' contracts to 'no'.

activities.title: Perdi metade da gravação
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A study partner panics about listening tasks and you explain your routine step by step.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Na parte de audição perco-me sempre. Ouço tudo e não fico com nada.
lines.en: In the listening part I always get lost. I hear everything and I end up with nothing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Antes de a gravação começar, lê as perguntas e sublinha as palavras-chave.
lines.en: Before the recording starts, read the questions and underline the key words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas há pouco tempo para isso.
lines.en: But there is little time for that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Chega. Na primeira audição procura só o tema e quem fala, não escrevas frases.
lines.en: It is enough. On the first play look only for the topic and who is speaking, do not write sentences.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as datas e os preços? Passam a correr.
lines.en: And the dates and the prices? They go by in a flash.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Escreve-os no momento em que os ouves, mesmo que ainda não saibas a pergunta.
lines.en: Write them down the moment you hear them, even if you do not yet know the question.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se falhar uma resposta, volto atrás mentalmente?
lines.en: And if I miss an answer, do I go back over it in my head?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nunca. Se perderes uma, segue em frente e recupera-a na segunda audição.
lines.en: Never. If you miss one, carry on and pick it up on the second play.

days.title: Produção escrita: o e-mail argumentado
days.objective: Write a formal argued email that opens properly, balances two arguments and closes with a request and a formal sign-off.
days.paretoFocus: The same four moves, formal opening, one argument each side, request, sign-off, cover complaints, applications and requests to any institution.
drills: Write a five line email disagreeing with a rule, using one 'por um lado' and one 'por outro lado'.
Rewrite your request sentence with 'peço que' plus the subjunctive.
Practise the formal opening and closing formulas from memory, without looking.

items: How do you open a formal written complaint?
Which mood follows 'peço que'?
What is the standard formal sign-off in Portuguese?

activities.title: Escrever um e-mail argumentado
activities.intro: A formal argued email in Portuguese follows a fixed shape. It opens with 'Venho por este meio', develops two sides with 'por um lado' and 'por outro lado', makes its request with 'peço que' plus the subjunctive, and closes with a set formula. The register stays impersonal throughout.

items.hr: Venho por este meio manifestar o meu desacordo com a nova regra.
items.en: I am writing to express my disagreement with the new rule.
items.note: venho por este meio + infinitivo: standard formal opening

items.hr: Por um lado, a medida prejudica quem trabalha por turnos.
items.en: On the one hand, the measure harms those who work shifts.
items.note: prejudicar = to harm; por turnos = on shifts

items.hr: Por outro lado, existem alternativas mais simples e menos dispendiosas.
items.en: On the other hand, there are simpler and less costly alternatives.
items.note: dispendioso is the formal word for expensive

items.hr: Por tudo isto, peço que a decisão seja revista com brevidade.
items.en: For all these reasons, I ask that the decision be reviewed shortly.
items.note: pedir que + conjuntivo; passive with ser plus participle

items.hr: Fico a aguardar uma resposta. Com os melhores cumprimentos,
items.en: I look forward to a reply. Kind regards,
items.note: ficar a aguardar; the sign-off ends with a comma before the name

activities.title: As peças do e-mail formal
activities.intro: Practise the openings, connectors and closings of a formal argued email.

questions.prompt: Which opening suits a formal written complaint to an institution?
options: Aqui vai o que eu penso
questions.explanation: Only this option keeps the impersonal formal register; the others address the reader informally.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Peço que a decisão ___ revista o mais depressa possível.'
questions.explanation: 'Pedir que' requires the subjunctive, and the passive is built with 'ser' plus the participle, giving 'seja revista'.

questions.prompt: Supply the missing word so the pair of arguments is balanced: 'Por um lado, é caro; por ___ lado, resolve o problema.'
questions.explanation: The fixed pair is 'por um lado ... por outro lado', used to present two sides of the same question.

questions.prompt: Supply the noun that completes the standard formal sign-off: 'Com os melhores ___,'
questions.explanation: 'Com os melhores cumprimentos' is the usual formal closing in European Portuguese correspondence.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I look forward to your reply.'
questions.explanation: 'Ficar a aguardar' uses the European Portuguese progressive with 'a', and 'vossa' addresses an institution.

activities.title: Rever o e-mail antes de enviar
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A study partner has drafted a formal email and you correct its structure and register.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Escrevi o e-mail. Comecei com 'Olá, queria só dizer que não concordo'.
lines.en: I wrote the email. I started with 'Hi, I just wanted to say that I disagree'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Isso é demasiado informal. Escreve antes: venho por este meio manifestar o meu desacordo.
lines.en: That is too informal. Write instead: I am writing to express my disagreement.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Depois pus três razões todas seguidas.
lines.en: Then I put three reasons one after the other.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fica melhor com dois lados: por um lado, o problema; por outro lado, a alternativa.
lines.en: It works better with two sides: on the one hand the problem, on the other hand the alternative.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E no fim escrevi 'mudem isso, por favor'.
lines.en: And at the end I wrote 'change that, please'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Um pedido formal fica assim: peço que a decisão seja revista.
lines.en: A formal request goes like this: I ask that the decision be reviewed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E assino como? Pus só o meu nome.
lines.en: And how do I sign off? I just put my name.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Acrescenta uma linha antes: fico a aguardar uma resposta, com os melhores cumprimentos.
lines.en: Add a line before it: I look forward to a reply, kind regards.

days.title: Treinar o oral: simulação guiada
days.objective: Keep a spoken interaction going by paraphrasing unknown words, buying time politely, asking for repetition and justifying every opinion.
days.paretoFocus: In speaking tasks and in real life, fluency is judged on whether you keep going, not on whether you know every word.
drills: Describe five objects without naming them, using 'é uma coisa que serve para'.
Answer three opinion questions out loud, adding a concrete example each time.
Practise three ways of buying time before answering a difficult question.

items: How do you politely ask someone to repeat a question?
What do you say to buy a few seconds before answering?
What should follow every opinion you give?

activities.title: Falar sem parar
activities.intro: Speaking well under pressure is mostly a set of repair strategies. When a word escapes you, describe it instead; when you need a second, use a filler; when you did not catch the question, ask politely for a repetition. Every opinion should be followed by a concrete example.

items.hr: Se não me lembro de uma palavra, explico-a por outras palavras.
items.en: If I do not remember a word, I explain it in other words.
items.note: lembrar-se de; explico-a: enclitic pronoun for a feminine noun

items.hr: Ganho tempo com expressões como 'deixe-me pensar' ou 'é uma boa pergunta'.
items.en: I buy time with expressions such as 'let me think' or 'that is a good question'.
items.note: deixe-me pensar: formal you; deixa-me pensar with tu

items.hr: Quando não percebo a pergunta, digo: importa-se de repetir?
items.en: When I do not understand the question, I say: would you mind repeating?
items.note: importar-se de + infinitivo: polite request

items.hr: Justifico sempre a minha opinião com um exemplo concreto.
items.en: I always back up my opinion with a concrete example.
items.note: justificar com = to back up with

items.hr: No fim, faço um resumo curto do que disse em duas frases.
items.en: At the end, I make a short summary of what I said in two sentences.
items.note: fazer um resumo de = to summarise

activities.title: Estratégias para não parar
activities.intro: Practise the repair and time-buying strategies of a guided speaking simulation.

questions.prompt: Which phrase politely asks the other person to say the question again?
questions.explanation: 'Importar-se de' plus the infinitive is the polite way to ask a favour of someone you address formally.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Deixe-me ___ um momento antes de responder.'
questions.explanation: 'Deixar' plus another verb takes the infinitive, so 'deixe-me pensar'.

questions.prompt: Supply the first person verb of the paraphrase strategy: 'Se não sei a palavra exata, ___ a ideia por outras palavras.'
questions.explanation: 'Explicar por outras palavras' is the standard way to describe paraphrasing, here in the first person singular.

questions.prompt: Supply the noun for the piece of evidence that should follow an opinion: 'Justifico sempre a minha opinião com um ___ concreto.'
questions.explanation: An opinion in a speaking task counts for much more when it is supported by 'um exemplo concreto'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'At the end, I summarise what I said.'
questions.explanation: 'Resumir' takes a direct object, and 'o que' introduces the free relative clause with no preposition.

activities.title: Simulação: dois minutos a falar
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An examiner-style partner runs a short speaking simulation and you use repair strategies to keep going.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Fale-me de um objeto de que não conseguiria prescindir.
lines.en: Good afternoon. Tell me about an object you could not do without.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Deixe-me pensar um momento. É uma coisa pequena que serve para ver as horas e as mensagens.
lines.en: Let me think for a moment. It is a small thing that is used to see the time and messages.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está a falar do telemóvel?
lines.en: Are you talking about the mobile phone?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exatamente, o telemóvel. Não vivo sem ele porque trabalho fora todos os dias.
lines.en: Exactly, the mobile phone. I cannot live without it because I work away every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E acha que dependemos de mais no dia a dia?
lines.en: And do you think we depend on it too much day to day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Importa-se de repetir a pergunta? Não apanhei a parte do fim.
lines.en: Would you mind repeating the question? I did not catch the last part.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Claro. Acha que dependemos demasiado desse objeto?
lines.en: Of course. Do you think we depend too much on that object?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Acho que sim. Por exemplo, ao jantar em casa ninguém fala, estão todos a olhar para o ecrã.
lines.en: I think so. For example, at dinner at home nobody speaks, everyone is looking at the screen.

days.title: Revisão B1 I
days.objective: Consolidate the B1 verb system: presente do conjuntivo, futuro and condicional, and the compound tenses tenho feito / tinha feito.
days.paretoFocus: These four structures are the engine of B1, reactivating them together in mixed exercises cements them far better than reviewing each in isolation.
drills: Recite the seven irregular conjuntivos: seja, esteja, vá, dê, saiba, queira, haja.
Make three wishes for next year with 'Espero que...' + conjuntivo.
Tell a short story that uses 'já tinha + particípio' twice.

items: Recall the conjuntivo triggers: querer que, é preciso que, talvez, embora.
Say the difference between 'tenho estudado' and 'estudei'.
Translate: 'When I arrived, the train had already left.'

activities.title: O sistema verbal do B1
activities.intro: One key sentence per structure, read each one, cover it, and rebuild it from memory.

items.hr: Quero que venhas comigo ao mercado amanhã.
items.en: I want you to come with me to the market tomorrow.
items.note: querer que + presente do conjuntivo (vir → venhas)

items.hr: Talvez chova à tarde, é melhor levares o casaco.
items.en: Maybe it will rain in the afternoon, you had better take your coat.
items.note: talvez + conjuntivo (chover → chova)

items.hr: Embora seja caro, o restaurante vale a pena.
items.en: Although it is expensive, the restaurant is worth it.
items.note: embora + conjuntivo; seja is one of the seven irregulars

items.hr: Um dia falarei português na perfeição.
items.en: One day I will speak Portuguese perfectly.
items.note: futuro simples: infinitive + -ei/-ás/-á/-emos/-ão

items.hr: Gostaria de marcar uma consulta para amanhã de manhã.
items.en: I would like to book an appointment for tomorrow morning.
items.note: condicional for polite requests: gostaria, poderia

items.hr: Ultimamente tenho estudado todos os dias.
items.en: Lately I have been studying every day.
items.note: pretérito perfeito composto = repetition up to now, with 'ultimamente'

items.hr: Quando ligaste, eu já tinha saído de casa.
items.en: When you called, I had already left the house.
items.note: mais-que-perfeito composto = past before another past

activities.title: Tudo misturado
activities.intro: Conjuntivo, condicional, compound tenses, all side by side: pick the form each sentence demands.

questions.prompt: 'É preciso que tu ___ na estação às nove em ponto.'
questions.explanation: 'É preciso que' triggers the presente do conjuntivo; estar is irregular: esteja, estejas.

questions.prompt: 'No teu lugar, eu ___ com o senhorio antes de assinar.' (conditional)
questions.explanation: Hypothetical advice ('in your place') takes the condicional: falaria.

questions.prompt: 'Duvido que ele ___ mudar de emprego.'
questions.explanation: 'Duvidar que' expresses doubt, so it takes the conjuntivo; querer → queira is irregular.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pretérito perfeito composto: 'Este mês ___ trabalhado demasiado.' (ter, eu)
questions.explanation: Repeated action up to the present: presente de ter + particípio, tenho trabalhado.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quando cheguei ao cinema, o filme já ___ começado.' (ter, mais-que-perfeito)
questions.explanation: A past before another past: imperfeito de ter + particípio, tinha começado.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I hope you have a good weekend.'
questions.explanation: 'Esperar que' triggers the conjuntivo: ter → tenhas.

activities.title: Férias nos Açores
activities.intro: A friend is planning a group trip to the Azores. Play 'Me', wishes, doubts and plans all mix the B1 tenses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Espero que possas vir connosco aos Açores em setembro.
lines.en: I hope you can come with us to the Azores in September.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Talvez vá, mas é preciso que o chefe me dê os dias de férias.
lines.en: Maybe I'll go, but my boss needs to give me the days off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fala com ele já. Quando pedi as minhas, os voos baratos já tinham desaparecido.
lines.en: Talk to him now. When I asked for mine, the cheap flights had already disappeared.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão. Ultimamente tenho trabalhado imenso; mereço um descanso.
lines.en: You're right. Lately I've been working loads; I deserve a rest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nesse caso, gostaria de reservar a casa ainda esta semana.
lines.en: In that case, I'd like to book the house this very week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Embora setembro esteja longe, é melhor que trates disso agora.
lines.en: Although September is far off, it's better that you deal with it now.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vou reservar hoje à noite. Depois digo-te o preço.
lines.en: I'll book tonight. Then I'll tell you the price.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito. Duvido que encontres coisa melhor do que aquela casa!
lines.en: Perfect. I doubt you'll find anything better than that house!

days.title: Revisão B1 II
days.objective: Consolidate B1 themes and tools, bureaucracy, media and environment vocabulary woven with relatives, reported speech and connectors, and self-assess with a can-do checklist.
days.paretoFocus: Mixing themes with structures is exactly what real conversation demands; the can-do check tells you honestly whether the B1 block has landed.
drills: Simulate a Finanças visit: greet formally, explain your problem, ask the clerk to repeat.
Summarise a telejornal news story, then report it: 'O jornalista disse que...'.
Argue for recycling in four sentences, each starting with a different connector.

items: Can-do check: report what a friend said yesterday using 'disse que' with the tense shifted back, without hesitating.
Can-do check: give a one-minute opinion on recycling using porém, além disso and ou seja.
Can-do check: describe your neighbourhood with que, onde and cujo, if any of the three fails, revisit the relative pronouns day before moving on.

activities.title: O B1 em sete frases
activities.intro: Seven sentences, each combining a B1 theme with a B1 structure. Read, understand, then rebuild each from its English.

items.hr: Marquei atendimento nas Finanças para tratar dos impostos.
items.en: I booked an appointment at the tax office to deal with my taxes.
items.note: bureaucracy: marcar atendimento, tratar de

items.hr: O jornalista disse que a notícia tinha sido confirmada por duas fontes.
items.en: The journalist said the news had been confirmed by two sources.
items.note: media + reported speech + passive, all in one

items.hr: O documentário cujo tema são as alterações climáticas ganhou um prémio.
items.en: The documentary whose subject is climate change won a prize.
items.note: cujo + media + environment

items.hr: O ecoponto onde deixamos o vidro fica ao fundo da rua.
items.en: The recycling point where we leave the glass is at the end of the street.
items.note: onde + environment vocabulary

items.hr: A reciclagem dá trabalho; no entanto, é essencial para o ambiente.
items.en: Recycling takes effort; however, it is essential for the environment.
items.note: connector 'no entanto' structuring an opinion

items.hr: A funcionária pediu-me para preencher o formulário e assinar no fim.
items.en: The clerk asked me to fill in the form and sign at the end.
items.note: reported request: pedir para + infinitivo

items.hr: Depende de ti: podes desistir ou continuar a esforçar-te.
items.en: It depends on you: you can give up or keep making an effort.
items.note: regência recap: depender de, desistir, esforçar-se

activities.title: Prova geral do bloco
activities.intro: Everything at once: relatives, reported speech, connectors and the passive across the B1 themes.

questions.prompt: 'O documentário ___ tema é o ambiente ganhou um prémio.'
questions.explanation: 'Cujo' expresses possession and agrees with 'o tema': the documentary whose subject.

questions.prompt: How do you report the clerk's request «Preencha o formulário.»?
options: A funcionária pediu-me para preencher o formulário.
A funcionária disse que preencho o formulário.
A funcionária perguntou se preenche o formulário.
A funcionária pediu que preencho o formulário.
questions.answer: A funcionária pediu-me para preencher o formulário.
questions.explanation: Requests and commands are reported with 'pedir para + infinitivo' in spoken pt-PT.

questions.prompt: 'Separo sempre o vidro; ___, levo o papel ao ecoponto.' (addition)
questions.explanation: A second argument in the same direction needs the addition connector 'além disso'.

questions.prompt: Complete the reported speech: 'A jornalista disse que a manchete ___ sido escrita à pressa.' (ter)
questions.explanation: Reported past-before-past: mais-que-perfeito composto, tinha sido escrita (also passive!).

questions.prompt: Complete with the place relative: 'A junta de freguesia ___ tratei dos documentos fica perto do mercado.'
questions.explanation: 'Onde' = the place relative: the parish office where I sorted out my documents.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The proposal was accepted by all the neighbours.'
questions.explanation: Passive with the pt-PT participle 'aceite' and the agent introduced by 'por'.

activities.title: Balanço de um ano
activities.intro: A Portuguese friend asks how far your Portuguese has come. Play 'Me', this is your informal B1 self-assessment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já estás em Portugal há um ano. Sentes que o teu português melhorou?
lines.en: You've been in Portugal for a year now. Do you feel your Portuguese has improved?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sem dúvida. Ontem percebi o telejornal todo, o que era impossível há meses.
lines.en: Without a doubt. Yesterday I understood the whole TV news, which was impossible months ago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as coisas práticas? Consegues tratar de tudo nas Finanças?
lines.en: And the practical stuff? Can you handle everything at the tax office?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Consigo. A funcionária pediu-me para preencher um formulário e percebi tudo.
lines.en: I can. The clerk asked me to fill in a form and I understood everything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quando alguém fala depressa? O que é que fazes?
lines.en: And when someone speaks fast? What do you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Peço à pessoa para repetir; ou seja, já não finjo que percebo tudo.
lines.en: I ask the person to repeat; in other words, I no longer pretend I understand everything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Disseste-me que querias ler um romance português. Já começaste?
lines.en: You told me you wanted to read a Portuguese novel. Have you started?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comecei um livro cujo autor é do Porto. Custa; no entanto, não desisto.
lines.en: I started a book whose author is from Porto. It's hard; however, I'm not giving up.

days.title: DEPLE: Compreensão da Leitura
days.objective: Tackle the DEPLE reading paper with a strategy: skim announcements, letters and short articles, hunt for the deciding detail, and spot distractor traps.
days.paretoFocus: Reading is 25% of the DEPLE and the most trainable component: reading the questions first and scanning for names, dates and numbers wins most of the marks before you even read the full text.
resources: CAPLE, Exames de PLE (Universidade de Lisboa)
drills: Skim any Portuguese announcement online and answer in one sentence: quem, quando, onde, quanto?
Read a short pt-PT news item and give it a one-line title in Portuguese.
Invent two plausible wrong options ('distratores') for the question 'Quando reabre a biblioteca?' and explain each trap.

items: Recall: in the exam, what should you read first, the text or the questions?
Say three text types that appear in the DEPLE reading paper: anúncios, cartas, artigos curtos.
Translate: 'The library reopens on the 21st of September.'

activities.title: Estratégias de leitura para o exame
activities.intro: The DEPLE reading paper uses short authentic texts: classified ads, formal letters, notices and brief articles. Learn the strategy lines and the text-type formulas you will meet.

items.hr: Leia primeiro as perguntas: assim já sabe o que procurar no texto.
items.en: Read the questions first: that way you already know what to look for in the text.
items.note: core exam strategy, the questions tell you which details matter

items.hr: Vende-se T2 remodelado perto do metro. Contactar depois das 18h.
items.en: For sale: renovated two-bedroom flat near the metro. Contact after 6 pm.
items.note: classified ads are telegraphic, scan for numbers and times

items.hr: Exmo. Senhor Diretor, venho por este meio apresentar uma reclamação.
items.en: Dear Director, I hereby wish to make a complaint.
items.note: formal letter opening, it signals register and purpose in one line

items.hr: Procure nomes, datas, números e locais: as respostas escondem-se nos detalhes.
items.en: Look for names, dates, numbers and places: the answers hide in the details.
items.note: most reading questions are decided by a single small detail

items.hr: Cuidado com os distratores: usam palavras do texto, mas mudam um detalhe importante.
items.en: Beware of distractors: they use words from the text but change one important detail.
items.note: if an option repeats the text almost word for word, check it twice

items.hr: A partir de segunda-feira, o autocarro 23 passa a ter novo percurso.
items.en: From Monday, bus 23 will have a new route.
items.note: public notices often hinge on 'a partir de', the date is the answer

activities.title: Leitura tipo DEPLE
activities.intro: Exam-style reading. First read the text, then answer as in the exam, one detail decides each option. TEXT: 'A Biblioteca Municipal de Coimbra vai estar encerrada entre 2 e 20 de setembro para obras de modernização. Durante este período, os leitores podem devolver os livros na carrinha da biblioteca itinerante, estacionada ao lado do mercado municipal, de segunda a sexta, das 10h às 17h.
